title: Skid Row Beef Stroganoff
description: Quick and comforting, this Americanized beef stroganoff features ground beef, onions, and a creamy sauce. It's a satisfying dish that comes together in minutes, perfect for a weeknight meal served over macaroni or toast.
instructions:
1. Brown the onion, meat, salt and pepper.
2. Stir in flour, paprika and soup.
3. Simmer for 10 minutes.
4. Before serving, add sour cream.
5. Serve over macaroni or toast.

title: Dutch Kale And Potatoes
description: A hearty Dutch-inspired dish featuring mashed potatoes and kale, enriched with milk, butter, and seasonings. Topped with flavorful kielbasa, this makes for a simple yet satisfying meal.
instructions:
1. Peel and cook potatoes until soft.
2. Drain water.
3. Add kale with juice, milk, butter, salt and pepper.
4. Mash with masher or blender.
5. Leave all in pan and place kielbasa on top of mixture. Return to heat and steam until kielbasa is hot.
6. Watch that potatoes don't burn.
7. Serve with spicy mustard.

title: Matzo Meal Latkes(Pancakes)
description: Light and fluffy matzo meal pancakes, delicately sweetened with sugar and cinnamon. These golden-brown latkes are perfect for a festive breakfast or any time you crave a comforting treat.
instructions:
1. Separate eggs (yolks and whites).
2. Beat whites until stiff. Beat yolks and add other ingredients.
3. Add whites (fold in).
4. Drop by spoonful on greased frypan.
5. Brown both sides.
6. Serve with cinnamon and sugar or jelly.

title: Modena Mushroom Salad
description: A fresh Italian-inspired mushroom salad bursting with the flavors of garlic, balsamic vinegar, oregano, and basil. Crisp romaine lettuce and shredded carrot provide a refreshing crunch, all topped with crumbled Feta cheese.
ingredients: Filippo Berio extra virgin olive oil
instructions:
1. Wash mushrooms, lettuce and carrot; set on cotton dish towel to dry.
2. Break garlic and rub on inside of 1-quart resealable container (Mason jar or plastic).
3. Add olive oil, vinegar, oregano, basil, remainder of garlic and mushrooms.
4. Prepare in the morning and leave out during the day, upending periodically to mix.

title: Sauerkraut Casserole - Kalamazoo
description: An unexpectedly delicious sauerkraut casserole, sweetened with brown sugar and spiced with caraway seed. Slowly baked to perfection, this dish improves with reheating, offering deep, comforting flavors.
instructions:
1. Toss ingredients together and put into a casserole dish.
2. Dot butter on top.
3. Bake in a 350° oven for 2 hours.
4. This is much better than you would expect.
5. It improves when reheated.

title: Mihshe Malfuf(Stuffed Cabbage Leaves)
description: Traditional stuffed cabbage leaves, known as Malfuf, filled with a savory mixture of rice and lamb shoulder. Seasoned with cinnamon, allspice, garlic, and mint, then simmered in a tangy lemon sauce.
instructions:
1. Core and parboil cabbage until limp and easy to roll.
2. Place in colander and separate leaves.
3. Slice each leaf in half on rib. Leaves should be 4 inches long to 6 inches wide (keep uniform). Mix rice, meat and spices.
4. Place 1 tablespoon on each leaf. Spread lengthwise along rib and roll up.
5. Gently squeeze each roll when placing in pan.
6. Arrange in compact layers.
7. Dice 3 cloves of garlic and sprinkle between each layer.
8. Mash 1 clove garlic with mint and salt and add lemon juice.
9. Mix thoroughly and pour over cabbage rolls.
10. Cover cabbage with water, 1/2 inch above top layer.
11. Place a pottery plate over rolls to keep them firm and intact.
12. Cover pan and simmer 20 to 30 minutes until rice is done. Serves 6 to 8.

title: Jack-O-Lantern Cheese Ball(Childrens Treat)
description: A fun and festive Jack-O-Lantern shaped cheese ball, perfect for children and parties. Made with cheddar and cream cheese, pumpkin, and pineapple preserves, it's a sweet and savory treat served with crackers.
instructions:
1. Beat cheese, pumpkin, preserves and spices in medium bowl until smooth. Cover and refrigerate 2 or 3 hours, or until cheese is firm enough to shape. Shape into round pumpkin. Place on serving plate. Using knife, score vertical lines down pumpkin. Place pretzel
2. rod in top for stem. Cut bread into triangles for eyes and black olive slices curved upwards to form smile. Cut red pepper in one triangle for nose and two for ears.

title: Tommie'S Stuffed Cabbage Roll
description: Simple and comforting stuffed cabbage rolls, filled with a seasoned mixture of ground beef and rice. These delicious rolls are simmered in a tomato soup-based sauce, making for a hearty family meal.
instructions:
1. Use 1/2 can soup; mix together with ingredients.
2. Put mixture into pan boiled cabbage leaves.
3. Roll up and hold together with a toothpick.
4. (Will make 6 to 8 rolls.)

title: Apple-Raisin Bread
description: A warm and inviting Apple-Raisin Bread, spiced with cinnamon and nutmeg. Made with applesauce and oats, this moist loaf is perfect for breakfast, a snack, or any time you need a comforting treat.
instructions:
1. Sift flour, baking powder, soda, salt and spices.
2. Add sugar, eggs and applesauce.
3. Beat until well blended.
4. Stir in oats, raisins and oil.
5. Spoon into greased 9 x 5-inch loaf pan.
6. Bake 1 hour in 350° oven.
7. Remove from pan immediately and cool.
8. Slices better the second day.

title: Spinach-Mushroom Salad
description: A vibrant spinach and mushroom salad, tossed in a tangy red wine vinaigrette with a hint of mustard. This fresh and flavorful salad is a quick and healthy side dish or light meal.
instructions:
1. In medium salad bowl, combine spinach with mushrooms and red pepper strips.
2. In small bowl, combine vinegar with water, sugar and mustard.
3. Add oil, salt and pepper; mix well.
4. Pour dressing over salad and toss to coat.

title: Pasta Goulash
description: A comforting pasta goulash featuring ground hamburger and seashell pasta, simmered in a rich tomato sauce with bell pepper, onions, and garlic. This hearty dish is perfect for a satisfying family dinner.
instructions:
1. Boil pasta and drain.
2. Cook hamburger with chopped onions and bell pepper until hamburger is brown.
3. Add all other ingredients and cook 30 minutes longer.
4. Mix well.
5. Serves 6 people.

title: Meat Stuffed Cabbage Rolls
description: Flavorful meat-stuffed cabbage rolls, filled with lean ground beef, rice, and onion. These savory rolls are cooked in a simple tomato soup sauce, offering a satisfying and comforting meal.
instructions:
1. Cook the cabbage in boiling water to cover for a few minutes until wilted.
2. Drain.
3. Mix 2 tablespoons soup with remaining ingredients, mixing thoroughly.
4. Divide meat mixture evenly among cabbage leaves.
5. Starting at stem end, roll up and fold in edges. Pin leaves with wooden toothpicks.
6. Place rolls in skillet, seam side down, and pour remaining soup over them.
7. Cook, covered, over low heat for 40 minutes or until fork-tender. Stir now and then and spoon sauce over rolls.
8. Makes 8 servings.

title: German Meat Balls
description: Delicious German meatballs, seasoned and simmered in a unique sauce with sauerkraut, brown sugar, and raisins. This sweet and savory combination makes for a hearty and flavorful dish.
instructions:
1. Combine beef, bread, half onion, 1 1/2 teaspoons salt and pepper.
2. Mold into balls.
3. In Dutch oven, combine remaining onion, sugar, raisins, kraut, gingersnaps and tomato sauce.
4. Add meat balls; simmer for 1 1/2 hours.

title: Loose Cabbage Rolls
description: A deconstructed version of stuffed cabbage, featuring seasoned ground beef, rice, and vegetables layered with cabbage. Baked in a casserole dish with V-8 juice, it offers all the flavor with less effort.
instructions:
1. Saute first 4 ingredients until meat is brown.
2. Remove from heat.
3. Add rice.
4. Salt and pepper heavily.
5. Layer sliced cabbage and meat mixture in a 2-quart casserole.
6. Pour V-8 juice over top. Cover and cook in a 350° oven for 1 hour and 30 minutes.

title: Holupki - Stuffed Cabbage
description: Traditional Holupki, or stuffed cabbage, filled with a savory mixture of beef, pork, and rice. These comforting rolls are cooked with sauerkraut and tomato soup, creating a rich and flavorful meal.
instructions:
1. Brown onion in hot fat.
2. Add beef, pork, rice and seasonings. Mix well.
3. Cook separated cabbage leaves in boiling water for several minutes until soft enough to roll.
4. When cool, place 1 heaping tablespoon of meat mixture on each leaf and roll up and tuck in sides.
5. Take 1/2 of the sauerkraut and place in bottom of saucepan, then cabbage rolls in layers, then the other half of the sauerkraut.
6. Pour 1 can of tomato soup and enough water to cover. Cook slowly in covered saucepan about 1 hour.

title: Unstuffed Cabbage
description: A simplified, "unstuffed" version of cabbage rolls, layering ground beef, rice, onion, and cabbage in a baking dish. Covered with tomato soup and cheese, it's a comforting casserole without the rolling.
instructions: 1. Place the cabbage in a greased 2 1/2-quart baking dish. In a skillet, cook beef and onion until done. Stir in rice, salt and pepper, if desired. Spoon over cabbage. Combine soup and water; pour over beef mixture. Cover and bake at 350° for 40 to 50 minutes or until rice and cabbage are tender. Uncover. Sprinkle with cheese. Bake 5 to 10 minutes longer or until cheese is melted. Makes 4 servings.

title: Seafood Lasagne(Betty Crocker'S Cookbook)
description: A luscious seafood lasagne from Betty Crocker, featuring tender crabmeat and shrimp layered with creamy sauces and cheeses. This elegant dish is perfect for a special occasion or a seafood lover's delight.
instructions:
1. Heat margarine in 3-quart saucepan over low heat until melted. Add garlic.
2. Stir in flour and salt; cook, stirring constantly, until bubbly.
3. Remove from heat.
4. Stir in milk and broth.
5. Heat to boiling, stirring constantly.
6. Boil and stir 1 minute.
7. Stir in Mozzarella cheese, basil and pepper.
8. Cook over low heat, stirring constantly until cheese is melted.
9. Spread 1/4 of the cheese sauce (about 1 1/2 cups) in ungreased 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking dish.

title: Meatballs And Cabbage(The Quick Way For Stuffed Cabbage)
description: A quick and easy way to enjoy the flavors of stuffed cabbage, with seasoned meatballs and chopped cabbage cooked together. This dish offers a delicious and hearty meal with less active preparation time.
instructions:
1. Drop head of cabbage in boiling water until wilted.
2. Remove core and chop coarsely.
3. Mix meat, rice, spices, ketchup and liquid.
4. Roll into balls and brown in oil.
5. Remove and saute onions.
6. Layer cut up cabbage, meatballs and onions, ending with cabbage.
7. Mix soup, sugar, raisins, salt and pepper to taste. Pour over meat.
8. Cook approximately 2 hours.

title: Reuben Sandwich In A Dish
description: A deconstructed Reuben sandwich baked in a dish, featuring corned beef, caraway, and tomato layers. Topped with a Thousand Island and mayonnaise blend, mozzarella, and rye bread cubes, it's a comforting casserole.
instructions:
1. In an 8 x 8-inch glass dish, layer first 4 ingredients, then mix the Thousand Island and mayonnaise together and spread over top.
2. Sprinkle cheese over all.
3. Add bread cubes to top.
4. Bake in a 350° oven for 30 minutes.
5. Serve immediately.
6. This recipe can be doubled very easily.

title: Reubens
description: Classic Reuben sandwiches, prepared with corned beef, sauerkraut, and Swiss cheese on rye bread. Baked until warm and melty, these hearty sandwiches are a satisfying and flavorful meal.
instructions:
1. Put corned beef, sauerkraut and French dressing on rye bread. Top with Swiss cheese.
2. Bake at 400° for 8 to 10 minutes.

title: Chicken Kiev(Yields 4 Servings)
description: This Chicken Kiev recipe features chicken breasts stuffed with a flavorful yogurt-herb mixture, then baked until tender and juicy. It's a delightful and aromatic dish perfect for any meal.
instructions:
1. Mix together yogurt, chives, garlic, red pepper, black pepper and salt in bowl.
2. Cut a pocket under the breast alongside the bone and fill with mixture.
3. Fill all 4 breasts with mix and leftover put on top of chicken.
4. Put in baking dish and bake at 450° for 30 to 35 minutes.
5. Serve immediately.

title: Beef Soup(Borscht)
description: A comforting beef and vegetable soup, this Borscht features tender beef simmered with beets, carrots, cabbage, and other fresh vegetables. It's a hearty and nourishing meal, perfect for a chilly day.
instructions:
1. In a large kettle of water seasoned with salt and pepper, simmer the meat until tender.
2. Remove meat and cut up in bite sized pieces.
3. Return meat to kettle.
4. Add vegetables; cook until tender.
5. Add a tablespoon of sour cream just before serving. Serve in large bowls with buttered French rolls.

title: Old Fashion Bran Muffin
description: Start your day with these classic Old Fashion Bran Muffins, featuring a wholesome blend of bran, flour, eggs, and milk. They are easy to make and perfect for a quick breakfast or snack.
instructions:
1. Cream together shortening and sugar in a large bowl, with mixer.
2. Add eggs.
3. Blend in milk, Bran Buds mixture and remaining 2 cups Bran Buds.
4. Add all dry ingredients; stir until moist.
5. Bake at 400° for 18 minutes.

title: German Scalloped Sauerkraut
description: German Scalloped Sauerkraut offers a delightful combination of tangy sauerkraut, sweet red apples, and brown sugar, baked until tender. This versatile side dish pairs wonderfully with pork or other savory meats.
instructions: 1. Layer in casserole the sauerkraut, apples and brown sugar. Cover and bake at 350° until apples are tender. Delicious served with pork.

title: Shake-It-Up Chicken Nuggets
description: These Shake-It-Up Chicken Nuggets are a quick and easy way to prepare crispy chicken using a simple breading mixture. Perfect for a family-friendly meal or a tasty snack.
instructions:
1. Crush crumbs by placing in Ziploc bag and rolling a rolling pin over bag.
2. Open bag and add Parmesan cheese.
3. Re-seal bag and shake to mix.

title: Turkey-Taco Salad
description: Enjoy a flavorful and vibrant Turkey-Taco Salad, combining seasoned ground turkey with fresh lettuce, carrots, cheese, tomatoes, and crispy corn tortillas. It's a light yet satisfying meal.
instructions:
1. Spray 10-inch nonstick skillet with nonstick cooking spray and heat over medium heat.
2. Add turkey and, using back of a wooden spoon to crumble meat, cook, stirring occasionally, until turkey is browned and cooked through, 5 to 8 minutes.
3. Add water and taco seasonings mix and stir to combine thoroughly.
4. Bring liquid to boil.
5. Reduce heat to low and let simmer, stirring occasionally, for 15 to 20 minutes.

title: Patty'S Panned Pierogies
description: These Panned Pierogies offer a comforting and rich filling of potatoes and Velveeta cheese, enveloped in tender lasagna noodles. Perfect for a satisfying homestyle meal.
instructions: 1. Yield: 2 (9 x 13-inch) pans.

title: Mushroom Salad
description: A refreshing Mushroom Salad featuring a mix of fresh mushrooms, sharp Cheddar cheese, and green onions, dressed with a zesty oil and wine vinegar dressing. This salad is simple to prepare and full of flavor.
instructions:
1. Mix mushrooms, cheese and green onion together.
2. Then mix the oil, vinegar and Cavender's Greek seasoning for dressing.
3. After mixing well, pour over mushroom mixture.
4. Serves 10.

title: Roasted Carrot And Beet Soup
description: This Roasted Carrot and Beet Soup offers a delightful blend of roasted carrots, celery, onion, and beets, spiced with ginger and mace, then finished with a dollop of sour cream. It's a comforting and earthy soup.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Place carrots, celery and onion in a shallow oven proof dish.
3. Dot with butter and sprinkle with the sugar, ginger and mace.
4. Pour 2 c. of broth into the bottom of the dish.
5. Cover tightly with aluminum foil.
6. Bake for 2 hours. Remove vegetables from the oven and transfer them along with any liquid, to a large pot.
7. Add 4 c. of broth.
8. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to medium and simmer for 10 minutes partially covered. Cool slightly.
9. Puree the soup along with the cooked beets in a blender or a food processor until smooth.
10. Return to the pot. Season with salt and pepper.
11. Add some of remaining broth for thinner soup, if desired.
12. Garnish with sour cream and chives.

title: Russian Tuna Salad
description: This Russian Tuna Salad combines herb rice with flaky chunk white tuna, crisp celery, onion, and a creamy mayonnaise dressing infused with dill pickle and garlic. It's a hearty and flavorful salad perfect for a light meal.
instructions:
1. Prepare rice according to package directions.
2. Put into sealable plastic bowl.
3. Add chopped celery, onion and pickle; mix well.
4. Mix mayonnaise, parsley, salt, pepper and garlic powder in a separate bowl; blend into the rice mixture.
5. Open and drain tuna.
6. Flake into rice mixture; mix thoroughly.
7. Open and drain peas.
8. Add to rice mixture; mix thoroughly.
9. Seal bowl; place in fridge.
10. Chill 4 to 6 hours.
11. Serve with garnish of lettuce or endive, egg and tomato slices.
12. Makes 6 to 8 generous servings.

title: Stroganoff For A Crowd
description: This hearty Stroganoff for a Crowd features tender sirloin strips and mushrooms in a rich, creamy sauce, served over rice. It's a classic and comforting dish, perfect for feeding a large gathering.
instructions:
1. Mix flour and salt. Sprinkle meat that has been cut into bite size strips with flour mixture.
2. Melt butter.
3. Add onions and garlic. Cook until tender, but not brown. Remove from skillet and brown meat well.

title: Dukhobor(Borscht)
description: This Dukhobor Borscht is a rich and vibrant soup, combining tender vegetables like beets, carrots, and cabbage with a creamy texture from whipping cream. It's a comforting and flavorful dish.
instructions:
1. Place 1/4 pound of butter and onion in a large saucepan and simmer until onion is shiny.
2. Add tomatoes, finely grated carrot, 1/2 grated beet and 1/2 chopped green pepper and boil 20 minutes, stirring constantly.
3. Place 3 quarts boiling water in a large kettle. Add 3 potatoes, cut in half, remaining beet, diced carrot, diced celery and salt.
4. Boil until potatoes are done. Remove potatoes and mash.
5. Add the rest of the butter and the cream. Add 3 diced potatoes and let boil for 1 minute, then add boiled tomato mixture, shredded cabbage and mashed potatoes.
6. Bring to a boil. Add chopped green pepper, dill and black pepper.
7. Remove from heat. Cover with lid. Serve hot.

title: Russian Fluff
description: Russian Fluff is a simple and comforting casserole dish featuring ground hamburg and onion, mixed with rice, peas, and tomato soup, then baked until bubbly. It's a quick and easy meal for any day of the week.
instructions:
1. Fry hamburg and onion.
2. Add other ingredients and mix.
3. Bake 30 minutes at 350°.

title: Danish Style Stuffed Cabbage
description: Danish Style Stuffed Cabbage features tender cabbage leaves filled with a savory mixture of ground pork and rice, simmered in a rich broth with molasses. It's a comforting and flavorful dish perfect for a family dinner.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°.
2. Grease a 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan. Cut out core and cook cabbage in water to cover for 15 minutes to loosen outer leaves.
3. Cool in cold water.
4. Simmer rice in milk 30 minutes to form a creamy mixture.
5. Cool.
6. Mix meats, salt and rice mixture.
7. Add egg whites, one at a time, then pepper and onion. Filling is soft.
8. Carefully peel off 12 cabbage leaves; place on board.
9. Cut ribs to make leaves lie flat.
10. Place a heaping teaspoon of filling in center of each.
11. Roll up, folding in ends. Place seam side down in pan.
12. Pour broth around rolls.
13. Drop 1 teaspoon molasses on each.
14. Bake, uncovered, for 30 minutes; turn rolls and baste.
15. Bake 30 minutes longer.
16. Transfer rolls to a platter.
17. Ladle pan juices into skillet.
18. Bring to a boil. Combine cornstarch and cold water to make a paste.
19. Stir in cornstarch paste.
20. Cook and stir until thickened.
21. Serve sauce over rolls.

title: Meat Burritos
description: These Meat Burritos are packed with seasoned meat strips, fresh vegetables, and a flavorful blend of spices, all wrapped in soft flour tortillas. Quick to prepare, they make for a satisfying and versatile meal.
instructions:
1. Fry meat strips in oil in a skillet over medium heat until browned.
2. Add carrots, bell pepper, onion and garlic.
3. Saute, then add tomatoes, green chilies, water and seasonings. Bring to a boil.
4. Lower heat and cook until done. Warm tortillas.

title: Sweet & Sour Cabbage Borscht
description: This Sweet & Sour Cabbage Borscht features tender brisket simmered with cabbage, tomatoes, beets, and sauerkraut, balanced with a hint of lemon for a vibrant sweet and sour flavor. It's a hearty and comforting soup.
instructions: 1. Boil meat in water, add cabbage. When cabbage is soft, add other ingredients.

title: Polish Sauerkraut And Sausage
description: This Polish Sauerkraut and Sausage dish combines tangy sauerkraut with savory sausage, onion, and caraway seeds, baked until tender. It's a hearty and flavorful one-pot meal.
instructions:
1. Drain and rinse sauerkraut.
2. Combine with remaining ingredients and bake in covered roaster for 1 hour at 350°.
3. Toss lightly with a fork a couple of times while baking.

title: German Style Pork Steaks
description: German Style Pork Steaks are seasoned with salt and pepper, then cooked to perfection alongside sauerkraut, tomatoes, and onion, resulting in a savory and tender meal. Perfect for a hearty dinner.
instructions:
1. Salt and pepper black ribs to taste.
2. Place in "roast-in-bag" (hole in top), wrapped in heavy foil.
3. Put over coals and cover with big pot.
4. Heat for 45 minutes.
5. Take out of bag, then grill with basting of mustard sauce on both sides.
6. Place back in foil and heat on back of grill until ready to serve.

title: Potluck Kraut Dish
description: This Potluck Kraut Dish is a comforting casserole featuring ground chuck, rice, and sauerkraut, all baked together with tomato soup until perfectly done. It's an easy and satisfying meal, perfect for sharing.
instructions:
1. Mix all ingredients and place in 2 1/2-quart buttered casserole.
2. Cover and bake 2 hours or until rice is done.
3. Bake at 350°.

title: Pronto Enchiladas
description: These Pronto Enchiladas offer a quick and satisfying meal with seasoned ground beef, a creamy chicken soup and Ro-Tel mixture, all rolled in tortillas and topped with cheese. They are easy to prepare and rich in flavor.
instructions: 1. Cook beef in large skillet; drain. Stir in cream of chicken soup and half of Ro-Tel. Spoon 1/2 cup beef mixture down center of each tortilla. Sprinkle each with 2 1/2 tablespoons cheese. Roll up tortillas and place each, seam down, in a lightly greased 9 x 13 baking dish. Pour cheese soup over tortillas. Sprinkle remaining Ro-Tel and cheese over the soup. Bake, covered, at 350° for 20 minutes. Uncover and bake 5 more minutes. Serve with salsa and sour cream.

title: Real Swedish Rye Bread
description: Indulge in the hearty goodness of homemade Swedish Rye Bread, a rustic loaf with a rich flavor profile. This recipe combines oats, molasses, and rye flour for a dense, satisfying texture.
instructions:
1. Pour boiling water over oatmeal, brown sugar, salt, butter and molasses.
2. Cool, add yeast. stir.
3. Add 2 cups flour.
4. Beat hard 2 minutes.
5. Add rest of flour, just enough to make soft dough.
6. Rest 10 minutes.
7. Knead until smooth and elastic.
8. Place in greased bowl.
9. Cover and let rise until double.
10. Punch down and let rise again.
11. Divide dough in half, shape into two loaves.
12. Let rise about 45 minutes.
13. Bake in oven (375°) for 35 to 40 minutes.
14. When baked, brush tops with butter.

title: Easy Sauerkraut Salad
description: Enjoy a refreshingly simple sauerkraut salad, perfect as a side dish or light meal. This quick recipe combines crisp sauerkraut with fresh vegetables and a touch of sweetness.
instructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the first four ingredients.
2. Pour sugar over and mix well.
3. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
4. Serve chilled.
5. Yield:
6. 8 to 10 servings.

title: Sweet And Sour Polish Sausage
description: Savor the delightful flavors of Sweet and Sour Polish Sausage, a quick and easy dish that promises a perfect balance of tang and sweetness. Ideal for a weeknight dinner, this recipe combines savory sausage with a zesty sauce.
instructions:
1. In large saucepan, cut Polish sausage into 1/2-inch slices. Then add remaining ingredients.
2. Cook on medium for about 30 minutes, then simmer for 15 minutes.

title: Stuffed Cabbage(Low In Cholesterol And Makes About 12.)
description: Create classic Stuffed Cabbage rolls, a comforting and flavorful dish perfect for any occasion. Tender cabbage leaves are filled with a savory mixture of meat and rice, then simmered in a rich tomato sauce.
instructions:
1. Cut out cabbage core and steam in hot water, core side down. Peel leaves to dry.
2. Mix hamburg, rice, 1/2 can tomato soup, garlic, onion, salt and pepper.
3. Roll mixture in cabbage leaf, stem side outside.
4. Put 1/2 can tomato soup in crock-pot; layer with cabbage leaves, then cabbage rolls.
5. Add 2 cans tomato soup.
6. Cook 6 hours.

title: Polish Wedding Pound Cake
description: Bake a luscious Polish Wedding Pound Cake, a rich and moist dessert perfect for celebrations. This cake boasts a tender crumb, studded with walnuts and maraschino cherries for a festive touch.
instructions:
1. Cream shortening and sugar, 1 cup at a time and cream after each.
2. Add flour mixed with salt, alternating with milk and flavoring, ending with flour.
3. Add cherries and nuts.
4. Pour into greased angel food pan.
5. Place in cold oven on lower shelf.
6. Bake at 300° for 1 hour and 45 minutes to 2 hours.
7. Let stand in pan 1/2 hour before moving from pan.
8. Cool on rack.

title: Raw Mushroom Salad
description: Delight in a fresh and vibrant Raw Mushroom Salad, a quick and healthy appetizer or side dish. Earthy mushrooms are tossed with zesty lemon juice, olive oil, and green onions for a light and flavorful experience.
instructions:
1. Toss mushrooms with lemon juice until slightly moistened.
2. Add onions, oil, salt and toss again.
3. Chill.

title: Potato Pancakes(Potato Latkes Are Eaten During Hanukkah.)
description: Whip up a batch of classic Potato Pancakes, also known as Latkes, a beloved dish often enjoyed during Hanukkah. These crispy, golden-brown pancakes are made from grated potatoes and onions, perfect with sour cream or applesauce.
instructions:
1. Put all ingredients in blender container in order listed. Cover and run on high speed just until all potatoes go through blades, about 10 seconds.
2. Do not overblend or potatoes will be liquified.
3. Pour small amount onto a hot greased griddle or frypan. Fry until brown on both sides, turning once. Serve with applesauce and sour cream.

title: Mini Blinis
description: Indulge in delightful Mini Blinis, small, thin pancakes that are perfect for a sweet treat. These delicate rolls are filled with a creamy, sweet cheese mixture and coated in cinnamon sugar.
instructions:
1. Blend the egg yolk, 1/4 cup sugar and cream cheese.
2. Roll bread slices flat.
3. Spread mixture and roll up.
4. Cut in half.
5. Dip in the melted butter.
6. Roll in mixture of 1 1/2 cups sugar and cinnamon. Freeze.
7. Bake seam side down at 350° for 15 minutes.

title: Avocado Mousse With Raw Mushroom Salad
description: Savor a luxurious Avocado Mousse with Raw Mushroom Salad, a sophisticated and refreshing appetizer. Creamy avocado mousse is paired with a vibrant, zesty mushroom salad for a delightful textural and flavor contrast.
instructions:
1. Pare and pit avocados; sprinkle each with lemon juice.
2. Puree in blender or food processor; transfer to large bowl.
3. Dissolve bouillon cube in 1/2 cup boiling water.
4. Add salt, pepper and the pepper sauce.
5. Combine gelatin and 1/4 cup cold water in small bowl.
6. Place bowl over hot water, stir until gelatin is dissolved. Mix bouillon mixture and gelatin, stir into pureed avocado.
7. Beat cream until it forms stiff peaks; fold into avocado mixture; stir in mayonnaise.
8. Pour into 7-cup ring mold.
9. Refrigerate, covered, until firm, 6 hours or overnight.

title: Enchilada Casserole(Makes 8 Servings)
description: Prepare a hearty Enchilada Casserole, a comforting and flavorful dish perfect for family dinners. Layers of seasoned hamburger, rich gravy, and tortillas are baked with plenty of cheese.
instructions:
1. Fry hamburger and onion until done.
2. Make gravy of the margarine, flour, water, tomato juice and chili powder.
3. Heat tortillas in gravy.
4. Place a layer in baking dish.
5. Sprinkle with meat and grated cheese.
6. Alternate layers of tortillas, meat and cheese until all is used.
7. Bake 1 1/2 hours at 250°.
8. Can be made in the crock-pot or microwave.

title: Sauerkraut Pie
description: Discover a unique and comforting Sauerkraut Pie, a savory dish combining classic flavors in an innovative way. Layers of seasoned sausage, tangy sauerkraut, and creamy mashed potatoes are baked to perfection.
instructions:
1. Make up 3 cups mashed potatoes and set aside.
2. Fry the sausage; drain well.
3. Wash the sauerkraut and let it drain.
4. In a large deep dish pie pan, pat 1/2 the mashed potatoes about 1/4-inch thick on the bottom and sides.
5. Layer 1/2 of the sauerkraut, sausage, cheese and make a second layer using the remaining sauerkraut, sausage and cheese.
6. Using the rest of the mashed potatoes, pat a crust on top and pinch the ends together.
7. Rub the dash of butter on the top and sprinkle pepper lightly on the top. Bake at 350° for 8 to 10 minutes or until the top turns a golden brown.

title: Mushroom Salad(Preparation Time: 20 Minutes Plus Chilling Time)
description: Enjoy a simple and elegant Mushroom Salad, a quick and refreshing appetizer or side dish. Fresh mushrooms, baby Swiss cheese, and green onions are combined for a light and flavorful experience.
instructions: 1. Clean and slice mushrooms into a large salad bowl. Add cheese and onions. Chill.

title: Lazy Stuffed Cabbage
description: Whip up an easy and comforting Lazy Stuffed Cabbage, a simplified version of the classic without all the rolling. This hearty dish combines seasoned ground beef and rice with tender cabbage, baked in a flavorful tomato sauce.
instructions:
1. Saute meat until browned.
2. Drain.
3. Add onion soup mix.
4. Add rice to meat mixture.
5. Put cabbage in a large baking pan.
6. Spread meat mixture over cabbage.
7. Mix soup and water; pour over meat mixture.
8. Cover and bake for 1 1/2 hours at 325°.

title: Gladys' Polish Mistakes
description: Indulge in Gladys' Polish Mistakes, a quick and savory appetizer perfect for parties. These delightful open-faced sandwiches feature a cheesy, seasoned meat topping on crispy party rye bread.
instructions:
1. Brown hamburger and sausage; add salt to taste.
2. Drain.
3. Add oregano.
4. Chunk the Velveeta cheese.
5. Add cheese to meat mixture; mix well while meat is hot so cheese will melt.
6. Spread on rye bread.
7. Can freeze on cookie sheet, then bake at 350° for 20 minutes.

title: Polish Beet Soup
description: Savor the rich and tangy flavors of Polish Beet Soup, a traditional and comforting dish. This vibrant soup features tender beets, spareribs, and a touch of vinegar, creating a deeply satisfying meal.
instructions:
1. Drain beet juice into a glass and add 2 tablespoons vinegar and 1 teaspoon salt.
2. Cut beets in thin strips and place in a bowl with 3 tablespoons vinegar and 1 teaspoon salt.
3. Set aside.

title: Polish Mistakes
description: Prepare delicious Polish Mistakes, a quick and savory snack perfect for any gathering. These party bites feature a cheesy, meaty topping spread over crispy rye bread and broiled until golden.
instructions:
1. Fry sausage and hamburger together; drain grease off.
2. Put in a bowl while warm; mix Cheez Whiz.
3. Put on party rye bread (rye bread) and broil until bread is lightly brown and crisp.
4. Watch close.

title: Cheese Blintzes(Cinnamon Roll-Ups)
description: Delight in delightful Cheese Blintzes, also known as Cinnamon Roll-Ups, a sweet and comforting treat. These easy blintzes are made with flattened white bread, filled with a creamy cheese mixture, and flavored with vanilla and cinnamon.
instructions:
1. Remove crust from bread and flatten each slice with a rolling pin. Mix together cream cheese, flavorings and egg yolk. Spread approximately 1 heaping teaspoon of the mixture per slice of bread.
2. Roll the bread up as a jelly roll and cut into three pieces.

title: Chinese Stroganoff
description: Explore a unique twist with Chinese Stroganoff, a fusion dish combining rich beef with Asian-inspired flavors. Tender beef and vegetables are simmered in a savory soy sauce-based broth, offering a delightful culinary crossover.
instructions:
1. Brown meat in oil.
2. Add onions (both kinds), bamboo shoots, water chestnuts, mushrooms, broth and sugar.
3. Cover and simmer 5 minutes.
4. Blend cornstarch with 1 tablespoon water and the soy sauce.
5. Add to mixture and simmer, stirring occasionally so it doesn't stick, until juice thickens.

title: Cheese Blintz
description: Indulge in sweet and creamy Cheese Blintz, a comforting treat perfect for breakfast or dessert. These delightful rolls feature a rich cheese filling encased in thin bread, pan-fried to golden perfection.
instructions:
1. Mix cream cheese, sugar, vanilla, and egg yolk until creamy. Trim crusts from bread and roll with rolling pin until thin. Spread with filling and roll in crescent roll shape.
2. Melt butter. Dip blintz in butter and roll or sprinkle in mixture of 1 cup sugar and 2 teaspoons cinnamon.
3. Add more cinnamon and sugar as needed.
4. Bake 350° for 15 minutes.

title: Polish Poczki
description: Bake traditional Polish Poczki, rich and fluffy doughnuts perfect for a celebratory treat. These delectable pastries are made with a yeast-based dough, enriched with egg yolks, butter, and vanilla for an irresistibly tender texture.
instructions:
1. Dissolve yeast in luke warm milk.
2. Add 2 cups flour; let stand in a warm place about 1/2 hour.
3. Then beat eggs, sugar, butter, vanilla and salt till light.
4. Add mixture to yeast mixture; add 5 cups flour cover and allow to rise for 1 hour or till double.
5. Turn on floured dough and pat to 1/2-inch thickness.
6. Cut with round doughnut cutter.
7. Cover and let rise again till double and light. Fry in deep fat at 375° till done on both sides.
8. Drain on paper towels.
9. When cool cut 1/2 way through and fill with jelly and sprinkle with powder sugar.

title: Stuffed Cabbage (Helupkies)
description: Classic golabki, tender cabbage leaves stuffed with a savory mixture of ground beef and rice, slow-cooked in a rich tomato sauce, create a hearty and comforting meal perfect for family dinners.
instructions:
1. Wash head of cabbage.
2. Cut the stem from the head of cabbage deep enough to start a separation of the very outer leaves from the core.
3. Carefully pull away the outer leaves.
4. Continue to remove the leaves until you have enough for the recipe.
5. If the leaves are not coming off, you may dip the head of cabbage in boiling water to loosen 3 or 4 leaves at a time.
6. Once you have the leaves you are going to use, steam them until tender.
7. (In order to steam, fill a Dutch oven 1 to 2-inches with water.
8. Place a colander inside and then the leaves in the colander.
9. Put the lid on and boil the water for 5 to 10 minutes.)

title: Polish Noodles And  Cabbage
description: This simple and satisfying dish combines tender egg noodles with buttery sautéed cabbage and onions, seasoned with caraway seeds and finished with a dollop of sour cream for a creamy texture.
instructions:
1. Melt butter in large skillet.
2. Add onion and saute until transparent.
3. Add cabbage and saute 5 minutes, or until tender, but still crisp.
4. Stir in caraway seed, salt and pepper.

title: Mom'S Special Quick Quiche
description: A quick and easy quiche featuring crispy bacon, a blend of Cheddar and Swiss cheeses, and sautéed mushrooms and onions, all baked into a flaky crust for a delightful breakfast or light meal.
instructions:
1. Heat oven to 400°.
2. Lightly spray a 9 or 10-inch pie plate or quiche pan with Pam.
3. Sprinkle cheese, bacon, mushrooms and onion evenly over bottom of pan.
4. Mix Bisquick, milk, eggs, salt and pepper in blender or food processor.
5. Pour mixture over ingredients already in pan.
6. Bake about 30 to 35 minutes, until golden brown.
7. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese while hot.
8. Let stand 5 minutes before cutting.
9. Great for breakfast or brunch! Refrigerate leftovers.
10. Makes 6 servings.

title: Irene'S Hot Borscht(Servings: 8)
description: A vibrant and tangy Ukrainian borscht, featuring cooked beets, potatoes, and cabbage, simmered in a savory broth with tomato paste and vinegar, offering a deeply flavorful and comforting soup.
instructions: 1. Peel beets; add to water in a large pot and bring to a boil. Cook until done, about 45 minutes. Add salt. Remove beets from water and set aside to cool. Peel potatoes and cut into 1/8's. Add to beet water. Cook until almost done.

title: Borscht(Russian Soup)
description: A classic Russian borscht made with tender spareribs, cabbage, and beets, simmered in a rich broth, offering a hearty and flavorful soup with a touch of lemon for brightness.
instructions:
1. Cover spareribs with water, salt, pepper and 1 whole onion. Boil and simmer 1 hour and a half.
2. Remove onion.
3. Chop one small head of cabbage.
4. Add remaining ingredients and simmer 20 minutes more.
5. Add additional vinegar, salt and pepper if needed.
6. Finally add as much sour cream as you wish.

title: Peanut Butter-Honey Balls
description: No-bake peanut butter-honey balls, a simple and sweet treat made by combining creamy peanut butter, golden honey, milk powder, and cereal, perfect for a quick snack.
instructions:
1. Mix together peanut butter and honey in bowl.
2. Stir well; slowly add milk powder and mix well.
3. Add cereal.
4. Shape into balls.
5. Ready to eat.
6. No cooking.
7. Store in refrigerator.

title: Stuffed Cabbage(Golabki)
description: Traditional Polish golabki, featuring tender cabbage leaves generously stuffed with a savory blend of ground pork, beef, veal, rice, and onions, simmered until tender in a flavorful broth.
instructions:
1. Mix together pork, beef, veal, eggs, onions, rice and salt and pepper.
2. Place cabbage (from which the core has been removed) into hot water to soften leaves.
3. Take approximately 3 tablespoons of meat mixture and place it in a cabbage leaf and wrap it.
4. Take can of tomato soup, some water and crushed tomatoes and salt and pepper and pour over cabbage rolls (after the cabbage rolls have been in the oven for 25 minutes).
5. Put in 350° oven for 1 1/2 hours.
6. Makes about 120.

title: Ukranian Winter Borscht
description: A hearty Ukrainian winter borscht, rich with butter-sautéed onions, garlic, carrots, and an array of root vegetables and beets, simmered in stock and finished with sour cream for a comforting meal.
instructions:
1. Melt butter in pot over moderate heat.
2. When foam subsides, add onion and garlic and saute about 5 minutes, then add carrots and saute 2 minutes.
3. Add turnip, parsnip and celeriac and saute 5 minutes more.
4. Add tomatoes, salt, pepper, vinegar and sugar. Saute 2 more minutes.
5. Stir to combine and bring to a boil, then simmer at lower heat 15 minutes.
6. In saucepan, bring 2 quarts beef stock to boil.
7. Add cabbage and potatoes.
8. Return to boil.
9. Simmer and cook 5 minutes.
10. Transfer cabbage, potatoes and beet stock to mixture.
11. Stir in parsley, 1/3 cup dill and shredded beet.
12. Simmer 15 minutes.
13. Season to taste.

title: Russian Dressing(Salad Dressing)
description: A classic Russian dressing, a creamy and tangy condiment made from a simple blend of mayonnaise, ketchup, chopped parsley, and dill, perfect for salads or sandwiches.
instructions:
1. Chop parsley and pickle very fine.
2. Add ketchup; stir and add mayonnaise.
3. Blend thoroughly.
4. Keep covered in refrigerator. Keeps indefinitely.

title: Beetroot Pickles
description: Sweet and tangy beetroot pickles, prepared by cooking and slicing beets, then marinating them in a flavorful brine with mustard, salt, sugar, and sliced onions for a vibrant condiment.
instructions:
1. Cook beets until tender.
2. Drain, reserving half pint of the liquid.
3. Slip skins and slice when cool.
4. Add the next 4 ingredients to the reserved beet juice and boil up again.
5. Slice onions thinly and layer them in jars with the beets.
6. Pour hot vinegar juice over the onions and beets and seal jars tightly. Let set for a week in a cool place.

title: Juan'S Pan Burrito
description: A quick and easy pan burrito featuring seasoned ground beef simmered with tomato paste and enchilada sauce, then wrapped in flour tortillas with cheese, a flavorful and satisfying meal.
instructions:
1. Brown and drain ground beef.
2. Mix tomato paste and sauce mixes with 2 cups of hot water.
3. Add half of this mixture to the ground beef.
4. Simmer for 10 minutes and add spices to taste.

title: Borsch(With Beef)
description: A robust Russian borscht made with tender beef and bone, slow-simmered in a rich broth with beets, cabbage, and onions, creating a deeply flavorful and comforting soup, perfect with sour cream.
instructions:
1. Trim all fat from beef; place beef and bone in deep pot.
2. Add water, bouillon cubes, salt and bay leaf.
3. Bring to a boil; skim. Cover and simmer 1 hour until beef is almost tender.
4. Remove beef, bone and bay leaf.
5. Cut beef into bite pieces, return to simmering stock.
6. Stir in tomato paste.
7. Add vegetables.
8. Bring to a full boil, cover, then simmer 1 hour longer.
9. Serve in large soup bowls topped with sour cream.

title: Pizza Sausage
description: Quick and cheesy pizza sausages, made by browning sausage with Worcestershire, oregano, and garlic salt, then melting Velveeta cheese into the mixture for a savory and satisfying topping.
instructions:
1. Brown, crumble and drain sausage.
2. Put sausage back in skillet and sprinkle with Worcestershire, oregano and garlic salt.
3. Add 1 pound Velveeta cheese, cut in cubes.
4. Stir until cheese melts. Put a "dab" (about 1 teaspoon) in the middle of a slice of party rye bread.

title: Potato And Mushroom Salad(Makes About 5 Cups)
description: A refreshing potato and mushroom salad, combining boiled potatoes, fresh mushrooms, celery, and green onions with a tangy dressing made from vegetable oil, lemon juice, mustard, and a hint of sugar.
instructions:
1. In shallow dish, combine potatoes, mushrooms, celery and onions.
2. In small bowl or jar, mix remaining ingredients; pour over potato mixture.
3. Cover; refrigerate 3 hours, stirring occasionally.
4. Refrigerate leftovers.

title: Pierogi(Basic Dough; Polish)
description: Basic Polish pierogi dough, a simple mixture of flour, salt, egg, sour cream, butter, and warm water, kneaded until smooth and ready to be filled with your favorite savory or sweet fillings.
instructions:
1. Sift flour and salt in bowl.
2. Add egg, sour cream and warm water.
3. Mix together.
4. Knead dough until smooth.
5. Divide dough and roll about 1/4-inch thick.
6. Cut in circles.
7. Fill with one of the following fillings.

title: Grass Soup(Polish Kvas)
description: A traditional Polish Kvas-inspired 'Grass Soup,' a refreshing cold soup made with crisp leaf lettuce, green onions, cucumbers, sour cream, and a touch of vinegar, perfect for a light, cool meal.
instructions:
1. Wash and chop lettuce.
2. Put into container.
3. Put 4 tablespoons (to be deducted from amount in ingredients) of vinegar, 12 ice cubes and 1/2 cup water over lettuce.
4. Chill (if time permits, overnight).
5. Combine lettuce, cucumbers and onions.
6. Add water until it starts showing through the vegetables as you press on vegetables with a spoon.
7. Add sour cream.
8. Add salt and vinegar. Amounts listed are what I used.
9. You may want to start with less and add to taste.

title: Crab-Cheese Mix
description: A quick and savory crab-cheese mix spread, combining creamy Cheez Whiz with crabmeat, butter, and Worcestershire sauce, perfect for broiling on English muffin halves for an easy appetizer.
instructions: 1. Mix Cheez Whiz, crabmeat, butter and Worcestershire sauce. Split English muffins. Spread with mixture. Bake at 350° for 10 minutes. Serves 6.

title: Russian Drop Cookies
description: Classic Russian drop cookies, a delightful blend of butter, sugars, eggs, flour, and aromatic spices, loaded with raisins, coconut, and nuts, offering a chewy and flavorful treat.
instructions:
1. Cream Crisco, butter and sugars.
2. Add eggs, vanilla, raisins, coconut, nuts, flour, soda and cinnamon.
3. Mix all real well.
4. Drop by tsp. on greased cookie sheet.
5. Bake at 350° to 375° oven until light brown.
6. These get better with age.
7. Keep in airtight tins.

title: Cocktail Ryes
description: Easy cocktail ryes, prepared by spreading a creamy mixture of mayonnaise, onion, and Parmesan cheese onto rye bread slices, then baking until bubbly and golden for a quick appetizer.
instructions:
1. Mix all ingredients.
2. Spread on bread liberally.
3. Bake 10 to 15 minutes at 350 degrees or until slightly bubbly and brown.
4. If baked too long bread will become dry and hard.

title: Polish Pickle Soup
description: A unique Polish pickle soup, featuring tender pork hocks slow-cooked with onions, potatoes, cabbage, and carrots, infused with the tangy flavor of pickle juice and fresh dill for a comforting and distinct dish.
instructions:
1. Put water in kettle and then add pork hocks and onion.
2. Cook 1 hour.
3. Remove pork hocks and cool.
4. When cooled, dice meat and set aside.
5. Add potato, cabbage and carrots to kettle.
6. Cook until tender.
7. Then add pickle juice, pickles, dill weed, pepper and diced meat.
8. Cook until heated through.
9. Serves 6 to 8.

title: Red Beet Soup
description: A comforting Red Beet Soup, reminiscent of traditional borscht, with tender neck bones, aromatic spices, and a hint of lemon. This hearty soup promises warmth and a delightful blend of earthy and tangy flavors.
instructions:
1. Brown shank or neck pieces in a large pot with the oil.
2. Turn over and add onions.
3. Lower heat and cook until onions soften (10 minutes).
4. Add salt and spices.
5. Cover with water.
6. Bring to a boil.
7. Lower heat and simmer uncovered until meat is tender (about 1 1/2 hours).
8. Wash beets.
9. Peel and cut roots and add to soup. Clean leaves and stems discarding tough or damaged leaves.
10. Cut stems in 1-inch pieces.
11. When beets are almost tender, add stems. Continue cooking until beets and stems are tender.
12. The meat should almost fall off bones.

title: Bratwurst-Apple Skillet
description: A quick and flavorful Bratwurst-Apple Skillet, combining savoury sausages with the tartness of cooking apples and sauerkraut. Perfect for a weeknight meal, delivering a delightful balance of sweet and sour notes.
instructions:
1. Brown bratwurst in medium skillet over medium-high heat, turning occasionally.
2. In medium bowl, combine all remaining ingredients.
3. Add to skillet.
4. Reduce heat; cover and simmer 8 to 12 minutes or until apples are tender, stirring occasionally. Makes 4 servings.

title: Raw Artichoke And Mushroom Salad
description: A fresh and elegant Raw Artichoke and Mushroom Salad, brightened with lemon and complemented by rich Parmesan and extra virgin olive oil. This simple salad is sophisticated yet easy to prepare.
instructions:
1. Squeeze lemons into a small bowl.
2. Set aside.
3. Pull off tough outer leaves from artichokes and discard.
4. Trim bottoms and tops. Slice lengthwise and pull out the fuzzy choke, if any.
5. Slice artichokes crosswise as thinly as possible and immediately place in lemon juice to prevent browning.
6. Slice mushrooms.
7. Remove artichokes from lemon juice with a slotted spoon.
8. Combine with mushrooms and dress with olive oil, salt and pepper.
9. Toss with half of the Parmesan and arrange on 4 salad plates.
10. Garnish with remaining cheese and serve.

title: Oven Baked Shrimp Stroganoff
description: A convenient Oven Baked Shrimp Stroganoff marrying tender shrimp with creamy mushroom soup, dill, and cheese, served over egg noodles. This comforting dish brings the beloved stroganoff flavours to a new, easy-to-bake format.
instructions:
1. Cook noodles according to package directions; drain and set aside. Combine sour cream, soup and dill weed in a large bowl, mixing well. Add onion, olives, 1/2 cup cheese and shrimp; Stir well.
2. Stir in noodles.
3. Spoon mixture into a lightly greased shallow 2 quart casserole.
4. Cover and bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
5. Uncover and sprinkle with remaining 1/2 cup cheese.
6. Bake an additional 5 minutes or until the cheese melts.
7. Yield: 6 to 8 servings.

title: Beefy Tortilla Pie
description: A hearty Beefy Tortilla Pie, layered with seasoned ground chuck, green chilies, and cheese, baked into a satisfying savoury dish. Garnish with your favourite toppings for a customizable meal that's bursting with flavour.
instructions:
1. Brown ground chuck and onions; drain and add seasonings.
2. Add all the cans, undrained.
3. Heat and garnish with sour cream, shredded cheese and taco chips as desired.

title: Scandinavian Rye Bread
description: A rustic Scandinavian Rye Bread featuring a rich blend of rye and all-purpose flour, with butter and salt for a deep, satisfying flavour. Enjoy the comforting aroma of freshly baked bread from your own kitchen.
instructions:
1. Choose mixing and kneading method, except food processor.
2. Add butter and salt to Rye Sponge.
3. Add all-purpose flour as specified.
4. Knead.
5. Let dough rise 45 minutes to 1 hour.

title: Grandma'S Sauerkraut
description: Grandma's Sauerkraut, a simple yet flavourful dish combining crispy bacon, sautéed onions, and tangy sauerkraut with caraway seeds. This classic side dish is a perfect accompaniment to many hearty meals.
instructions:
1. In a good size frypan, fry bacon until crisp.
2. Add onion and saute until onion is limp.
3. Fork in drained sauerkraut and stir until ingredients are evenly mixed.
4. Adjust the flame until it is as low as possible.
5. Continue to cook until sauerkraut begins to brown.
6. Stir in caraway seed.
7. Try not to eat all the sauerkraut out of the pan before serving!

title: Mercy'S Messy Nachos
description: Mercy's Messy Nachos, a delightful layering of tortilla chips, warm chili, melted cheese, and fresh toppings like onions, lettuce, and tomato. A perfect, fun dish for sharing and casual gatherings.
instructions:
1. Layer large platter with chips.
2. Heat chili and layer on chips.
3. Heat Cheez Whiz and layer.
4. Chop onions, lettuce and tomato and sprinkle on top.
5. Spread sour cream in middle.

title: Fresh Spinach And Mushroom Salad
description: A vibrant Fresh Spinach and Mushroom Salad featuring a zesty homemade dressing with garlic, thyme, olive oil, and white wine vinegar. This simple and refreshing salad makes for a healthy and quick side dish.
instructions:
1. In a small bowl or jar, combine garlic, salt, thyme, oil and vinegar.
2. Shake and pour over salad.

title: Polish Sausage Skillet Supper
description: A comforting Polish Sausage Skillet Supper, combining browned sausage, onions, green peppers, and tender potatoes in a single pan. This hearty and easy-to-prepare meal is perfect for a satisfying family dinner.
instructions:
1. In a large skillet, brown sausage on all sides.
2. Drain.
3. Add remaining ingredients.
4. Cover and cook over low heat 20 minutes until potatoes are tender.
5. Makes 4 servings.
6. Has 680 calories per serving.

title: Polish Stuffed Cabbage
description: Authentic Polish Stuffed Cabbage featuring tender cabbage leaves wrapped around a savoury filling of rice, onion, and mixture, baked in a rich tomato soup. This traditional dish is a labour of love, perfect for special occasions.
instructions:
1. Core head of cabbage.
2. Set cabbage hole down in large pot with 1 or 2 inches of water and steam.
3. Pull off leaves, one by one (total of about 22 to 26).
4. Cook rice.
5. Saute chopped onion in butter.
6. Mix meat loaf mixture with the rice and onion mixture with your hands.
7. Add eggs to the mixture and salt and pepper to taste.
8. Take each cabbage leaf and put some meat in the middle. Wrap envelope-style tightly.
9. Put 1 small box of raisins on bottom of pan.
10. Tightly pack 2 rows of cabbage balls in same pot as you boiled cabbage.
11. Sprinkle another few raisins on top.
12. Add tomato soup and 2 cans water.
13. Cover and bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer for 1 1/2 hours.
14. Serve with mashed potatoes and cucumber salad.

title: Polish Mazurki
description: Delightful Polish Mazurki, a sweet and buttery base topped with raspberry jam and a flavorful mixture of coconut, nuts, and vanilla. These traditional Polish bars are perfect for a festive treat or coffee break.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°.
2. Blend sugar, butter and flour.
3. Pat and press mixture into 13 x 9-inch pan.
4. Bake for 10 minutes.
5. Cool. Spread baked layer with jam.
6. Mix remaining ingredients in bowl and spread over jam.
7. Bake 20 to 25 minutes in 350° oven.
8. Cool and sprinkle with confectioners sugar.

title: Potato Dumpling Platter
description: A hearty Potato Dumpling Platter featuring savory pierogies, smoky kielbasa, and tender cabbage and onions sautéed until lightly browned. This comforting dish celebrates classic Polish flavours in a simple meal.
instructions:
1. Coarsely slice cabbage.
2. Discard tough ribs.
3. Thinly slice onion.
4. In hot salad oil, cook cabbage, onion, salt and pepper 20 minutes or until vegetables are tender and lightly browned, stirring occasionally.
5. Add sausage.

title: Borscht(Soup)
description: A classic Borscht (Soup) with tender meat, cabbage, potatoes, and a fresh blend of parsley and dill, finished with a swirl of sour cream. This comforting soup warms the soul with its rich, traditional flavours.
instructions:
1. Cook meat until tender.
2. Add all other ingredients, except tomatoes and cream.
3. Boil 15 minutes.
4. Add tomatoes and cook until done.
5. Add cream and serve.

title: Easy Stuffed Cabbage
description: Easy Stuffed Cabbage, a convenient version of the classic featuring ground meat and barley wrapped in tender cabbage, baked in a flavourful tomato sauce. This comforting dish offers a delicious meal with less fuss.
instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 350°. Cook the quick barley according to instructions on box. When done, rinse and drain. Cut up head of cabbage into pieces. Place in a large cooking vessel and boil for 5 to 7 minutes. Drain cabbage after cooked. In a large bowl, mix cooked cabbage and barley, ground meat, diced onion, salt and pepper. After mixed, pour into the 9 x 13 baking dish. In a bowl, mix the tomato soup with 1/2 can of water and 1/2 can of milk. Pour this over the cabbage mixture in the 9 x 13 dish (if any mixture is left, it can be added while baking because some of the liquid will evaporate during the baking process). Cover with foil. Bake for approximately 1 1/2 hours at 350°. Let set 5 minutes before serving.

title: Sweet-Sour Kraut And Pork Chops
description: A savoury Sweet-Sour Kraut and Pork Chops dish, featuring tender pork baked with potatoes, onions, pineapple, and sauerkraut for a delightful balance of flavours. This hearty meal promises warmth and comforting tastes.
instructions: 1. In 12 x 7 x 2-inch baking dish, combine potato slices and onion. Add water. Cover and bake in 350° oven for 45 minutes or until nearly tender.

title: Borschtsch Ukrainsky
description: An authentic Borschtsch Ukrainsky, a rich beef broth base with tender beets, cabbage, carrots, and tomatoes, all brightened with a touch of vinegar and finished with sour cream. This traditional soup is a labour of love, brimming with complex, savoury flavours.
instructions: 1. Cook beef in bouillon. (The exact amount wasn't mentioned in the original recipe. I assume it should be "enough.") Cut beet roots, carrots, parsley and onions in strips; put them into another large pan. Add tomatoes or tomato puree, vinegar, sugar and a little bit of beef broth with lard (or 1 to 2 tablespoons butter). Cover with lid and cook for a while. Stir the vegetables from time to time. If necessary, add some more broth or water.

title: Stuffed Cabbage Or Cabbage Rolls
description: Hearty Stuffed Cabbage or Cabbage Rolls, filled with a seasoned mixture of hamburger, pork, and rice, then baked in a savoury tomato soup. This comforting classic delivers rich flavours and satisfying tenderness in every bite.
instructions:
1. Boil large cabbage head until leaves can be pulled off head. Mix all remaining ingredients and roll into a ball; place in cabbage leaf.
2. Tuck ends in and place in pan.
3. Bake in tomato soup for 1 hour.

title: Red And Green Cabbage Slaw
description: A vibrant Red and Green Cabbage Slaw, tossed in a zesty Asian-inspired dressing with orange juice, soy sauce, sesame oil, and fresh ginger. This crisp and refreshing salad is perfect as a vibrant side dish.
instructions:
1. In large bowl, combine vegetable oil, orange juice, soy sauce, sesame oil, vinegar, ginger, garlic and pepper.
2. Using fork, beat well to blend; set aside.

title: Sauerkraut-Raisin Drops
description: Unique Sauerkraut-Raisin Drops, soft and fluffy cookies with an unusual yet delicious twist of sauerkraut, raisins, and warm spices like cinnamon. A surprisingly delightful treat, perfect for adventurous bakers.
instructions:
1. In mixer bowl, beat the 3/4 cup butter or margarine and the brown sugar until fluffy.
2. Add the egg, the first 2 tablespoons of milk and the 1 teaspoon of vanilla.
3. Beat until fluffy.

title: Borsch (Vegetable Beef Soup)(Ukranian)
description: Hearty beef and vegetable soup with beets, cabbage, and potatoes, slow-cooked to perfection. This traditional dish is usually served with a dollop of sour cream.
instructions: 1. Peel beet and cook it with beef stew meat in a medium size pot. Fill 3/4 of pot with water. Cook on high until it boils. Then reduce to medium to low heat and cook for 1 hour. During this hour peel potatoes and cut into cubes. Shred cabbage.

title: Cheese Balls(Baked)
description: Simple and quick baked cheese balls, perfect as a snack or appetizer. Made with just three ingredients and baked until golden.
instructions:
1. Blend until smooth.
2. Make into balls.
3. Bake at 375° for 10 to 15 minutes.
4. Makes 1 1/2 dozen.

title: Liz'S Stuffed Cabbage
description: Classic stuffed cabbage rolls filled with a savory mixture of ground beef, rice, and onions, simmered in a delicate tomato sauce. A comforting and satisfying meal.
instructions:
1. Mix all ingredients, except cabbage and tomato sauce.
2. Add to mixture 2 tablespoons tomato sauce.
3. Place in pot the green leaves of cabbage.
4. Boil until tender.
5. Wrap cabbage leaves around small balls of meat and rice mixture until all meat mix is used.
6. Place in pot.
7. Chop remaining cabbage; pour over stuffed cabbage.
8. Add garlic and tomato sauce.
9. Simmer for 45 minutes, covered.
10. Add little water if needed.
11. Avoid excessive stirring.
12. Be sure cabbage mix does not stick.

title: Patty Melts
description: A quick and delicious patty melt featuring a hamburger patty, melted cheese, and grilled onions between slices of buttered rye bread. Perfect for a satisfying lunch or dinner.
instructions:
1. Fry ground beef, made into 3 patties.
2. Butter each slice of bread on one side.
3. Place hamburger patty, cheese and onions between 2 slices of bread and grill until golden brown on each side.

title: Mushroom Salad(France)
description: A light and refreshing mushroom salad tossed with a zesty lemon-garlic dressing. Fresh mushrooms are complemented by crisp lettuce and parsley for a vibrant side dish.
instructions:
1. Clean mushrooms; wash in cold water to which vinegar has been added.
2. Dry in a cloth; slice.
3. To keep mushrooms from turning black, sprinkle with lemon juice.
4. Toss lightly but thoroughly with mixture of mayonnaise, garlic, salt and pepper. Place in refrigerator for 1 or 2 hours; mixture will become moist from juice of mushrooms.
5. Stir lightly several times during chilling. Serve on a bed of bibb lettuce and parsley.
6. Yields 10 to 12 servings.

title: Mocked Cabbage Rolls
description: Convenient slow-cooker 'mocked' cabbage rolls, layered with ground beef, rice, and cabbage, then simmered in a savory sauce. A simple way to enjoy a classic comfort food without the rolling.
instructions:
1. Brown ground beef and onion.
2. In crock-pot, put in layers the chopped cabbage, ground beef, onion and rice, then the jar of sauce.
3. Put 1 cup water in jar and shake.
4. Pour on top. Cover with lid and cook on low overnight or cook on high for 5 to 6 hours. You can also bake in oven, but you steam the cabbage in 1/2 cup water and use 3 cups cooked rice.
5. Place in layers in a large casserole.
6. Bake for 1 hour in a 350° oven. You can also use ground turkey in both ways.

title: Kapusniak - Sauerkraut Soup
description: A traditional Polish sauerkraut soup (Kapusniak) made with tender pork, tangy sauerkraut, and a creamy, flavorful broth. Perfect for a hearty and warming meal, often served with potatoes or dumplings.
instructions:
1. Cook meat in water until well done.
2. Add onion, sauerkraut juice and sugar.
3. Beat egg, flour, milk and cream.
4. Add to soup and bring to boiling point.
5. Serve with potatoes or dumplings.

title: Golabki (Cabbage Rolls)
description: Authentic Polish Golabki (cabbage rolls) filled with a delectable mix of ground beef, pork, rice, and seasonings, then baked in a rich tomato sauce. A labor of love that yields comforting results.
instructions:
1. Remove core from cabbage.
2. Scald cabbage in boiling water and remove leaves as they soften.
3. Saute onions in margarine very lightly; do not brown.
4. Combine with the meat, rice, eggs and seasonings.
5. Mix well.
6. Place 2 tablespoons of mixture in center of cabbage leaf and roll.
7. Wrap and push sides of roll in with finger to hold together.
8. Pour tomato sauce on rolls, then squeeze tomatoes from can and arrange on top of rolls.
9. Simmer over low heat for 2 hours in a heavy soup pot.

title: Chicken Cheese Tortilla Casserole
description: A cheesy and comforting chicken tortilla casserole, featuring tender chicken, creamy soups, green chilies, and melted cheeses. Layered with corn tortillas for a delightful texture.
instructions:
1. Cook chicken until done.
2. Combine soups, chilies, and Velveeta in medium pan and beat on low. Cut up chicken and add to mixture. (Add juice from chicken also.)
3. Heat grease in a skillet and heat tortillas 20 to 30 seconds on each side or until soft.
4. Layer tortillas and chicken mixture in a medium casserole dish.
5. Begin and end with tortillas.
6. Top with Cheddar cheese and bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

title: Rye Bread Dip(For Vegetables Or Chips)
description: A quick and flavorful rye bread dip with sour cream, mayonnaise, onion, and herbs. Perfect for serving with vegetables or chips for an effortless appetizer.
instructions: 1. Mix ingredients and serve with rye bread.

title: Party Rye Snacks
description: Savory party rye snacks topped with a delicious blend of seasoned hamburger, sausage, and melted Velveeta cheese. These small bites are perfect for any gathering.
instructions:
1. Brown hamburger and sausage together; drain the grease.
2. Put meat in a bowl.
3. Melt cheese in a pan on low heat.
4. Add meat to cheese; stir together.
5. Add seasonings; stir.
6. Line cookie sheet with bread; spoon meat onto bread.
7. Bake at 350° for 8 to 10 minutes, or pop them in the microwave.
8. You can freeze them on the cookie sheets and place them in plastic bags to be eaten later.

title: Simply Great! Pineapple Blintzes
description: Light and fluffy pineapple blintzes made with a sweet yogurt-pineapple filling and delicate whole wheat crepes. A delightful treat for breakfast or dessert, offering a burst of fruity flavor.
instructions:
1. Spoon the yogurt into a sieve lined with cheesecloth.
2. Place over a bowl and set aside to drain for 20 minutes.
3. Mix yogurt and pineapple in bowl; set aside.
4. Place egg whites in medium bowl and whisk until frothy.
5. Wisk in the milk and honey.
6. Add flour and baking powder; mix well.
7. Spray pan with Pam; allow pan to get warm.
8. Add about 1/4 cup batter to the pan and tilt the pan in all directions to spread the batter thinly all over bottom.
9. Cook 1 minute or until brown, browned the other side.
10. Place on wire rack to cool.
11. Repeat until all the batter is used.
12. To serve, divide the pineapple filling among this blintzes, spooning down the center of each.
13. Fold in the sides.
14. Makes 12.

title: Dave'S Mom'S Potato Pancakes
description: Classic potato pancakes, crispy on the outside and tender on the inside, made with grated potatoes, eggs, flour, and onion. A delicious and versatile dish, perfect as a side or light meal.
instructions:
1. Place potato grating on a towel and wring the towel to extract as much moisture from the potatoes as possible.
2. Place potatoes in a bowl and stir in eggs.
3. Add flour, salt and onion.
4. Shape into 1/4-inch thick, 3-inch diameter (small) patties.
5. Saute in 1/4-inch or more hot oil.
6. Turn and brown until crisp.
7. Best served hot.

title: Pierogi Shells
description: Cheesy pierogi shells stuffed with a creamy potato and Velveeta mixture, then baked and topped with buttered grilled onions. A comforting and satisfying twist on traditional pierogi.
instructions:
1. Mash potatoes and add Velveeta.
2. Stuff shells with potato mixture and put in glass baking dish.
3. Grill onions and butter in pan, then pour over shells.
4. Cover and heat through at 350°.
5. This tastes like pierogi, only it's a quicker way.

title: Little Pizzas For Teens
description: Simple and quick little pizzas made on party rye bread with pimento cheese and Italian seasoning. A perfect snack or appetizer, especially popular with teens.
instructions:
1. Spread party rye bread with pimento cheese.
2. Sprinkle with seasonings.
3. Place on cookie sheet and bake at 250° until cheese melts.

title: Polish Sausage And Potato Soup
description: Hearty Polish sausage and potato soup featuring kielbasa, cabbage, carrots, and a savory beef broth. A comforting and flavorful meal perfect for a chilly day.
instructions:
1. In a large kettle, melt butter and add kielbasa, onion and celery.
2. Cook until very tender.
3. Add remaining ingredients, except potatoes.
4. Cover and cook for 1 1/2 hours.
5. Add potatoes, cover and cook 25 minutes longer.
6. Good with hot French bread.

title: Russian Potato Pancakes
description: Crispy Russian potato pancakes, finely shredded and fried to golden perfection, often served with sour cream. A delightful and traditional side dish or light meal.
instructions: 1. Into a large bowl half filled with cold water, coarsely shred potatoes and small onion. In a colander lined with a clean towel or cheesecloth, drain shredded potatoes and onion. Wrap potatoes and onion in towel. Squeeze towel to remove as much water as possible. In the same large bowl, beat eggs. Add the shredded potatoes and onion, flour, salt and pepper. Toss together until well mixed. In 12-inch skillet over medium heat in hot salad oil, drop mixture by scant 1/4 cupfuls into 4 mounds. With pancake turner, flatten each mound to make a 4-inch pancake. Cook until golden brown on one side. This is about 4 minutes. Turn and brown on the other side. Remove to paper towel-lined cookie sheet to drain. Keep warm. Repeat to make about 12 more pancakes, stirring mixture occasionally. Garnish with parsley sprigs or sour cream.

title: Cashew Chicken(Serves 4)
description: Tender cashew chicken marinated in a sweet and savory honey-soy sauce, baked with pineapple and red pepper. A flavorful and easy weeknight dinner with a delightful crunch from cashews.
instructions:
1. In small bowl, combine garlic, honey, soy sauce and ginger.
2. In 11 x 7-inch baking dish, arrange chicken.
3. Spoon about 2 tablespoons garlic mixture over chicken.
4. Cook, covered, at 350° for 30 minutes or until done.
5. Spoon juices over chicken halfway through.

title: Mild Mexican Casserole
description: A comforting and mildly spicy Mexican casserole with seasoned ground meat, jalapeños, and creamy soups layered with tortilla chips and melted Cheddar cheese. Perfect for a family dinner.
instructions:
1. Brown the meat, onions and peppers.
2. Drain the fat.
3. Add the milk, enchilada sauce and 2 soups.
4. (It works better if you combine all this before you add it to the pan.)
5. In a Pyrex casserole, alternate the tortilla chips, meat mixture and a little cheese twice, ending with the bulk of the cheese on the top. Bake at 350° for 40 to 45 minutes.

title: Beef Dip In Bread
description: A creative party dip served inside a hollowed-out rye bread loaf, filled with a creamy mix of sour cream, mayonnaise, herbs, and chipped beef. Bread chunks are used for dipping.
instructions:
1. With fingers pull out bite sized chunks of bread until about an inch shell of bread remains.
2. Fill with a mixture of the other ingredients and place bread chunks around outside for dipping.

title: Bunue Los(Sweet Treats)
description: These sweet treats feature crispy fried flour tortillas coated in a delightful mix of sugar, nutmeg, and cinnamon, offering a quick and satisfying dessert.
instructions:
1. Combine first 3 ingredients in a large plastic bag, mixing well and set aside.
2. Cut flour tortillas into 3 (12-inch) strips; fry a few at a time in hot oil until crisp and golden brown. Drain on paper towel.
3. While they are still hot place a few at a time in the bag that has the sugar mixture and shake gently to coat them.
4. Store in an airtight container.
5. Makes about 5 dozen.

title: Chicken Sour Cream Enchiladas
description: Chicken Sour Cream Enchiladas are a hearty casserole dish with tender chicken, condensed cream, green chilies, and sour cream, layered with tortillas and topped with Cheddar cheese.
instructions: 1. Cut chicken into bite-size pieces. Set aside. Mix chicken soup, chilies and sour cream. Place a layer of tortillas (whole or strips) in bottom of a 2 1/2-quart casserole dish. Spoon on soup mixture, then chicken pieces. Sprinkle with cheese. Repeat all layers. Bake at 350° for 40 minutes. Serves 4 to 6.

title: Party Rye Loaf
description: This Party Rye Loaf turns a simple bread into a delicious dip bowl, filled with a creamy mix of sour cream, mayonnaise, dill, onion, parsley, and chipped beef, perfect for sharing.
instructions:
1. Mix all ingredients except bread.
2. Hollow out loaf of bread so that loaf resembles a bowl.
3. Fill with dip.
4. Cut up bread that was scooped into bite size pieces.

title: Citrus Beet Soup
description: A refreshing Citrus Beet Soup combines the earthy sweetness of beets with a bright tang of orange and lemon juice, balanced with chicken broth and a swirl of low-fat yogurt.
instructions:
1. Combine the onion, beets and orange juice in a 2-quart casserole.
2. Cover tightly and cook on High for 8 to 12 minutes or until the beets are tender, stirring once or twice.
3. Add the lemon juice, broth, cucumber and pepper.
4. Cover again and cook on High for 3 to 5 minutes, or until heated through, but not boiling.

title: Borsch(Ukrainian)
description: This traditional Ukrainian Borsch features savory beef broth, tender beets, cabbage, and tomatoes, all brightened with lemon juice and often served with a dollop of sour cream.
instructions:
1. Brown beef bones.
2. Add 3 cups water and simmer at least 1 hour.
3. Remove bones. Cool broth and skim off all fat.
4. Cut off meat from bones and cut into small pieces. Add to cooled broth. Saute chopped onion in oil until golden.
5. Add beets, carrot and celery.
6. Cook 5 minutes.
7. Add 6 cups boiling water, 1 whole onion, 1 bay leaf, dill, lemon juice and tomatoes. Simmer 15 minutes. Add cabbage and simmer another 20 minutes. Remove the bay leaf, whole onion and dill stalk. Add remaining mixture to the beef broth. Add potatoes and, if desired, a can of mixed vegetables. Heat.
8. Serve with a dollop of sour cream.

title: Beef Stroganoff For 60
description: A classic Beef Stroganoff for a crowd, this dish features tender ground beef sautéed with onions, simmered in a rich sauce of catsup, beef broth, and mushroom soup, finished with sour cream.
instructions: 1. Saute onion in butter until golden brown. Add ground beef and cook until browned, stirring occasionally. Blend in remaining ingredients, except sour cream. Heat until bubbling. Just before serving, stir in sour cream. Serve over noodles or rice.

title: Party Pieces
description: Party Pieces are a simple and satisfying appetizer, combining browned ground chuck and sausage with melted Velveeta cheese, served hot on small squares of rye bread.
instructions:
1. Brown ground chuck and hot sausage in skillet until done. Drain.
2. Slice or cube Velveeta cheese and add to ground chuck and hot sausage. Simmer until cheese is melted.
3. Serve on small squares of party rye bread or whatever you prefer.

title: Sara Jane Gilliam'S Franks And Kraut
description: Frank and Kraut are elevated with this recipe, featuring jumbo franks stuffed with sauerkraut, spiral-wrapped in bacon, then grilled or broiled until perfectly crisp.
instructions:
1. Split franks lengthwise 2/3 deep.
2. Fill slit with sauerkraut. Spiral wrap each frank with 1 slice of bacon and secure with toothpick.
3. Grill or broil until bacon is well done.
4. Delicious!

title: Russian Salad Dressing
description: A classic Russian Salad Dressing, this simple concoction blends confectioners' sugar, vinegar, garlic, catsup, and oil for a versatile and tangy flavor.
instructions: 1. Mix well in blender or mixer.

title: Cheese Kolacky(Polish)
description: Delicious Cheese Kolacky are a Polish pastry made with a rich dough of butter, cream cheese, and flour, perfect for filling with sweet preserves or cheeses.
instructions:
1. Cream shortening.
2. Mix in 1 package cream cheese at a time. Add flour.
3. Continue mixing by hand until mixture works away from side of bowl. Cover with waxed paper.
4. Place in refrigerator overnight.
5. Roll a small portion at a time in a cool place on powdered sugar until 1/8-inch thick.
6. Cut in squares or rounds. Fill with favorite filling (Solo in cans works well).
7. Bake 10 to 15 minutes at 325°.

title: Cabbage And White Beans
description: This quick and flavorful Cabbage and White Beans dish combines savory kielbasa with crisp cabbage, garlic, and fresh green onions, simmered in chicken broth and white wine.
instructions:
1. Mix first 3 ingredients in skillet.
2. Add cabbage and onions. Stir-fry for 1 minute and add to skillet remaining ingredients. Heat until cabbage is crisp-tender and serve immediately.

title: Tony'S Cube Steak Stacks
description: Tony's Cube Steak Stacks feature tender beef cube steaks seasoned with cracked pepper, quickly browned and served with toasted rye bread, onions, and Parmesan cheese.
instructions:
1. Sprinkle the steaks with cracked pepper, using from 1/4 to 1/2 teaspoon, depending on your taste preference.
2. Preheat broiler. Heat the oil and butter in heavy skillet.
3. Brown the steaks quickly about 4 minutes per side.

title: Christmas Tortilla Roll-Up
description: These colorful Christmas Tortilla Roll-Ups are a festive appetizer, filled with a creamy mixture of cream cheese, celery, green onion, red pepper, and green olives.
instructions: 1. Mix ingredients together well. Refrigerate. Spread on tortilla; then roll tortilla up. Slice into 1-inch pieces.

title: Polish Kraut And Wieners
description: Polish Kraut and Wieners is a comforting and hearty dish, featuring savory sausage browned with tender cabbage and onions, all brought together with Italian dressing.
instructions:
1. Cut sausage into bite-size pieces.
2. Brown in large skillet. Add cabbage, onions and dressing.
3. Cook on low until cabbage is tender.
4. Serve with cornbread.

title: Holubky(Slovak Stuffed Cabbage)
description: Holubky, or Slovak Stuffed Cabbage, are a traditional and satisfying dish, with tender cabbage leaves wrapped around a flavorful filling of rice, pork, and beef, simmered in tomatoes and sauerkraut.
instructions:
1. Combine meat, rice, egg and seasonings.
2. Core cabbage and parboil about 20 minutes, covered.
3. Separate leaves, when cool; stuff with meat mixture and roll up.
4. In large kettle, place a layer of sauerkraut, then a layer of cabbage rolls, then a layer of tomatoes, then pieces of pork shoulder, seasoning as you go. When all is used, cover to the top of the kettle with boiling water and cook over medium heat about 2 1/2 hours.

title: Cinnamon Blintzes
description: Cinnamon Blintzes offer a sweet treat, made from thin, rolled-out bread filled with a creamy mixture of cream cheese, egg yolks, and sugar, then coated in a buttery cinnamon-sugar blend.
instructions:
1. Trim bread and roll out until thin.
2. Cream the cream cheese, egg yolks and sugar.
3. Spread thinly on the bread.
4. Roll up.
5. Melt butter.
6. In separate bowl mix brown sugar and cinnamon.
7. Dip rolls first in butter then in cinnamon-sugar mixture.
8. Place close together on cookie sheet.
9. Freeze.
10. Remove from freezer and slice each roll in half.
11. Put in oven straight from freezer on greased cookie sheet.
12. Bake at 350° for 15 to 20 minutes.
13. Makes 8 dozen.

title: Galumpki'S(Ukrainian Stuffed Cabbage)
description: Galumpki's, also known as Ukrainian Stuffed Cabbage, are a comforting classic with tender cabbage leaves wrapped around a savory filling of hamburg and rice, simmered in a rich tomato sauce.
instructions:
1. Core cabbage, parboil and peel whole leaves off.
2. Set aside. In large skillet, saute small cubed salt pork and one large onion (diced) until salt pork is golden brown and onion translucent.
3. Put hamburg and rice into bowl; add salt pork and onion mixture and salt and pepper.
4. Mix well.
5. Roll in cabbage leaves and set aside. In large pot put garlic, 1 large onion (chopped), 1 cup salt pork chunks and 1 can tomato puree, then stack cabbage rolls tight. Dump stewed tomatoes between layers and on top.
6. Cover.
7. Cook over low heat 3 to 4 hours or until done.

title: Surneye Paeske(Russian Easter Cheese Mold)
description: Surneye Paeske, a traditional Russian Easter Cheese Mold, combines rich cream cheese and butter with sugar, vanilla, lemon, and candied orange rind, often studded with almonds and raisins.
instructions:
1. Cook orange rind in 3 to 4 tablespoons sugar and 2 tablespoons water.
2. Simmer on low for 45 minutes.
3. Do the same with the lemon rind.

title: Ham And Egg Breakfast Souffle
description: This Ham and Egg Breakfast Souffle is a delicious and cheesy morning meal, combining crisp bacon, savory ham, and eggs baked into a fluffy, flavorful dish.
instructions:
1. Fry bacon; drain and crumble.
2. Reserve 2 tablespoons bacon grease.
3. Mix together cornmeal, cheese, salt and pepper.
