4. In a skillet, heat 1 tablespoon bacon grease with half of cooking oil. Coat tomato slices well with cornmeal mixture.
5. When grease is sizzling hot, cook tomatoes on both sides until crisp and lightly brown.
6. Repeat with remaining.
7. Drain on paper towel.
8. Transfer fried tomatoes to a large plate; sprinkle with crumbled bacon. Serve hot.

title: Apple Puffed Pancakes
description: These delightful Apple Puffed Pancakes are light and airy, with a sweet apple topping and a hint of cinnamon, perfect for a special breakfast or brunch.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°.
2. In a blender or large bowl, mix eggs, milk, flour, granulated sugar, vanilla, salt and cinnamon until blended.
3. If using a mixer, batter will be slightly lumpy.

title: Stuffed Cabbage With Meat(Malfoof Mihshee)
description: Classic stuffed cabbage rolls with a flavorful meat and rice filling, simmered to perfection. This dish offers a comforting and hearty experience, perfect for a family meal.
instructions:
1. Makes approximately 60.
2. Boil a large pot of water with 1 tablespoon salt.
3. Cut all around core of cabbage with a small sharp knife.
4. Put cabbage in boiling water and separate leaf by leaf.
5. Cut large leaves into 2 to 3 pieces removing the thick part of the stem and prepare stuffing.
6. Combine meat, rice, salt, pepper and allspice.
7. Add 2 tablespoons lemon juice.
8. Place 1 tablespoon stuffing on each leaf in log shape.
9. Begin to roll folding over sides.
10. Squeeze and place in pot.
11. In the pot you should have a lamb bone for flavor and the pieces of stem you have trimmed away.
12. Arrange cabbage rolls in rows and layers, placing slices of garlic in between.
13. When process is complete, mash 1 clove garlic with 1 teaspoon salt and dried mint.
14. Pour water over cabbage to just cover.
15. Bring to a boil.
16. Add mashed garlic mixture and 2 tablespoons lemon juice.
17. Cover with a plate to weigh down.
18. Cover and simmer 30 minutes on very low heat.

title: Bill'S Goulash
description: A quick and easy goulash made with hamburger and pork, enhanced with Worcestershire sauce and mustard. This simple, comforting dish is perfect for a weeknight meal.
instructions:
1. Scramble fry hamburger.
2. Add chopped onion.
3. Drain grease. Add rest of the ingredients and cook slowly to heat through.

title: Borsch(Ukrainian Christmas Soup)
description: A traditional Ukrainian Christmas soup, featuring earthy mushrooms and vibrant beets, simmered into a rich and flavorful broth. This hearty soup is a festive and comforting starter for any special occasion.
instructions:
1. Simmer the dried mushrooms in 3 cups of water for 1 hour. Strain them through a cheese cloth, but save the stock.
2. Wash mushrooms thoroughly and save.

title: Borscht
description: A hearty borscht brimming with chicken, beans, and colorful vegetables like beets and cabbage, simmered in a rich broth. Garnished with fresh parsley and a dollop of sour cream, this soup is both nourishing and deeply satisfying.
instructions:
1. Fill big pot with hot water.
2. Put in chicken and dry beans. Pour in beet juice from can.
3. Cut beets into narrow strips and add to water before it boils.
4. Bring to boil and cook 1 hour.

title: Burrito Surprise
description: A simple and quick burrito filling featuring softened cream cheese, olives, and green chiles. This versatile mixture can be wrapped in tortillas for an easy snack or light meal.
instructions:
1. Soften cream cheese.
2. Add all other ingredients, including juice from green chilies or a little jalapeno juice if you like it hot (I add this).
3. Mix with fork.
4. Place a little filling on a tortilla (burrito style) and roll up.
5. Put enough on the tortilla so you can taste it.
6. Chill well.
7. Slice when ready to serve. Makes at least 5 burritos.
8. Keeps refrigerated for a week.

title: Broccoli-Mushroom Salad
description: A refreshing and crisp salad combining fresh broccoli, water chestnuts, celery, mushrooms, and black olives. Dressed with Italian dressing, it's best marinated overnight for enhanced flavor.
instructions:
1. Mix all vegetables together.
2. Sprinkle dry dressing over, then liquid dressing.
3. Toss and marinate overnight.

title: Beet Borscht(Good To Make In Early A.M. To Be Served On A Very Hot Day.)
description: A cooling and vibrant beet borscht, perfect for a hot day, blended with lemon, dill, and scallions. This refreshing soup is finished with a creamy swirl of sour cream or yogurt, making it both light and flavorful.
instructions:
1. In blender or food processor, combine beets with stock.
2. Add lemon, dill, salt and scallions.
3. Mix until smooth.
4. Pour into a bowl; blend in the sour cream or sour cream and yogurt.
5. Chill in refrigerator until very cold.
6. Taste for seasonings.
7. It should be nice and lemony.
8. Top each bowl with another spoonful of sour cream and the chopped chives.

title: Pork Chops And Kraut
description: Tender pork chops baked with tangy sauerkraut, seasoned simply with salt and pepper. This comforting and easy dish offers a harmonious blend of savory and sour flavors, perfect for a family dinner.
instructions:
1. In
2. casserole
3. dish,
4. arrange
5. pork
6. chops
7. in a single layer. Salt and pepper to taste.
8. Cover pork chops with layer of sauerkraut.
9. Cover
10. casserole with aluminum foil.
11. Bake in slow oven, 200°, for 3 to 4 hours or until chops are fork-tender.

title: Borsch
description: A robust borscht loaded with vegetables like beets and cabbage, simmered in beef stock and seasoned with warming spices. This soup balances savory and slightly sweet notes with the addition of honey and vinegar, creating a truly comforting bowl.
instructions:
1. Place stock, vegetables and seasonings in soup kettle.
2. Bring to a boil; reduce heat, then cover and simmer 45 minutes. Saute chopped onion in oil until golden; remove from heat and add honey, vinegar, tomato and Tamari. Stir mixture into soup. Garnish soup with sour cream or plain yogurt; serve with dark rye bread. Yields 2 1/2 to 3 quarts. Best made a few hours in advance.

title: Russian Tea Ii
description: A quick-to-prepare blend of instant tea, sugar, cinnamon, allspice, and lemonade, perfect for a comforting warm drink. This aromatic mix stores well and is ready whenever you need a cozy cuppa.
instructions:
1. Mix and use as needed.
2. Stores well in airtight covered container.

title: Baked Split Pea Soup
description: A hearty baked split pea soup featuring tender kielbasa sausage, carrots, celery, and onions, slow-cooked to perfection. This comforting dish is rich in flavor and requires minimal stirring.
instructions:
1. Slice sausage 1/4 inch thick.
2. Add this after the first 2 hours of baking.
3. Bake above ingredients 2 hours at 350° in covered Dutch oven (not necessary to stir at all).
4. Bake 30 minutes more after adding sausage or until peas are tender and sausage heated through.
5. (You may use fresh onion and chicken broth for part of liquid.)
6. Could also use ham bone and chunks of cooked ham.

title: Little Pizzas
description: Miniature pizzas made with savory hot sausage, creamy Velveeta cheese, and tangy ketchup, spread on party rye bread. Topped with pizza sauce and Parmesan, these make a perfect appetizer or snack.
instructions:
1. Cook sausages; drain well.
2. Mix all other ingredients together until cheese melts.
3. Put about 1 spoon of mixture on 1 slice of bread.
4. Add 1 teaspoon pizza sauce on top.
5. Sprinkle with Parmesan or Mozzarella cheese.
6. Freeze on cookie sheet.
7. Put in bags to keep frozen until needed.
8. Take out of freezer, put on cookie sheet and heat at 400° for 10 minutes.

title: Swiss Potato Torte
description: A savory Swiss potato torte layered with thinly sliced potatoes, fragrant garlic, thyme, and rich cheese, baked until golden in chicken broth. This elegant dish makes a superb side or light main course.
instructions:
1. Grease 1 1/2-quart baking dish with cooking spray.
2. Pare and thinly slice potatoes in bowl.
3. Toss potatoes.
4. Add parsley, garlic, salt and spices in prepared dish.
5. Alternating with layers of potatoes, add broth.
6. Cover with foil.
7. Bake at 400° for 30 minutes.
8. Remove foil and bake 30 minutes longer.
9. Sprinkle with remaining cheese.
10. Bake until potatoes are tender, about 15 minutes.
11. Sprinkle with parsley.
12. Serves 8.

title: Ham And Purslane On Rye(A Delectable Sandwich)
description: A simple yet delectable sandwich featuring savory ham and fresh purslane on hearty rye bread, enhanced with a kick of horseradish. This quick snack offers a delightful balance of flavors and textures.
instructions: 1. For the bread you can use whole wheat, pumpernickel or sour dough instead of rye.

title: Banana Pecan Torte
description: A moist and flavorful banana pecan torte, rich with ripe bananas and crunchy pecans, and a hint of vanilla. This delightful cake is perfect for dessert or a special tea-time treat.
instructions:
1. In mixing bowl, cream butter and sugar.
2. Add eggs and beat. Beat in bananas and vanilla.
3. Add dry ingredients to creamed mixture alternately with buttermilk.
4. Stir in pecans.
5. Pour into 3 greased and floured 9-inch round cake pans.
6. Bake at 350° for 30 to 35 minutes or until a toothpick inserted near the center comes out clean.
7. Cool in pan 10 minutes; remove to cool completely.

title: Chicken And Sauerkraut
description: Tender chicken pieces braised with tangy sauerkraut, onions, and garlic in a savory chicken stock with a kick of hot green chilies. This comforting dish beautifully combines rich flavors and textures.
instructions:
1. Remove skin from chicken.
2. Dry and sprinkle with salt and pepper; set aside.
3. Put sauerkraut in colander and soak, changing water several times.
4. Wring it out and let drain.
5. Brown chicken in oil, a few pieces at a time, and keep warm on a platter.
6. When chicken is done, add onions to fat and turn heat down.
7. Add garlic. When onions are clear, add sauerkraut and chicken stock.
8. Bring to a boil.
9. Add chilies.
10. Put in the chicken and let simmer about 1 hour.
11. (Gets better the longer it cooks.)

title: Frozen Vienna Tortes
description: Elegant frozen Vienna tortes with layers of chocolate, coffee, and vanilla ice cream in a graham cracker crust. Swirled with cinnamon, these desserts are quick to assemble and delightful to serve.
instructions:
1. Spread half of the quart of chocolate ice cream in each of the crusts; freeze.
2. Spread half of the coffee ice cream on top of the chocolate layer; freeze.
3. Soften the vanilla ice cream; whip cinnamon into it.
4. Divide and spread on each pie.
5. Freeze.

title: Unstuffed Cabbage(Tastes Just Like The Labor-Intensive Old Favorite)
description: A deconstructed version of classic stuffed cabbage, featuring seasoned ground beef, tender cabbage, and tomatoes, all simmered with a hint of brown sugar and vinegar. This easy, comforting dish delivers all the flavor without the fuss.
instructions:
1. Crumble beef into Dutch oven.
2. Add onions and cook over medium heat for 5 to 6 minutes, stirring until beef is lightly browned.

title: Dawn'S German Skillet Supper
description: A hearty German-inspired skillet supper featuring crisp bacon, tender cabbage, and flavorful Polish kielbasa, seasoned with garlic and caraway seeds. This one-pan meal is both comforting and efficient.
instructions:
1. In a large skillet, cook the bacon slices until crisp.
2. Remove the bacon from the skillet and drain on paper towel.
3. Reserve 2 tablespoons of the bacon drippings in the skillet.
4. To the skillet, add cabbage wedges, chopped onion, water, sugar, minced garlic and caraway seed.
5. Bring the liquid to boiling.
6. Reduce heat.
7. Cover and cook the cabbage-onion mixture over medium-low heat for 10 minutes.

title: Russian Borscht
description: A vibrant Russian borscht packed with potatoes, carrots, cabbage, onions, and beef, brightened with beets and tomatoes. Generously seasoned with dill weed and served with a dollop of sour cream, this soup is comforting and wholesome.
instructions:
1. Make vegetable soup with first five ingredients, including beets (if using raw ones).
2. Add canned beets (if using canned) when soup is almost done.
3. Add tomatoes and generous amount of dill weed (to your own taste of course).
4. Serve with dollop of sour cream or cottage cheese.

title: Easy Stuffed Cabbage Casserole
description: This easy stuffed cabbage casserole offers the comforting flavors of traditional halupkies without the fuss. Browned ground round and onion are mixed with rice, tomato soup, and seasonings, then baked to perfection in a single dish.
instructions:
1. Brown hamburger and onion in skillet, drain.
2. In bowl mix soup, water, rice, salt and pepper, and hamburger mixture.
3. Set aside.
4. Spray casserole dish with non-stick cooking spray on bottom and sides.
5. Evenly spread cabbage in casserole dish, pour soup mixture on top of cabbage, salt and pepper again.
6. Cover with casserole lid or aluminum foil.
7. Bake in 350° oven for 1 hour, remove cover or foil and bake 1/2 hour or until topping becomes slightly crisp.
8. DO NOT over bake.

title: Pinto Bean Dip
description: Savor this creamy pinto bean dip, enhanced with zesty hot pepper sauce and rich avocado. Perfect for entertaining, it's spread with a layer of mashed beans, topped with avocado, and garnished with fresh vegetables and cheese.
instructions:
1. In a bowl, mash beans with a fork.
2. Stir in 3/4 teaspoon salt, pepper and hot pepper sauce.
3. Spread onto a 12-inch serving plate. Mash avocados with lemon juice and remaining salt.
4. Spread over bean mixture.
5. Combine sour cream, mayonnaise and taco seasoning. Spread over avocado layer.
6. Sprinkle with onions, tomatoes, cheese and olives.
7. Serve with tortilla chips.

title: Chicken Fajitas(Yields 50 Servings, 2 Fajitas Per Serving)
description: Prepare a large batch of flavorful chicken fajitas, featuring tender chicken marinated in fajita sauce. Serve with warm tortillas and a medley of fresh toppings like lettuce, tomatoes, and sour cream.
instructions:
1. Cover diced chicken with sauce at least 1 day before serving. Store in walk-in.
2. Heat mixture in steamer 45 to 60 minutes. Place tortillas, covered, on warmer 1 to 2 hours before putting fajitas together.
3. Place 1 ounce of mixture in center of tortilla. Wrap tortilla around mixture.
4. Wrap 2 tortillas in foil for serving.
5. Store in warmer until serving time.
6. Have already cupped for serving one serving each of fajitas:
7. 1/2 ounce grated cheese, 1/4 cup diced tomato and 1/4 cup chopped lettuce.

title: Halupkies(Cabbage Rolls)
description: Delight in these classic Halupkies, or cabbage rolls, filled with a savory mixture of beef, rice, and onion. Simmered in a rich tomato sauce, they offer a taste of heartwarming home-style cooking.
instructions:
1. Mix thoroughly all ingredients; salt and pepper to taste.
2. Cut core out of head of cabbage.
3. Scald head of cabbage and remove leaves.
4. Cut thick seam off cabbage leaves.
5. Split large leaves in half.
6. Put 1 tablespoon of filling in each cabbage leaf.
7. Roll and place in pan with seam down.
8. Blend 1 large can of tomatoes; add 1 tablespoon sugar, 1 tablespoon wine vinegar and remaining half of puree or sauce.
9. Pour over cabbage rolls; add enough tomatoes or water to cover rolls.
10. Cut up unused cabbage and place in pan. Bake at 325°.

title: Spiral Sandwich Roll-Ups(Students' Favorite Demonstration Request)
description: These elegant spiral sandwich roll-ups feature fragrant Armenian cracker bread spread with a creamy herb cheese mixture, then layered with turkey, tomato, and romaine lettuce. They are perfect for a light lunch or appetizer.
instructions:
1. Dampen cracker bread on both sides by holding the bread briefly under gently running cold water.
2. Place the moistened bread between 2 damp, clean towels, making sure the sesame seed is facing down.
3. Let the bread stand at room temperature about 1 hour or until it has softened enough to roll.

title: Cream Of Beet Soup
description: Indulge in a velvety cream of beet soup, rich with butter, milk, and chicken broth, rounded out with a hint of onion and finished with a swirl of whipping cream. This comforting soup is a delight for the senses.
instructions:
1. Melt butter in saucepan.
2. Add flour; cook until mixture bubbles.
3. Stir in milk.
4. Cook, stirring constantly, until thickened.
5. Reduce to very low; cook 10 minutes.
6. Stir in chicken broth, beets, salt, pepper and onion juice; heat.
7. Serve hot.
8. Top each serving with whipped cream or sour cream.
9. Garnish with sprig of parsley.
10. Yields 6 servings or 1/2 cup each.

title: Chicken Or Beef Hekka
description: Experience the savory flavors of Chicken or Beef Hekka, where tender meat is simmered in a soy sauce base with ginger, garlic, and bamboo shoots. This hearty dish is designed to be served with warm rice.
instructions:
1. Cook chicken and beef in soy sauce until brown.
2. Add flour and water to cover meat.
3. Add ginger and garlic cloves to your taste. Before adding watercress, take garlic and ginger out.
4. Add sugar to your taste.
5. If you like bamboo shoots, add some sliced pieces. Serve over rice.

title: Pico De Gallo(Salsa With Cilantro)
description: Whip up a fresh Pico de Gallo, a vibrant salsa bursting with diced tomatoes, finely chopped onion, zesty lemon, and aromatic cilantro. This zesty condiment is perfect for accompanying tortilla chips.
instructions: 1. Dice tomatoes by hand. Put onion and jalapeno in food processor (I use a little chopper) and chop very fine. Chop cilantro in food processor fine. Mix all ingredients together. Keep refrigerated until use.

title: Fresh Mushroom Salad
description: Enjoy a refreshing fresh mushroom salad, combining sliced mushrooms, grated Mozzarella, and crisp green onions, all tossed in a simple oil and vinegar dressing with special seasonings. It’s a delightful light dish.
instructions:
1. Slice mushrooms into salad bowl.
2. Grate Mozzarella cheese and add to mushrooms.
3. Chop green onions and parsley; add to mushrooms.
4. Mix oil, vinegar and Cavender's seasoning separately. Do not pour dressing over mushroom salad until ready to serve. Parsley must be fresh.

title: Egg Noodle Stroganoff
description: Savor this rich and comforting egg noodle stroganoff, featuring ground beef cooked with bacon, onion, and a creamy sour cream sauce. It's a delightful and hearty meal perfect for any occasion when you're craving a classic.
instructions:
1. Wonderful, rich fish dish enjoyed by many.
2. Use fresh spinach for perfect bed.
3. Serve with French bread, and carrot/pea/scallion vegetables.

title: Cheesy Onion Roll-Ups
description: Prepare these irresistible cheesy onion roll-ups, featuring a creamy blend of sour cream, cream cheese, and Cheddar cheese with green onions and lime juice, all spread onto tortillas. These make a perfect appetizer or snack.
instructions:
1. Combine first 6 ingredients in a bowl; mix well.
2. Spread on one side of tortillas and roll up tightly.
3. Wrap and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
4. Slice into 1 inch pieces.
5. Serve with picante sauce. Makes 5 dozen.

title: Spice And Applesauce Cake
description: Bake a moist and flavorful spice and applesauce cake using a simple cake mix, enhanced with apple pie filling, and optional raisins and nuts. This delightful dessert is effortlessly delicious.
instructions:
1. Pour oil into a 13 x 9-inch pan.
2. Put cake mix, eggs and pie filling into pan (you may add 1 cup of raisins and nuts).
3. Stir with a spoon until blended.
4. Bake at 350° for 35 to 45 minutes.

title: Creamed Chicken In Biscuit Bowls
description: Indulge in creamy chicken served in warm biscuit bowls, featuring tender chicken, mushrooms, and vegetables simmered in a rich cream sauce. This comforting dish is perfect for a cozy meal.
instructions: 1. Melt butter in a large skillet. Add onion, celery and mushrooms. Saut 2 to 3 minutes until tender. Whisk in chicken soup and milk and cook over medium-low heat 3 to 4 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add cheese, stirring constantly, until melted. Stir in chicken, peas, carrots, pimiento, salt and pepper. Cook over low heat 8 to 10 minutes. Spoon into Biscuit Bowls.

title: Warm Mushroom Salad
description: Enjoy a warm mushroom salad, featuring earthy mushrooms and vibrant red pepper sautéed until tender, then tossed with fresh spinach leaves and a tangy vinaigrette. This quick salad is both healthy and delicious.
instructions:
1. Rinse mushrooms and dry.
2. Cut each in half.
3. In a large skillet, saute mushrooms and pepper in oil 6 minutes until vegetables are lightly browned and tender.
4. Meanwhile, remove and discard stems from spinach; wash and dry well.
5. Place in serving bowl; stir vinegar and mustard into skillet.
6. Bring mixture to a boil.
7. Toss with spinach.
8. Makes 4 servings.

title: Hot, Spiced Russian Tea
description: Sip on a comforting cup of hot, spiced Russian tea, infused with cinnamon sticks, fruit juices, and sugar. This fragrant beverage is perfect for warming up on a chilly day.
instructions:
1. Place water, cinnamon and cloves in gallon pot and simmer for 20 minutes.
2. Add tea bags and steep for 10 minutes.
3. Remove tea bags and add sugar and fruit juices.
4. Serve hot.

title: Greek Mushroom Salad
description: Savor this fresh Greek mushroom salad, combining sliced mushrooms, Swiss cheese, and crisp green onions, all lightly dressed in oil, wine vinegar, and seasoning. It's a quick and flavorful side dish.
instructions: 1. Mix first 3 items and, before serving, toss with last 4 items.

title: Austrian Sausage And Sauerkraut Soup
description: Warm up with a hearty Austrian sausage and sauerkraut soup, blending rich chicken broth, savory sausage, and tangy sauerkraut with vegetables. This comforting soup is perfect for a slow-cooked meal.
instructions:
1. Combine all ingredients in a 4-quart pot.
2. Cover and cook on low heat for 5 hours or until vegetables are tender.
3. If necessary, skim off fat before serving.
4. May cook in crock-pot 10 to 12 hours.
5. Excellent hearty soup for cold days.

title: Bahamian Salad
description: Prepare a simple Bahamian salad, starring crisp cabbage dressed in a creamy mayonnaise and lime juice mixture. This light and refreshing side dish is perfect for any meal.
instructions:
1. Basic ingredient is cabbage or use whatever you have in your dehydrator, such as, onion, pepper, celery, carrot, radish, tomato, etc.
2. Add salt and pepper.
3. Secret is mayonnaise mixed with lime (fresh) juice.
4. Use your own taste buds!

title: Shchi(Russian Cabbage Soup)
description: Savor the rich and tangy flavors of Shchi, a classic Russian cabbage soup, simmered with beef broth, fresh cabbage, sauerkraut, and tomato. Garnished with sour cream and dill, it's a deeply comforting and traditional dish.
instructions:
1. Melt butter in a large pot.
2. Add the onion, leek and carrot. Saute until just beginning to get soft.
3. Pour in the beef stock and bring to boil.
4. Stir in the cabbage, sauerkraut and the tomato.
5. Cover and simmer for about 50 minutes or until cabbage is tender.
6. Check for seasoning.
7. Serve hot with a dollop of sour cream and sprinkling of dill (fresh is best).
8. Serves 4 to 6. Best if made day before and refrigerated.

title: Deep Skillet Beef Stroganoff
description: Indulge in a rich Deep Skillet Beef Stroganoff, featuring tender beef stew meat, mushrooms, and onions in a creamy sauce, served over egg noodles. This hearty and flavorful dish is a classic comfort food.
instructions:
1. Slice stew meat 1/4 inch thick or in small strips 1/4 inch wide.
2. Brown quickly on medium-high heat in 1/4 cup oleo in skillet.
3. Push meat to one side.
4. Add mushrooms and chopped onion. Cook on medium heat until tender.
5. Add 1 teaspoon seasoning. Blend in meat.

title: Connie'S Russian Tea
description: This quick and easy "Russian" tea beverage combines fruit juices, sugar, and hot water, perfect for serving warm on a chilly day. It's a simple, comforting drink to prepare for an afternoon refreshment or a cozy gathering.
instructions:
1. Mix first four ingredients and add the sugar and boiling water.
2. Serve hot.

title: Beet Borscht *
description: Hearty and nutritious, this classic beet borscht features sautéed vegetables, root vegetables, and cabbage simmered in stock, creating a rich and flavorful soup. It's a comforting meal, often served with a dollop of sour cream.
instructions:
1. Saute onion and celery in 1 tablespoon butter.
2. Place sauteed onion and celery in large pot with 1 grated beet, garlic, tomatoes with juice, potatoes, stock, water, pepper, parsley and salt. Bring to boil; lower heat immediately and simmer, uncovered, 45 minutes.
3. Strain out vegetables; puree in food processor and return to soup.
4. Saute grated beets and carrots in 1 tablespoon butter; add to soup and simmer 15 minutes.
5. Do not boil, as this will turn red beet color brown.
6. Saute cabbage in 1 tablespoon or more butter for 5 minutes; add to soup and simmer 15 minutes more. Add lemon juice.
7. Taste for salt and pepper.
8. Serve hot with sour cream.

title: Steak Au Poivre
description: A sophisticated French classic, Steak Au Poivre features pan-seared steak crusted with crushed peppercorns and fennel, finished with a rich Cognac cream sauce. This elegant dish is perfect for a special occasion.
instructions:
1. In a heavy-duty sealable plastic bag or between 2 sheets of wax paper, crush peppercorns and fennel seed coarse with bottom of a heavy skillet.
2. Pat steaks dry and coat both sides with peppercorn mixture.

title: Potato Pancake
description: Crispy and golden, these potato pancakes are made by pressing shredded potatoes into an even layer and cooking until tender and browned. Seasoned simply with salt and pepper, they make a delightful side dish or light meal.
instructions:
1. Put 1 tablespoon oil and 1 tablespoon butter in a large skillet.
2. Set over medium heat until butter melts.
3. Put potatoes in the skillet.
4. Press them down into an even compact layer. Cook, covered, for 5 minutes.
5. Uncover, turn heat to low and cook for 15 minutes more.
6. Slide the pancake onto a plate.
7. Put 1 tablespoon oil and 1 tablespoon butter into the skillet.
8. When the butter melts, put the pancake back into the skillet with the browned side up.
9. Press down to compact the potatoes and cook, uncovered, for 15 minutes more. Cut into 4 wedges and serve warm.

title: Herbed Mushroom Salad
description: A refreshing mushroom salad featuring fresh mushrooms marinated in a zesty dressing of lemon juice, herbs, and sauces, then served over shredded lettuce. This light and flavorful salad is perfect as an appetizer or side.
instructions:
1. Place mushrooms in small bowl.
2. Combine all ingredients except shredded lettuce and pour over mushrooms.
3. Marinate a few hours or overnight.
4. Arrange shredded lettuce in salad bowls; top with mushrooms.

title: Sausage Party Snacks
description: These simple and savory sausage party snacks combine browned sausage, Velveeta cheese, onion, and spices, served on party rye bread. They are perfect for entertaining and can be prepared in advance.
instructions:
1. Scramble sausage; drain and add onion, oregano and catsup. Add cheese until melted.
2. Spoon onto bread; can be frozen until needed.
3. Bake at 325° for 15 to 20 minutes.
4. Serves 12 to 20.

title: Country Style Chicken Casserole
description: A creamy and comforting Country Style Chicken Casserole featuring tender chicken, stroganoff egg noodles, and pimentos in a rich, cheesy sauce. This hearty dish is perfect for a satisfying family dinner.
instructions:
1. Cook noodles; drain.
2. In a large skillet, melt butter, then blend in flour over medium heat.
3. Gradually add broth, milk, pimentos, salt and pepper.
4. Bring to boiling point, then simmer, stirring constantly, until sauce is thickened, about 5 minutes. Grease a 2 1/2-quart casserole.
5. Combine noodles, chicken and sauce.
6. Top with cheese.
7. Bake for 20 minutes at 350°.

title: Ukrainian Supper(Borscht; Very Good)
description: A truly authentic Ukrainian Borscht, this recipe includes soup meat, bones, and knuckles simmered with a medley of vegetables like onion, carrot, potatoes, and cabbage, finished with tomato puree and beans. This rich, slow-cooked soup is deeply flavorful and a true culinary experience.
instructions:
1. In a heavy pot put 10 cups cold water, soup meat, soup bones and pig knuckles.
2. Bring to a slow boil.
3. Add leek or onion, carrot and parsley.
4. Reduce heat and let simmer uncovered 2 1/2 to 3 hours.
5. Add more liquid when needed.
6. Cool, then refrigerate several hours or overnight.
7. Discard surface fat.
8. Strain stock; set aside meat and bones.
9. Return liquid to pot, adding water if necessary to make 8 cups.
10. Bring to a boil.
11. Add potatoes, cabbage, tomato puree and garlic.
12. Simmer covered 30 minutes.
13. Add beets. Bring again to a boil.

title: Little Party Pizza
description: These easy-to-make Little Party Pizzas feature a savory topping of browned ground beef, sausage, and melted Velveeta cheese, seasoned with ketchup and oregano, spread over rye bread slices. Perfect for quick party appetizers, they can be frozen for later use.
instructions: 1. Brown sausage and ground beef. Drain grease. Add cheese and melt. Add slices and let cool. Spread on small slices of rye bread. Good to freeze for later use.

title: Bavarian Goulash
description: This hearty Bavarian Goulash features beef braised with onions, tomatoes, paprika, and bay leaf in a rich meat stock, forming a tender and flavorful stew. It's a comforting and robust main course, perfect for a satisfying meal.
instructions: 1. Heat oil. Add beef and onion, saute until meat is browned. Add tomatoes. Season to taste with salt, pepper, and paprika. Add bay leaf. Pour in stock, cover and simmer for 1 1/2 hours or until beef is tender.

title: Dip For Round Rye Bread
description: A quick and flavorful dip made with sour cream, mayonnaise, fresh herbs like parsley and dill, and onion, perfect for pairing with round rye bread. This easy recipe is ideal for entertaining or a casual snack.
instructions:
1. Mix together all ingredients.
2. Refrigerate ingredients.
3. Do not put ingredients in bread until ready to serve.

title: Creamy Pea Salad(Makes 24 Servings)
description: A creamy and refreshing pea salad combining frozen green peas, onion, celery, and bacon in a rich sour cream dressing, garnished with cashews. Chilled for hours, it’s a delightful side dish for any gathering.
instructions:
1. Combine first seven ingredients, stirring gently.
2. Chill 3 to 4 hours.
3. Stir in bacon and cashews.
4. Serve on lettuce leaves.

title: Speedy Beef Stroganoff
description: This Speedy Beef Stroganoff features tender beef browned with onion and garlic, then simmered in a creamy sauce made with cream of mushroom soup, sour cream, and ketchup. Served over noodles, it's a quick and satisfying classic.
instructions:
1. In skillet or chafing dish, brown meat in hot oil; add onion and garlic.
2. Cook until onion is crisp-tender.
3. Blend soup, sour cream, mushrooms, ketchup and Worcestershire sauce.
4. Pour over meat; cook and heat slowly.
5. Cook and drain noodles.
6. Toss with butter and poppy seed.
7. Serve Stroganoff over hot noodles.
8. Serves 4.

title: Linda Mini Pizza
description: These quick and easy Linda Mini Pizzas are made with a savory topping of seasoned ground chuck and sausage, melted Velveeta cheese, and fresh vegetables, spread over party rye bread slices. Baked until golden, they're perfect for appetizers.
instructions:
1. Brown chuck, sausage, onion and pepper; drain well.
2. Add oregano and anis.
3. Cut Velveeta into chunks.
4. Add and stir until melted.
5. Spread on bread.
6. Bake in 350° oven 10 minutes.

title: Patty'S Mushroom Salad
description: A vibrant and simple mushroom salad featuring fresh button mushrooms, cherry tomatoes, and a mix of green and black olives, with a hint of anchovy. This refreshing salad is perfect as an appetizer or light side.
instructions: 1. Mix all salad ingredients together except anchovies.

title: Russian Creme(The Peachtree Tea Room)
description: A creamy and delicate Russian Creme dessert, featuring gelatin, sugar, half-and-half, and vanilla, chilled until set. This elegant sweet treat offers a subtle flavor and smooth texture, perfect for a refined indulgence.
instructions:
1. In a small saucepan, add sugar to half and half and heat until lukewarm.
2. Stir constantly with a whisk until sugar dissolves. Soften gelatin in water and add all to warm half and half mixture. Continue to stir over heat until gelatin is dissolved.
3. Remove from heat and refrigerate until the mixture begins to thicken.

title: Crock-Pot Stuffed Cabbages
description: Hearty Crock-Pot Stuffed Cabbages, featuring a savory mixture of ground chuck, rice, onions, and peppers, wrapped in tender cabbage leaves and slow-cooked in tomato juice. This comforting dish is perfect for a satisfying family meal.
instructions:
1. Mix into meat the rice, onions, peppers, salt and garlic powder.
2. Precook cabbage and cut around core of cabbage until leaves fall off.
3. Form meat in shape of balls the size of cabbage leaves. Fold in and roll.
4. Put in 6 1/2-quart crock-pot on high. Cover with some tomato juice.
5. When juice cooks down, drain grease and add rest of juice.
6. Cook 5 to 6 hours until done.

title: Sausage-Tortellini Soup
description: A robust Sausage-Tortellini Soup featuring Italian sausage, garlic, and vegetables simmered in a rich broth with tomatoes, red wine, and tortellini. This hearty and flavorful soup is a perfect comforting meal.
instructions:
1. Discard sausage casings.
2. Cook sausage, onion, and garlic in a Dutch oven over medium-high heat, stirring until sausage crumbles and is no longer pink; drain.
3. Return mixture to pan.
4. Stir in broth and next 6 ingredients; bring to a boil.
5. Reduce heat; simmer 30 minutes.
6. Skim off fat.
7. Stir in zucchini and tortellini; simmer 10 minutes.
8. Sprinkle each serving with cheese.
9. Yields 10 cups.

title: No-Fat Mock Russian Dressing
description: A light and flavorful No-Fat Mock Russian Dressing made with plain yogurt, ketchup, honey, and a hint of pepper, perfect for salads. This healthy dressing is quick to prepare and can be stored in the refrigerator.
instructions:
1. Combine all ingredients in a wide mouth jar or small bowl. Mix vigorously with a fork or wire whisk until yogurt, ketchup and honey are well blended.
2. Chill.
3. Keep covered in refrigerator for storing.

title: Marinated Potato And Mushroom Salad
description: A vibrant Marinated Potato and Mushroom Salad, featuring tender potatoes and fresh mushrooms bathed in a zesty dressing of oil, lemon juice, mustard, and bouillon. This refreshing salad is perfect for picnics or as a flavorful side dish.
instructions:
1. In a large, shallow dish, combine oil, lemon juice, mustard, sugar and bouillon; mix well.
2. Add potatoes, mushrooms, celery and onions.
3. Cover; marinate in refrigerator 3 hours or overnight, stirring occasionally.
4. Makes about 5 cups.

title: Rye Rounds
description: A delicious rye rounds recipe.
instructions:
1. Mix first 5 ingredients.
2. Spread on party rye bread.
3. Top with 1 slice onion.
4. Bake at 350° for 15 minutes.
5. Makes approximately 16 rounds.

title: Old Fashion Sauerkraut Soup
description: A delicious old fashion sauerkraut soup recipe.
instructions:
1. Cook some pork bones and reserve that meat.
2. Cook potatoes and add meat, sauerkraut and salt.
3. Cook for about 10 minutes and simmer for 2 hours.

title: Polish Paluszki
description: A delicious polish paluszki recipe.
instructions:
1. Using first 4 ingredients, make dough as for pie crust.
2. Heat until lukewarm milk, dissolve 2 yeast cakes in lukewarm milk.
3. Add eggs to milk and yeast mixture.
4. Add dry ingredients to milk mixture and mix with hands only until it holds together.
5. Shape into roll, cover with waxed paper and chill 6 hours or overnight. Roll out on granulated sugar by pieces (thin).
6. Cut in squares, spread apricot filling (solo) in middle and flip 2 corners over it.
7. Bake 20 to 25 minutes at 375° on cookie sheet.

title: Delicious Hungarian Goulash Soup
description: A delicious delicious hungarian goulash soup recipe.
instructions:
1. Saute vegetables.
2. Add meat and all other ingredients, except potatoes. Simmer until meat is tender. Add potatoes.
3. Cook 45 minutes longer.
4. Garnish with sour cream and green onions.

title: Easy Pizza Appetizers
description: A delicious easy pizza appetizers recipe.
instructions:
1. Brown ground beef and sausage in skillet, stirring until crumbly.
2. Drain, then add next 4 ingredients, stirring until cheese is melted.
3. Spread on bread.
4. Place on baking sheet.
5. Bake at 400° for 12 minutes or until bubbly.

description: A delicious hot spice tea (or russian tea) recipe.
instructions:

4. bags, cloves and cinnamon sticks; bring to

6. Remove tea bags and spices. Add

9. Delicious and cozy on a cold evening!

title: Dill Pickle Soup
description: A delicious dill pickle soup recipe.
instructions:
1. Saute grated pickles in butter and flour until completely wilted.
2. Add the stock and simmer slowly for one-half hour.
3. In small bowl, stir together sour cream and 1 cup of broth.
4. Stir in to soup and simmer.
5. Serve hot.
6. However, if soup is not sour enough, add pickle juice to taste.
7. Serves 6.

title: Latkes(Potato Pancakes)
description: A delicious latkes(potato pancakes) recipe.
instructions:
1. Peel and grate potatoes, onion and apple on coarse grater. Beat eggs lightly; add grated potatoes, onion and apple and blend well.
2. Add salt, flour and baking powder and mix thoroughly.
3. Pour 1-inch oil into skillet and heat.
4. Drop mix by tablespoons into hot oil.
5. Fry and brown both sides.
6. Serve hot.
7. Drain off any liquid before frying each cake.
8. Serve with sour cream or applesauce.
9. Makes 20.

title: Pierogi(Filled Dumplings)
description: A delicious pierogi(filled dumplings) recipe.
instructions:
1. Mound flour on kneading board and make hole in center.
2. Drop eggs into hole and cut into flour with knife.
3. Add salt and water and knead until firm.
4. Let rest for 10 minutes, covered with a warm bowl.
5. Divide dough into halves and roll thin.
6. Cut circles with large biscuit cutter.
7. Place a small spoonful of filling a little to one side on each round of dough.
8. Moisten edge with water, fold over and press edges together firmly.
9. Be sure they are well sealed to prevent the filling from running out.
10. Drop pierogi into salted boiling water.
11. Cook gently for 3 to 5 minutes.
12. Lift out carefully with perforated spoon.

title: Szekely Gulyas
description: A delicious szekely gulyas recipe.
instructions:
1. Rehydrate minced onion in 1/3 cup water for 10 minutes; set aside.
2. In a large heavy saucepot, heat bacon fat.
3. Add pork; brown on all sides, a few pieces at a time; remove and set aside. To drippings left in saucepot, add sauerkraut and rehydrated onion; saute until golden, about 5 minutes.
4. Return pork to saucepot.
5. Stir in 1 cup water, wine, paprika, caraway, salt, tarragon, black pepper and bouillon cube.
6. Bring to the boiling point.
7. Reduce heat and simmer, covered, until pork is tender, about 1 1/2 hours.
8. Stir in sour cream.
9. Heat until hot.
10. Sprinkle with parsley flakes.
11. Serve with cooked noodles, if desired. Yields 6 to 8 portions.

title: Blintzes Or Crepes (Pancakes)
description: A delicious blintzes or crepes (pancakes) recipe.
instructions:
1. Mix together all ingredients until smooth.
2. Grease an 8-inch skillet lightly with 1 teaspoon vegetable shortening, heat, pour enough batter to coat bottom.
3. Tilt skillet to cover evenly and cook until delicate brown.
4. Loosen and remove with spatula. Continue until all batter is used up.

title: Polish Potato Casserole
description: A delicious polish potato casserole recipe.
instructions:
1. In medium saucepan, melt the butter or margarine; stir in flour.
2. Cook, stirring, for 1 minute, then gradually add the milk, stirring constantly to eliminate lumps.
3. Cook the mixture over moderately low heat, stirring it, until the sauce begins to thicken.
4. Stir in the salt and pepper.
5. Set the sauce aside.

title: Sausage Pizza Rounds
description: A delicious sausage pizza rounds recipe.
instructions:
1. Brown sausage meat and hamburg together. Drain well.
2. Cut cheese into cubes and mix into hot meat mixture until melted.
3. Add seasonings and mix well.
4. Mound mixture on party rye bread (may be frozen at this point).
5. Broil until bubbly and browned.

title: Broccoli, Bacon And Mushroom Salad
description: A delicious broccoli, bacon and mushroom salad recipe.
instructions:
1. Steam broccoli until tender but still crunchy; cool.
2. Cook bacon until crisp.
3. Cool on paper towels and then crumble. Combine broccoli, bacon, and mushrooms in a large bowl.
4. In a small bowl combine remaining ingredients to make dressing.
5. Pour over the broccoli mixture.
6. Toss to combine. Chill for at least 30 minutes before serving.

title: One Dish Cabbage Rolls
description: A delicious one dish cabbage rolls recipe.
instructions:
1. Cut up cabbage and place in buttered 2-quart casserole dish. Brown meat and onions, add seasonings and salt to taste.
2. Simmer and drain.
3. Add rice, soup and water, tomato sauce, sugar and vinegar.
4. Simmer 5 minutes longer.
5. Place over cabbage (do not stir), cover and bake at 350° for 2 hours.

title: Sarama(Yield: 8 Servings)
description: A delicious sarama(yield: 8 servings) recipe.
instructions:
1. 1. Par broil cabbage.
2. Remove from pan and let cook some 2 cups of water in pan.

title: Borsch(May Prepare Ahead.  Serves 6 To 8.)
description: A delicious borsch(may prepare ahead.  serves 6 to 8.) recipe.
instructions:
1. Finely chop drained beets, but reserve the juice.
2. Combine with beet juice, bouillon, water and minced onion.
3. Heat thoroughly but do not boil.
4. Add salt and lemon juice.
5. Serve hot or cold topped with sour cream.

title: Beef Stew In Crock-Pot
description: A delicious beef stew in crock-pot recipe.
instructions: 1. Layer all ingredients except peas in crock-pot and cook about 5 or 6 hours. Add frozen peas the last 30 minutes.

title: Easy Quick Chili
description: A delicious easy quick chili recipe.
instructions:
1. Brown ground beef.
2. Add remaining ingredients.
3. Bring to a boil.
4. Reduce heat.
5. Simmer 15 minutes.
6. Serves 3 to 4.

title: Cream Cheese And Mushroom Enchiladas
description: Flavorful enchiladas filled with a creamy mushroom mixture, baked to perfection. A delightful and satisfying vegetarian dish with a hint of spice.
instructions:
1. Cook onion and garlic in oil until tender.
2. Stir in undrained tomatoes, honey, 1 teaspoon chili powder and remaining spices. Bring mixture to a boil; reduce heat and simmer, uncovered, about 30 minutes, until thick, stirring occasionally.
3. Set aside.

title: Taco Salad - Low-Fat
description: A lightened-up taco salad featuring lean ground beef, beans, corn, and fresh vegetables, served in crispy tortilla cups. Perfect for a healthy and satisfying meal.
instructions:
1. Prepare tortilla cups; set aside.
2. Prepare tomatillo guacamole; cover and chill.
3. In a medium skillet cook beef and garlic until beef is brown.
4. Drain off fat.
5. Stir in kidney beans, corn, taco sauce and chili powder.
6. Bring to boiling; reduce heat. Cover and simmer for 10 minutes.

title: Kielbasa Bean Soup
description: A hearty and warming soup combining tender vegetables with flavorful kielbasa and comforting bean with bacon soup. Quick to prepare and perfect for a chilly day.
instructions:
1. In Dutch oven, bring veggies and water to boil.
2. Reduce heat and simmer 10 minutes or until tender.
3. Add meat and bean soup. Heat thoroughly.

title: Kolbasi And Sauerkraut
description: A traditional Polish dish featuring kolbasi (sausage) baked alongside flavorful sauerkraut, enhanced with onions and salt pork. A simple yet robust comfort food.
instructions:
1. Bake kolbasi in 325° oven for 1 hour in pan with water to cover.
2. Thoroughly rinse and drain sauerkraut.
3. Place in part with 2 whole onions and salt pork; add salt and pepper to taste.
4. Add water to cover; simmer 45 minutes.
5. When kolbasi is cooked, drain and cut into 4-inch pieces.
6. Add to sauerkraut and continue cooking for 30 minutes.
7. Brown flour in bacon fat.
8. When golden brown, add to kolbasi and sauerkraut and mix thoroughly.

title: Creamy Beef Stroganoff
description: Classic beef stroganoff with tender sirloin steak, earthy mushrooms, and a rich, creamy sauce finished with sour cream. Served over noodles for a comforting meal.
instructions:
1. Partially freeze steak; slice diagonally across grain into 1/4-inch strips.
2. Brown meat in hot oil in a large skillet; remove meat from skillet, reserving pan drippings.
3. Saute mushrooms, onion and garlic in reserved pan drippings until tender.
4. Return steak to skillet; add sherry and next 6 ingredients.
5. Cook over medium-low heat 10 to 12 minutes or until most of liquid evaporates.
6. Remove from heat; add cream cheese, stirring until cheese melts.
7. Stir in sour cream; return to heat.
8. Cook just until hot.
9. (Do not boil.) Serve stroganoff over noodles or rice.
10. Yield:
11. 4 to 6 servings.

title: Bavarian Casserole
description: A savory casserole layering crispy bacon, tangy sauerkraut, succulent pork chops, sliced potatoes, and onions. A comforting and hearty one-dish meal.
instructions:
1. Line bottom of baking dish with bacon.
2. Drain sauerkraut and put over bacon.
3. Lay pork chops over kraut.
4. Peel and slice potatoes over pork chops.
5. Slice onions and separate into rings. Put on potatoes.
6. Pour can of tomatoes on top of casserole (do not drain).
7. Salt and pepper to taste.
8. Bake at least 2 hours at 350°. (For first hour, you may want to cover with foil.)

title: Polish Pie Crust
description: A simple and versatile Polish pie crust recipe, perfect for a variety of sweet or savory fillings. Easy to mix and prepare for your baking needs.
instructions:
1. Mix dry ingredients together and make a hole in the middle of the mixture.
2. Mix egg, vinegar and water.
3. Put this mixture in the hole and mix together for pie crust.

title: Clam Mushroom Salad
description: A refreshing salad combining crisp lettuce, fresh mushrooms, and red onion, dressed with a savory clam juice vinaigrette. A unique and light appetizer or side dish.
instructions:
1. Core, rinse and drain lettuce.
2. Chill in disposable plastic bag or plastic crisper.
3. Clean mushrooms with damp paper towels; trim stems.
4. Slice lengthwise.
5. Combine clam juice, oil, vinegar, salt, tarragon and Tabasco in large jar or juice container; cover and shake well.
6. Add mushrooms and onion.
7. Shake to coat.
8. Chill.
9. Tear enough lettuce to make 5 cups.
10. Chill remainder for use another time.
11. Cut tomato into wedges.
12. Combine with lettuce and mushroom mixture in large salad bowl; toss lightly.
13. Add fresh clams to taste.
14. Makes 5 to 6 servings.

title: Polish Chop Suey
description: A quick and easy Polish-inspired dish featuring browned ground beef and onion mixed with cooked cabbage and egg noodles, lightly seasoned with soy sauce.
instructions:
1. Brown and drain beef and onion.
2. Add to cooked cabbage and cooked noodles.

title: Raspberry Shortbread
description: Delicious raspberry shortbread bars with a crumbly cake mix base, a layer of sweet raspberry preserves, and a delicate almond glaze. A perfect treat for any occasion.
instructions:
1. Combine cake mix, nuts, butter and egg.
2. Mix until crumbly. Press into bottom of greased 9 x 13-inch pan.
3. Spread with preserves.
4. Bake at 350°
5. for 25 minutes.
6. Combine sugar, water and almond extract and mix until smooth.
7. Drizzle over warm shortbread.
8. Cool and cut into bars.

title: (Dry) Russian Tea
description: A warming dry mix for instant Russian tea, combining instant tea, Tang, lemonade mix, and aromatic spices. Perfect for a quick and comforting hot beverage.
instructions:
1. Mix
2. all
3. together.
4. Store
5. in a tight jar or container. Use 2 teaspoons into 1 cup boiling water.
6. Stir and enjoy.

title: Savory Chicken Soup
description: A flavorful and comforting chicken soup brimming with tender chicken, rice, and vegetables, seasoned with aromatic herbs and a hint of cayenne pepper.
instructions:
1. In a stock pot, combine broth, seasonings, vegetables, chicken and rice.
2. Bring to a boil; reduce heat and simmer about 30 minutes or until vegetables and rice are tender.
3. Add a little cold water to cornstarch and stir into soup to thicken.
4. Simmer 5 minutes.
5. Taste for seasoning and add a dash of cayenne pepper, if desired.

title: Potato Latkes *
description: Classic potato latkes, crispy and golden-brown, made from grated potatoes and onions, perfect for a savory side dish. Best served with sour cream or applesauce.
instructions:
1. Beat eggs in large bowl.
2. Add matzo meal, salt and pepper. Grate onions and potatoes (after peeling) and place in colander. Run boiling water through to help retard color change.
3. Mix onions and potatoes in bowl with egg mixture.
4. Fry in oil until brown on both sides.
5. Drain on paper towels.
6. Makes about 14 medium size latkes.

title: Dilled Ricotta Torte
description: A savory torte featuring a whole wheat and almond crust filled with a creamy ricotta and cream cheese mixture, flavored with fresh dill and lemon. An elegant appetizer.
instructions:
1. In a bowl, combine bread, almonds and butter.
2. Press mixture onto the bottom and one-inch up the sides of a well buttered 9-inch spring-form pan.

title: Party Rye Sandwiches
description: Quick and easy party appetizers made with Swiss cheese, bacon, olives, and onion, spread on party rye bread and broiled until golden. Perfect for entertaining.
instructions: 1. Combine cheese, bacon, stuffed olives, onion and mayonnaise. Spread 1 tablespoon on each rye slice. Top with black olive slices. Broil 2 minutes.

title: Crispy Potato Pancake
description: Crispy and delicious potato pancakes, perfectly seasoned and fried until golden brown. A versatile side dish, wonderful with a dollop of sour cream and fresh parsley.
instructions:
1. To shred potatoes:
2. Peel and rinse.
3. Run potatoes lengthwise through the coarse blades of a vegetable shredder.
4. Immerse shredded potato in cold water to prevent darkening until ready to cook.
5. Drain well.

title: Baked Beans With Kielbasa
description: Hearty baked beans made with a combination of beans, brown sugar, ketchup, and mustard, topped with flavorful kielbasa. A comforting and satisfying meal.
instructions:
1. Mix together all ingredients, except kielbasa.
2. Bake at 300° for 1 hour.
3. Arrange kielbasa over the top and bake for 10 more minutes.

title: *Stuffed Cabbage
description: Traditional stuffed cabbage rolls, filled with a savory mixture of meat, rice, and herbs, simmered in a rich tomato sauce and sauerkraut. A hearty, classic comfort food.
instructions:
1. In a large pot of boiling water, add cored cabbage and simmer. Remove leaves of cabbage as they soften.
2. Allow to cool and carefully remove large rib of each leaf.
3. (Or cut out core of cabbage, wrap tightly in plastic wrap and microwave on High for 15 to 20 minutes.
4. Cabbage will turn bright green and yield to pressure.
5. Let stand 5 minutes.
6. Unwrap and rinse under cold running water.)

title: Red Russian Salad Dressing
description: A quick and easy dressing with a distinct Russian flavor, combining oil, sugar, catsup, and a hint of onion. Perfect for enhancing your favorite salads.
instructions: 1. Combine all ingredients on low speed in food processor; beat 20 minutes.

title: Hot Cheese Hors D'Oeuvres
description: Savory hot cheese hors d'oeuvres made with sharp cheese, bacon, onion, and garlic spread on party rye bread and broiled until bubbly. Easily prepared ahead for entertaining.
instructions:
1. Grind together cheese, bacon, onion and garlic.
2. Combine mayonnaise and mustard.
3. Mix well.
4. Spread on bread.
5. Broil for 6 to 7 minutes about 5 inches from broiler heat.
6. These can be frozen.
7. When ready to use, take out of freezer about half hour before needed and broil 6 to 7 minutes or until done.

title: Vinegret
description: A vibrant and refreshing Russian salad, Vinegret combines boiled root vegetables like beets, potatoes, and carrots with pickles and green peas. Dressed simply with oil or mayonnaise, it's a staple for any meal.
instructions: 1. Boil all vegetables except green peas. Let them cool down and then peel. Chop beets, potatoes, cucumbers and carrots. Add green peas, if you want. Season with vegetable oil or mayonnaise. Add salt to taste.

title: Doris' Potato Pancakes
description: Crispy and savory potato pancakes, perfect as a side dish or a light meal. The potatoes are grated and mixed with eggs, flour, and seasoning, then fried until golden brown.
instructions:
1. Peel large potatoes.
2. Cover with cold water.
3. Soak several hours before using.
4. Drain and grate.
5. Stir in sieve to release most of water.
6. Mix in remaining ingredients.
7. Drop by teaspoonful into hot, well greased skillet; brown on both sides.
8. Good served with applesauce.

title: Beef Stroganoff(Serves 4 To 6)
description: Classic Beef Stroganoff features tender beef strips simmered in a rich, creamy mushroom sauce. It's an indulgent Russian dish, often served over noodles or rice.
instructions:
1. Combine flour, salt and pepper; trim fat from meat.
2. Rub both sides with garlic.
3. Pound flour into meat.
4. Cut meat in 1 1/2 x 1-inch strips.
5. In hot butter, in Dutch oven, brown meat, turning often.
6. Add onion, saute until golden brown.
7. Add water, stir to dissolve brown bits in bottom of pan.
8. Add soup, mushrooms.
9. Cook uncovered over low heat, stirring occasionally until mixture is thick and meat is tender.
10. Just before serving, stir in sour cream. Heat but do not boil.
11. Serve over rice or noodles.

title: Country Borsch
description: Hearty and flavorful, this Country Borsch is a rich soup made with tender beef, beets, cabbage, and other vegetables. It's a comforting and robust take on the traditional Eastern European staple.
instructions:
1. In large covered saucepan, simmer meat in broth and water for 45 minutes or until tender.
2. Stir in salt, onion, beets, potato, carrot and celery; cover and simmer 15 minutes more or until vegetables are tender.
3. Stir in cabbage; cook until just tender. Combine flour and lemon juice; slowly add to soup.
4. Stir in remaining ingredients, except sour cream and croutons.
5. Cook, stirring occasionally until mixture thickens.
6. Serve hot, garnished with sour cream and croutons.

title: Strawberry Angel Food Dessert
description: A delightful layered dessert featuring fluffy angel food cake, sweet strawberries, and light whipped cream. This easy-to-make treat is perfect for special occasions or a refreshing indulgence.
instructions:
1. Dissolve the jello in 1 1/2 cups boiling water. Stir in thawed strawberries, sugar and salt. Cool until it begins to thicken. Fold in whipped cream. Cover bottom of a 9-inch pan with 1/2 of the torn pieces of cake.
2. Pour over half of the strawberry cream mixture. Add another layer of remaining cake pieces and pour remaining strawberry cream mixture on top. Refrigerate at least 4 hours.

title: Polish Cabbage Rolls("Gobabki")
description: Traditional Polish Cabbage Rolls, known as 'Gołąbki', are made with tender cabbage leaves filled with a savory mixture of meat and rice. These rolls are then slow-cooked in a rich tomato sauce until perfect.
instructions:
1. Parboil cabbage until leaves may be separated (about 30 minutes); cool enough to handle.
2. Separate leaves and cut off thick membrane.

title: German Skillet
description: This easy German Skillet combines browned ground beef with tangy sauerkraut, vegetables, and tomato juice. It's baked until tender, often topped with melted cheese for an extra layer of flavor.
instructions:
1. Brown meat and drain.
2. Rinse sauerkraut and drain.
3. Put all together and mix well.
4. Cover and bake at 350° for 1 hour and 15 minutes.
5. The last 15 minutes, put slices of cheese on top and return to oven (uncovered).

title: String Bean And Mushroom Salad
description: A fresh and vibrant salad featuring crisp string beans and earthy mushrooms, tossed in a zesty vinaigrette. This light and healthy dish is perfect as a side or starter.
instructions:
1. In a large saucepan bring water to a boil. Add beans and salt to taste.
2. Cook about 5 minutes or until tender.
3. Drain and cool. Wash and dry mushrooms; slice thinly.
4. Prepare sauce by whisking together the mustard, vinegar, cumin, Tabasco, salt and pepper. Slowly add oil while whisking vigorously.
5. In a salad bowl; place beans, mushrooms, onions and chives.
6. Pour sauce over the vegetables and toss gently.

title: Cold Reuben Spread
description: This quick and easy Cold Reuben Spread combines cream cheese, Swiss cheese, corned beef, and sauerkraut with zesty cocktail sauce. Perfect for serving with crackers or bread as an appetizer.
instructions:
1. Mix cream cheese and cocktail sauce in a medium bowl.
2. Add remaining ingredients and blend thoroughly.
3. Store in covered container in refrigerator.
4. Serve with bread or rye crackers. Makes approximately 2 1/2 cups.
5. Can use canned beef from Super Market Deli.

title: Pot Roast Norway
description: A comforting beef pot roast, slowly cooked until exceptionally tender in a flavorful sauce with tomato, onion, and aromatic spices like caraway and bay leaves. Perfect for a hearty family meal.
instructions:
1. Roll meat in flour; brown slowly on all sides in little hot fat.
2. Mix and add remaining ingredients.
3. Cover; cook slowly 2 1/2 hours or until tender.

title: Orange Mushroom Salad
description: A refreshing salad combining crisp lettuce, juicy orange slices, mushrooms, and red onion, all tossed in a zesty French dressing. Finished with a sprinkle of raisins for a touch of sweetness and texture.
instructions:
1. Toss lettuce, orange slices, mushrooms and onions with dressing until well coated.
2. Serve on lettuce leaves.
3. Top with raisins, if desired.

title: Poor Man'S Pierogies
description: An easy and comforting take on pierogies, using pasta, mashed potatoes, and cheese, layered and baked. It's a simplified version of the Polish classic, offering similar flavors with less effort.
instructions:
1. Cook butter and onion in skillet. Pour 3/4 of the onions into the bottom of a Pyrex dish, reserving the remaining onion.
2. Cook bow ties.
3. Drain.
4. Put half of the bow ties on top of the onion in the Pyrex dish. Add cheeses and mashed potatoes.
5. Add the rest of the bow ties and mix.
6. Pour the rest of the butter and onions on top. Bake at 350° for 1 hour.

title: Hungarian Stuffed Cabbage
description: Hungarian Stuffed Cabbage features tender cabbage leaves filled with a savory mixture of ground beef and rice, slow-cooked in a rich tomato sauce. This hearty and flavorful dish is a classic Eastern European comfort food.
instructions:
1. Core the cabbage and cook it in boiling water until barely tender.
2. Drain and separate 12 large leaves and trim ribs. Combine onion, shallot, egg whites, garlic, carrot and parsley until thoroughly blended.
3. Combine onion mixture with rice, ground beef, bread crumbs, vegetable seasoning and tomato juice, using a fork to mix.
4. Add frozen peas.
5. Place 1/3 cup of the mixture in each cabbage leaf and roll up, tucking in sides.
6. Makes 12 rolls. Shred remaining cabbage; sprinkle in bottom of 2-quart casserole. Place cabbage rolls in casserole seam-side down, packing tightly.

title: Polish Christmas Cookies
description: Delicate and sweet Polish Christmas Cookies, perfect for holiday celebrations. These buttery treats are flavored with anise and vanilla, offering a traditional taste of Polish festive baking.
instructions:
1. Cream together first 3 ingredients.
2. Boil Hartshorn in 2 tablespoons water until dissolved.
3. Add anise oil, Hartshorn and vanilla to creamed mixture.
4. Sift baking powder and 2 cups flour into creamed mixture and beat.
5. Alternate milk and enough flour to stiffen.
6. Roll out on floured surface to desired thickness.
7. Cut with cookie cutters.
8. Bake on ungreased cookie sheet at 350° for 10 to 12 minutes.
9. Frost with a Crisco frosting and dip into colored sugars within the same 24 hours.
10. Bake thin and crispy or thick and cakey.
11. Yields 500 to 1000 cookies.

title: Marion'S Rye Bread
description: A hearty and flavorful homemade rye bread, perfect for sandwiches or as a side with rich stews. This recipe yields a dense and aromatic loaf, an ode to traditional European baking.
instructions:
1. Pour 1 quart boiling water on top.
2. Mix well.
3. When cooled to lukewarm (115°), add 1 package dry yeast, dissolved in 1/4 cup warm water.
4. Add white flour (takes quite a bit).
5. Let rise, make into 4 loaves, prick with a fork and let rise again.
6. Bake at 350° for 35 minutes.

title: Kartoflane Kluski(Polish Potato Noodles)
description: Traditional Polish Potato Noodles, known as 'Kartoflane Kluski', are simple yet delicious. Made from potatoes, flour, and egg, they are often served as a side dish or with savory sauces.
instructions:
1. Stir all ingredients together until well mixed.
2. Dough will be soft and appear wet.
3. With the side of spoon that has been dipped in cold water, cut off approximately 1/4-inch portion of dough against side of bowl.
4. Working quickly, drop one portion at a time into four quarts salted, boiling water.
5. Cook for 15 minutes, stirring frequently.
6. Drain in colander.

title: Borscht(Yield: 6 Servings)
description: This slow-cooker Borscht is a comforting and rich beet soup, packed with vegetables like carrots, celery, and potatoes, and seasoned with dill and caraway seeds. It yields a deeply flavorful broth, perfect for a warming meal.
instructions: 1. Lightly spray a 4 to 6-quart slow cooker with vegetable oil cooking spray. Place the remaining ingredients in the slow cooker and stir to combine. Cover and cook on low for 8 to 10 hours or until vegetables are tender. Add salt to taste. If desired, serve with dollops of sour cream and fresh dill.

title: Quick Russian Tea
description: A quick and effortless Russian Tea mix, combining lemonade, cinnamon, cloves, and sugar for a warmly spiced beverage. Simply add hot water for a comforting drink.
instructions: 1. Mix all ingredients together thoroughly and put into a tightly closed container. Use 2 to 3 heaping teaspoons of mixture for each cup hot water. Will keep up to six months.

title: The Best Pound Cake
description: A classic and rich pound cake, boasting a dense yet tender crumb and a delightful buttery flavor. Perfect for serving plain, with fruit, or a dollop of whipped cream.
instructions:
1. Cream butter and sugar.
2. Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition.
3. Add vanilla.
4. Gradually add the flour.
5. Bake in a large, greased and floured tube pan at 350° for 1 hour to 1 hour and 15 minutes.

title: Forget Me Nots
description: Forget Me Nots are savory appetizers featuring a flavorful mixture of ground beef and pork, melted Velveeta cheese, and Italian seasonings, served on party rye bread. They're quick to make and perfect for a gathering.
instructions:
1. In frying pan, brown beef and pork.
2. Add cheese, oregano, Worcestershire sauce, garlic salt and Italian seasoning.
3. Cook over low heat, stirring frequently, until cheese is melted. Spread on bread and bake at 350° for 10 minutes or until bubbly. May be prepared ahead and frozen before baking.
4. Serves 16.

title: Unstuffed Cabbage Casserole
description: A comforting baked casserole featuring layers of shredded cabbage, seasoned ground beef and rice, all simmered in a savory tomato soup sauce. This dish offers all the flavors of traditional stuffed cabbage rolls without the rolling effort, perfect for a hearty family meal.
instructions: 1. Shred cabbage and place in greased 9 x 13-inch baking dish. Brown hamburger with onion. Stir in rice. Place meat/rice mixture on cabbage. Mix tomato soup and water. Add bouillon and pour over mixture in baking dish. Cover with foil and bake at 350° for 1 1/4 hours.

title: Quick Quesadilla
description: A quick and easy quesadilla recipe featuring buttered tortillas filled with cheese dip, crispy bacon, and green chilies. Perfect for a fast snack or light meal, these quesadillas are stacked and cooked until golden and delicious.
instructions:
1. Lightly brush each tortilla with melted butter.
2. Spread cheese dip over 1 tortilla to within 1/2 inch of edges.
3. Sprinkle with bacon and chilies.
4. Top with second tortilla.
5. Place stacked tortillas on microwave-safe plate.
6. Microwave on High (100%) power for 1 1/2 minutes.
7. Cut into wedges.
8. Serve with Old El Paso Thick 'n Chunky salsa or picante, if desired.
9. Makes 1 quesadilla.

title: Quick Borscht
description: A rapid rendition of the classic beet soup, combining beef broth, beets, cabbage, and onion, seasoned with sugar and lemon juice. This chilled borscht is a refreshing and flavorful starter, perfect when time is of the essence.
instructions:
1. Heat together beef broth, beets (with juice), cabbage, onion and sugar to boiling point.
2. Reduce heat and simmer gently 5 minutes.
3. Stir in lemon juice and chill thoroughly.
4. Top each serving with a spoonful of sour cream.

title: Sauerkraut In A Jar
description: A traditional method for preparing and preserving fresh cabbage as sauerkraut in glass jars. Shredded cabbage is packed with canning salt and boiling water, then sealed for a long-lasting, tangy condiment perfect for various Eastern European dishes.
instructions:
1. Gather fresh cabbage (is best).
2. Shred as desired.
3. Pack, not too tight, into sterilized jars.
4. To each quart jar, add canning or clean stock salt.
5. Pour briskly over this boiling water.
6. With stainless steel knife, punch out air.
7. Quickly seal jars and set aside.
8. In a few days, it will spoil but don't bother it.
9. In about 18 to 21 days, it is ready to use or replace lids with new ones and hot water bath for 5 minutes.
10. Remove and it seals.
11. This will keep for a long time.

title: Stuffed Cabbages
description: Classic stuffed cabbage rolls, featuring tender cabbage leaves wrapped around a hearty mixture of ground beef, pork, rice, and onion, then simmered in a rich tomato and sauerkraut sauce. This traditional dish offers comforting flavors, perfect for a family dinner.
instructions:
1. Core and steam cabbage until leaves can be peeled off in whole pieces.
2. In a large bowl, mix together meats, rice, onion, salt and pepper.
3. Add water to meat/rice mixture until "sloppy" to the touch; more is better than not enough.
4. Line bottom of roast pan with outer, torn cabbage leaves (reserve enough to cover casserole, also).
5. Pour 1/2 can of sauerkraut over leaves.
6. Roll small amount of meat/rice mixture in individual cabbage leaves, starting from stem side and tucking in edges.
7. Place rolls in layers on top of sauerkruat.
8. Add remaining sauerkraut with juice, tomatoes
9. with juice and 1/2 can water.
10. Top with remaining torn leaves which will burn while cooking and will be discarded. Cover and bake at 350° for 1 hour. Serve with mashed potatoes and crusty bread.

title: Barley-Stuffed Cabbage Rolls
description: Hearty cabbage rolls filled with a savory mixture of barley, onion, garlic, cheddar cheese, and egg, then baked in a flavorful tomato-chicken broth with a hint of lemon. This dish offers a hearty and satisfying meal, perfect for a comforting dinner.
instructions:
1. Place cabbage leaves in a large bowl.
2. Cover with boiling water.
3. Set aside. Bring 2 cups of water to a boil in medium saucepan.
4. Stir barley into boiling water.
5. Cover.
6. Simmer about 1 hour or until tender, stirring occasionally.
7. Drain.
8. Cook onion and garlic in oil in skillet until tender.
9. Add spinach.
10. Cook and stir 1 minute.

title: Reuben Sausage Casserole
description: A savory casserole combining partially boiled potatoes, browned ground sausage, tangy sauerkraut, and Swiss cheese, baked until golden and bubbly. This dish offers a hearty and comforting meal, perfect for a quick and satisfying dinner.
instructions:
1. Partially boil potatoes in water and drain.
2. Brown ground sausage in skillet.
3. Put all ingredients together (except the cheese) and put in baking dish.
4. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
5. The last 5 or 10 minutes, add the shredded Swiss cheese on top to melt.

title: Potato Latke Recipe
description: Crispy and savory potato pancakes, known as latkes, made from grated potatoes, onion, and eggs, seasoned and fried until golden brown. Perfect served with applesauce or sour cream, these are a delicious side dish or appetizer.
instructions:
1. Grate potatoes and onion on the fine side of grater, or in food processor or through blender with a little water added to it. Add eggs and mix well.
2. Add matzo meal and sesaoning and mix well. Heat oil in frying pan, then add mixture 1 tablespoon at a time into frying pan.
3. When golden brown, turn over and brown other side.

title: Krusciki(Polish Crullers)
description: Delicate Polish crullers, or Krusciki, made from a rich dough of egg yolks, sugar, sweet cream, and vanilla, fried until golden and crispy. These traditional pastries are a sweet and elegant treat, perfect for special occasions.
instructions:
1. Beat egg yolks in bowl until thick; add salt and vanilla. Continue beating.
2. Add cream and flour alternately until well blended.
3. Turn on floured board.
4. Cover, allow to rest for 10 minutes.
5. Knead until dough begins to blister (about 5 minutes). Roll out thin and cut into strips 1-inch wide and 4 inches long. Cut a gash in the center and twist ends through.
6. Fry in deep fat at 375° until lightly browned.
7. Drain on absorbent paper.
8. When cool, dust with powdered sugar.

title: Mexican Willies
description: Easy-to-make appetizers featuring a creamy mixture of cream cheese, green onions, and Cheddar cheese, spread onto tortillas and rolled. Refrigerate overnight, then slice and serve for a quick and crowd-pleasing snack.
instructions:
1. Mix first 5 ingredients together.
2. Spread on large tortilla shell.
3. Roll.
4. Refrigerate overnight.
5. Slice and serve.

title: Russian Borsch
description: Authentic Russian borscht, prepared with a rich meat stock as a base, then enhanced with cabbage, onion, pureed beets, carrots, and garlic. This warming and flavorful soup is a staple of Russian cuisine, perfect for a comforting meal.
instructions:
1. Add cabbage and onion to boiling stock.
2. Meanwhile, cook and puree other vegetables, then add to the soup and cook about 10 minutes more.
3. Salt to taste.

title: Steak And Bacon Tornedos
description: Elegant tornedos of flank steak wrapped in bacon, seasoned with garlic powder and fresh ground pepper, then baked until tender. These savory bites are perfect as an appetizer or a sophisticated main course, ideal for entertaining.
instructions: 1. You will need toothpicks.

title: Cheese Stuffed Eggs
description: Flavorful deviled eggs filled with a creamy mixture of mashed yolks, cheese spread, spicy green chili peppers, and salt, then sprinkled with paprika. This classic appetizer is quickly prepared and chilled, perfect for any gathering.
instructions:
1. Halve eggs lengthwise.
2. Remove yolks; mash.
3. Blend yolks with cheese spread, chili peppers and salt.
4. Refill egg whites with yolk mixture and sprinkle each with paprika.
5. Chill.

title: Bavarian Casserole(Kielbasa)
description: A hearty Bavarian-style casserole featuring sliced kielbasa sausage, potatoes, sauerkraut, and a creamy sauce made with mayonnaise and cream of chicken soup. Topped with bread crumbs and baked until bubbly, it's a comforting and easy one-dish meal.
instructions:
1. Mix all.
2. Top with bread crumbs.
3. Bake at 350° for 1 hour.

title: Quick Skillet Potato Pancakes
description: Quick skillet potato pancakes made from instant mashed potato flakes, milk, egg, and onion, fried until golden and crispy. These convenient pancakes are a delicious alternative to traditional latkes, perfect with applesauce.
instructions: 1. In a small bowl, combine potato flakes and flour. Add remaining ingredients, except oil and applesauce and, using a fork, blend thoroughly. In a 10-inch nonstick skillet, heat oil. Drop batter into skillet by heaping tablespoons, forming 4 pancakes. Cook until bottoms of pancakes are browned. Turn pancakes and brown other side. Remove to serving plate and serve with applesauce.

title: Jello Russian Salad
description: A retro-inspired Jell-O salad combining lime Jell-O with marshmallows, cream cheese, pecans, pineapple, and banana, set until firm. This sweet, tangy, and creamy dessert or side dish is a unique and refreshing treat.
instructions:
1. Put water in pan and bring to boil.
2. Add jello.
3. Mix in marshmallows and cream cheese; stir until melted.
4. Let cool.
5. Add remaining ingredients and chill.

title: Blini
description: Classic Russian blini, thin and tender pancakes made from a simple batter of flour, buttermilk, and egg, fried quickly until golden. These versatile crepes are perfect served with sweet or savory fillings, from sour cream and jam to caviar.
instructions:
1. Mix all ingredients in a large bowl.
2. Pour 1/2 cup at a time into hot oil.

title: Polish Sausage Supper
description: A rustic and hearty Polish supper combining savory sausage, tender potatoes, wedges of cabbage, and sweet corn, all simmered in chicken broth. This simple one-pot meal offers comforting flavors, perfect for a satisfying family dinner.
instructions:
1. Put sausage in the bottom of a large kettle.
2. Cut cabbage into 6 wedges.
3. Core and place on top of sausage.
4. Peel potatoes.
5. Cut in half lengthwise and place on top of cabbage.
6. Husk and remove silk from corn.
7. Place corn on top of potatoes.
8. Pour broth over layered meat and vegetables.
9. Cover and bring to a boil.
10. Reduce heat and simmer until potatoes are tender, about 45 minutes.

title: Clear Borsch
description: A traditional clear borscht, made by simmering soup meat with cold water, salt, beets, onion, cabbage, tomato juice, garlic, celery, and carrots. This robust and flavorful broth-based soup is slowly cooked for maximum depth of flavor.
instructions:
1. Cover the meat with the water.
2. Add the salt, bring slowly to a boil and skim.
3. Cover and simmer for about 1 hour.
4. Add the vegetables and continue simmering for another hour.
5. Pour in the tomato juice.
6. Use enough lemon juice to get desired tartness. The garlic may or may not be added.
7. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
8. Bring to a boil.
9. If time permits, let the borsch stand for a while to blend the flavors.
10. Strain through a sieve.
11. Serve the clear borsch hot or cold with sour cream.

title: Bacon Cheddar Appetizer
description: Savory bacon cheddar appetizers, featuring a creamy mixture of crispy bacon, sharp Cheddar cheese, onion, and mayonnaise, spread on party rye bread. Baked until bubbly and golden, these are a perfect crowd-pleasing snack.
instructions:
1. Mix all ingredients well.
2. Spread a generous amount of mixture on party rye slices.
3. Bake on cookie sheet at 350° until brown and bubbly.
4. If you prefer, you can broil rather than bake.

title: Neysa'S Stroganoff
description: A classic Russian Stroganoff made with lean ground beef, onion, and sour cream, perfect for a hearty and comforting meal. This simple version comes together quickly on the stovetop.
instructions:
1. Sprinkle flavor crystals over ground beef.
2. In large skillet, brown beef; crumble and drain fat.
3. Add flour, milk, onion flakes and onion soup mix.
4. Cook on low heat for 20 minutes, stirring constantly.
5. Add sour cream and cook on low heat for another 10 minutes.
6. Serve over cooked noodles or rice.
7. Serves 6.

title: Country Spareribs And Sauerkraut
description: This comforting slow-cooked dish features tender country spareribs, tangy sauerkraut, and sweet root vegetables. Ideal for a cozy dinner with minimal effort thanks to the crock-pot.
instructions: 1. Brown the spareribs in a skillet with oil. Put the ribs in a crock-pot and then add sauerkraut, brown sugar, potatoes and carrots. Add about 1/4 cup of water. Cook on low for about 8 hours.

title: Meatless Borsch
description: A hearty, meatless adaptation of the classic Russian borscht, brimming with a variety of vegetables like cabbage, beets, and carrots. Simmered to perfection for a flavorful and warming soup.
instructions:
1. Melt oleo and put all vegetables in with the tomato soup and water.
2. Cover and simmer until vegetables are soft, about 1 hour. Add 2 quarts water and shredded beets shoestring style and continue to cook.
3. Then beat together milk, egg and flour.
4. Add to soup and simmer for a few minutes (one or two).
5. Good with or without sour cream.

title: Hot Sausage On Rye Bread
description: Quick and savory open-faced sandwiches featuring a rich blend of ground beef, hot pork sausage, and melted Velveeta cheese. Broiled to perfection, these make for a satisfying snack or light meal.
instructions:
1. Brown meat together.
2. Drain off fat.
3. Add spices, sauce and cheese.
4. Mix well until it melts.
5. Put on bread.
6. Put under broiler.

title: Russian Salad
description: A sweet and creamy dessert salad featuring marshmallows, lime Jell-O, cream cheese, pineapple, and pecans. This chilled treat is a delightful and refreshing option for any occasion.
instructions:
1. Mix marshmallows, Jell-O and cream cheese with hot water; dissolve over low heat.
2. Remove from heat.
3. Add other ingredients. Chill in molds until set.

title: Dark Bread
description: A robust dark bread, enriched with molasses and brown sugar, offering a full-bodied flavor and tender crumb. Ideal for sandwiches or served alongside savory dishes.
instructions:
1. Soak yeast and sugar for 15 minutes in water.
2. Add remaining ingredients and 2 cups white flour.
3. Beat 5 to 10 minutes with mixer.
4. Stir in rest of flour, enough to make a not too stiff bread dough.
5. Knead.
6. Place in greased bowl and cover.
7. Let rise until double in bulk.
8. Punch down.
9. Let rise again.
10. Shape into loaves.
11. Let rise and bake at 375° until done, about 45 minutes.

title: Okroshka
description: A refreshing Russian cold soup, combining chopped meat, eggs, radishes, and green onions in a tangy sour cream base. Traditionally served with kvas or beer, perfect for warm weather.
instructions:
1. Mix ingredients thoroughly; chill.
2. Serve with kvas or beer.

title: Russian Tea With Pineapple
description: A comforting and aromatic Russian tea blend featuring instant tea, orange-pineapple Jell-O, cinnamon, and cloves. Simply mix and store for a quick, warming beverage.
instructions:
1. Mix and store in jar.
2. Use 3 tablespoons to a cup of hot water.

title: Ham Stuffed Cabbage Rolls
description: Savory cabbage rolls, generously stuffed with a flavorful mix of ground ham, onions, breadcrumbs, and spices. Braised in a rich tomato sauce, these make a hearty and satisfying meal.
instructions: 1. Stir the first 8 ingredients together in a bowl. Drop head of cabbage into a large pot of boiling water and start nudging off the leaves as they loosen.
