Divide the filling into 12 portions; roll into sausage shape and put into cabbage leaf. Saute the 1 cup sliced onions with the butter; cook until onions are translucent. In large frypan, arrange cabbage rolls in a single layer; add Italian tomatoes with juice, salt and pepper to taste. Bury the bay leaves in pan. Cover and simmer slowly, basting occasionally, for 30 minutes or until cabbage is tender.

title: Salad Dressing For Broccoli And Mushroom Salad
description: A simple yet flavorful vinaigrette, perfect for enhancing a broccoli and mushroom salad. Its balanced blend of sweet and tangy notes complements fresh vegetables beautifully.
instructions: 1. Mix and add to salad just before serving.

title: Easy Sauerkraut Supper
description: An easy and flavorful sauerkraut supper featuring slow-simmered sauerkraut with onions, apples, and caraway seed, enhanced by white wine, and served with tender red potatoes and sausage.
instructions:
1. In medium saucepan, melt butter over medium heat.
2. Add onion; cook, stirring often, 3 minutes.
3. Add sauerkraut, wine, apple, brown sugar and caraway seed; mix well.
4. Cover and simmer 30 minutes.
5. Add potatoes and cook, stirring occasionally, 30 minutes or until tender.
6. Add sausage or franks and cook 20 minutes or until heated through.

title: Crock-Pot Casserole
description: A minimal-effort Crock-Pot casserole combining tangy sauerkraut with savory sausage and potatoes, topped with melted Cheddar cheese and crunchy bread crumbs for a comforting meal.
instructions:
1. In the bottom of the crock-pot, cook sauerkraut and sausage until sausage is cooked throughout.
2. Note:
3. You may need to drain some juice off your sauerkraut depending on whether or not it is vacuum packed.
4. Layer mashed potatoes over sauerkraut and sausage. Then add cheese and bread crumbs and cook until cheese is melted, about 15 minutes on low.

title: Mushroom Sauce(Served On Christmas Eve Over Varenyky-Pyrohy)
description: A rich and savory mushroom sauce, traditionally served over varenyky or pierogi on Christmas Eve. Made with fresh and dried mushrooms, onion, and a hint of butter, it's deeply flavorful.
instructions:
1. Rinse dried mushrooms and soak about 5 to 10 minutes in enough water to cover.
2. Chop mushrooms and put in 1 quart saucepan.
3. Add cold water to cover, about 2 to 3 inches from the top of the mushrooms.
4. Cover; bring to a boil, reduce heat to simmer and add fresh chopped mushrooms.
5. Cook about 1 hour until soft.

title: Strawberries With White Chocolate Custard Sauce
description: An elegant dessert featuring fresh strawberries paired with a luxurious white chocolate custard sauce. This delicate and creamy sauce provides a sophisticated complement to sweet berries.
instructions: 1. In bowl, whisk yolks and sugar. In saucepan, bring cream, milk and vanilla bean to a boil. Gradually whisk hot cream mixture into yolk mixture. Return this mixture to saucepan; over medium heat, stir constantly with heat-resistant rubber spatula, 3 minutes until thick enough to coat spatula (do not let boil). Remove from heat. Add chocolate, stirring to melt. Strain through fine sieve into bowl. Remove bean; scrape out seeds into sauce. Place bowl in large bowl filled with ice cubes and water. Stir sauce until very cold. Place strawberries in 6 dessert glasses. Spoon 3 tablespoons custard sauce over each serving.

title: Tortilla Egg Casserole
description: A satisfying tortilla egg casserole, layered with savory turkey sausage, tangy salsa, and melted Cheddar cheese. Baked until golden, it’s perfect for breakfast, brunch, or a light dinner.
instructions:
1. Brown turkey sausage.
2. Heat oven to 350°.
3. Arrange tortillas to cover bottom and sides of greased 9 x 13-inch baking dish. Sprinkle with browned sausage and cheese.
4. Beat eggs slightly. Add milk and seasonings.
5. Mix well.
6. Pour over sausage and cheese. Bake 40 to 50 minutes, until knife comes out clean.
7. Serve salsa, sour cream and onion in side dishes.

title: Polish Filled Roll-Up
description: A delicious Polish filled roll-up, featuring a soft, tender dough made with yeast, milk, and eggs. This versatile dough is perfect for sweet or savory fillings.
instructions:
1. Combine first 2 ingredients in mixing bowl.
2. Add sugar, shortening, salt and milk.
3. Add eggs and the yeast, mix thoroughly. Blend in flour, beating well after each addition.
4. Cover.
5. Let rise in warm place 15 minutes.
6. Toss on floured surface to coat dough with flour.
7. Roll out and spread with filling, starting at one edge and spreading to within 1/3 inch of other three edges.
8. Roll as for jelly roll, starting with the covered edge.
9. Seal.
10. Place on greased baking sheet, seam-side down.
11. Brush with cream and cover. Let rise in warm place until light and doubled in size.
12. Bake at 375° for 25 to 30 minutes.

title: Potato Pancake Appetizers
description: Crispy potato pancake appetizers, secretly packed with grated carrot and zucchini, offering a delicious twist on a classic. Served with a dollop of low-fat sour cream and fresh herbs.
instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 425°. Wrap potatoes in several layers of paper towels; squeeze to remove excess moisture. Beat egg, flour, salt and pepper in large bowl. Add potatoes, carrot and zucchini; mix well. Oil 2 nonstick baking sheets. Place vegetable mixture by heaping spoonfuls onto baking sheets; flatten slightly. Bake 8 to 15 minutes, until bottoms are browned. Turn; bake 5 to 10 minutes more. Stir together sour cream and herbs; serve with warm potatoes.

title: Pierogi And Filling
description: Handmade Pierogi dough, kneaded to a soft, rollable consistency, ready to be filled with your favorite savory or sweet fillings. A comforting staple of Polish cuisine.
instructions:
1. Mix flour with salt.
2. Add warm water until it becomes doughy; knead with hands, adding flour if needed until it is a soft rollable dough.
3. Roll dough on floured board or counter until 1/4-inch thick.
4. Make circles with cutter dipped in flour about 2 to 3-inches in diameter.
5. Place 1 tablespoon of filling in center. Fold over, pinching edges together, and pinch to seal.
6. Drop the pierogi into boiling salted water and gently stir so not to stick together.
7. Cook until they float to the top and continue for 5 minutes.
8. Do not place on top of each other.
9. To prevent sticking, brush with vegetable oil when cool.
10. To serve, fry in margarine until golden brown.
11. Serve with sour cream.

title: Beef Stroganoff Sandwich
description: A comforting beef stroganoff sandwich, featuring seasoned ground beef, onions, and sour cream, served warm on French bread and topped with melted Cheddar cheese. A hearty and satisfying meal.
instructions:
1. Brown hamburger; drain.
2. Add onion, pepper, tomatoes and seasonings.
3. Stir in sour cream.
4. When ready to serve, cut French bread in half.
5. Cover each half with the hamburger mixture.
6. Cover with shredded cheese.
7. Bake at 350° for 20 minutes until cheese is melted.

title: Blintz Kuchen
description: A delightful Blintz Kuchen, featuring a rich, buttery pastry base with a sweet walnut, sugar, and cinnamon filling. Perfect for a comforting dessert or brunch treat.
instructions: 1. Grease bottom of an 8-inch round cake pan.

title: Paczki (Jelly Donuts)(Polish)
description: These delicious Polish jelly donuts, known as Paczki, feature a rich, yeasted dough often filled with fruit preserves or cream. Perfect for a sweet treat or special occasion.
instructions:
1. Sift 1 cup flour into boiling milk; remove from heat and beat until smooth.
2. Let cool.
3. Dissolve yeast in the 1/4 cup milk.
4. Add to flour mixture; stir and let stand a half an hour.

title: Italian Mushroom Salad
description: A simple and refreshing Italian mushroom salad, combining fresh mushrooms, olives, pimento, and parsley with a zesty Wish dressing. Perfect as a light appetizer or side dish.
instructions:
1. Combine all ingredients in large bowl.
2. Marinate at least three hours in refrigerator, stirring occasionally.
3. Makes about 4 cups.

title: Ham Tortilla Rolls(Serves 15)
description: Easy-to-make ham tortilla rolls, featuring a savory cream cheese and ham filling with green onions and chilies, all rolled in soft flour tortillas. Ideal for a quick appetizer or snack.
instructions:
1. Let cream cheese set out to soften.
2. Chop ham finely.
3. Mix all ingredients together and spread thin on flour tortillas.
4. Roll-up, cover and refrigerate for at least 3 to 4 hours, preferably overnight.
5. Slice in 1/2-inch rounds.
6. Serve with your favorite salsa on top.

title: Festive Hors D'Oeuvres
description: Festive spiral hors d'oeuvres made from tortillas spread with a creamy mixture of cream cheese, green onions, red pepper, and celery. A colorful and easy appetizer for any gathering.
instructions:
1. Mix all ingredients except tortillas and spread on tortillas. Roll up and chill 2 hours.
2. Cut into 1-inch slices and lay flat on serving platter for festive spiral hors d'oeuvres.

title: Cold Beet Soup
description: A vibrant and refreshing cold beet soup, pureed with chicken broth, onion, lemon, and fresh dill, then finished with a dollop of sour cream. Perfect for a light and cooling meal.
instructions:
1. In blender or food processor, combine beets, broth, onion, lemon, dill weed and salt to taste.
2. Process until all ingredients are pureed.
3. Pour into refrigerator container and stir in sour cream or yogurt.
4. Refrigerate several hours or overnight to blend flavors.
5. Serve cold.
6. Garnish with fresh dill, parsley and/or thin cucumber slices.
7. Makes 4 servings.

title: Parsley Potato Pancakes
description: Crispy and savory potato pancakes, mixed with eggs, flour, onion, salt, and fresh parsley, then fried to golden perfection. Serve with a dollop of sour cream for a delightful treat.
instructions:
1. Squeeze the grated potatoes in a towel until fairly dry.
2. Mix with the eggs, flour, onion, salt and parsley.
3. Heat a heavy pan or griddle.
4. Add a light coating of oil.
5. Ladle on the mixture and form pancakes.
6. Cook until brown.
7. Turn and cook on the other side (about 4 to 6 minutes a side).
8. Garnish with sour cream.
9. Makes 2 to 3 servings.

title: My Grandmother'S Stroganoff
description: A comforting stroganoff featuring tender beef pieces browned and then slowly baked with onions, mushrooms, cream of mushroom soup, and a hint of white wine. A classic, hearty meal.
instructions:
1. Cut meat in 1 1/4-inch pieces, roll in flour and brown in large pan with margarine.
2. Saute onions and mushrooms in drippings; add to meat with undiluted soup.
3. Place in casserole dish and bake at 350° until meat is tender, approximately 1 hour. In small pan, mix gravy mix, sour cream and wine.
4. Stir over low heat until well combined.
5. Add to casserole and serve over noodles, rice or mashed potatoes.

title: Hunter'S Stew(A Polish Recipe)
description: A hearty Polish Hunter's Stew, or Bigos, made with cabbage, sauerkraut, pork chops, and kielbasa, simmered with mushrooms and tomato sauce. A flavorful and satisfying dish perfect for colder days.
instructions:
1. In large Dutch oven, place cabbage, sauerkraut and pork chops. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
2. Add bay leaf, mushrooms and water; cook, covered, for 1 hour and 30 minutes, or until chops are tender.
3. Cool mixture, then remove bones from chops.
4. Cut chops into bite size pieces.
5. Refrigerate all until 1 hour before serving, then peel and slice kielbasi about 1/2-inch thick and add to cabbage with pork and tomato sauce.
6. Cook, covered, about 1 hour, or until pork is tender and stew is piping hot.
7. Serve with boiled potatoes.

title: Jules' Chicken Tortilla Soup
description: A quick and flavorful chicken tortilla soup, brimming with chicken, corn, Ro-Tel, and a blend of seasonings, simmered to perfection. Garnish with cheese and tortilla chips for a complete meal.
instructions:
1. Put broth, corn, Ro-Tel and tomato sauce in large stock pan. Add chicken, onion and seasonings.
2. Bring to a boil, then simmer until ready to eat.
3. Top with shredded Cheddar cheese, crumbled tortilla chips and chives.

title: Buttermilk-Buckwheat Pancakes
description: Fluffy buttermilk-buckwheat pancakes, a wholesome option for breakfast or brunch, combining the nutty flavor of buckwheat with the tang of buttermilk. Serve with your favorite toppings.
instructions: 1. Combine first 4 ingredients in a medium bowl; make a well in center of mixture.

title: Spinach And Mushroom Salad
description: A classic spinach and mushroom salad, featuring fresh spinach leaves, hard-boiled eggs, and sautéed mushrooms, often dressed with a warm bacon vinaigrette. A simple yet elegant side.
instructions: 1. Prepare spinach, tearing leaves into bite-size pieces and placing in a salad bowl. Add eggs, mushrooms and bacon.

title: Sauerkraut Rye Bread
description: A unique sauerkraut rye bread, combining all-purpose and rye flours with caraway seeds and undrained sauerkraut for a distinctive tangy flavor and moist texture. Perfect with savory dishes.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°.
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine 1 cup of all-purpose flour, 1/4 cup rye flour, yeast, dry milk, sugar, caraway seed, salt and ginger; mix well.
3. In saucepan, heat water and oil until warm (120° to 130°).
4. Add to flour mixture.
5. Blend at low speed until moistened; beat 3 minutes at medium speed.
6. By hand, gradually stir in sauerkraut, remaining rye flour and enough remaining all-purpose flour to make a firm dough.
7. Knead on floured surface until smooth and elastic, about 5 minutes.
8. Divide into 2 parts.

title: Mother-In-Law'S Beef Stroganoff
description: A comforting beef stroganoff with tender strips of seasoned beef, simmered with consommé, mushrooms, and sour cream, served over curlycue noodles. A hearty and flavorful family favorite.
instructions:
1. Cook noodles; drain.
2. Season steak; cut in strips.
3. Roll in flour; brown in butter.
4. Add consomme and garlic; simmer until tender.
5. Add mushroom soup, mushrooms and sour cream.
6. Mix well; add noodles.
7. Put in 3-quart casserole; top with grated cheese and potato chips.
8. Heat through in 350° oven until browned on top. Makes 10 servings.

title: Mini-Reuben Hors D'Oeuvres
description: Quick and easy mini-Reuben hors d'oeuvres, made with cocktail rye bread, corned beef, sauerkraut, Swiss cheese, and salad dressing, then broiled until warm and melty. Perfect party bites.
instructions:
1. Layer bread slices on cookie sheet.
2. Put a teaspoon of dressing, then a piece of corned beef, sauerkraut and top with a small piece of Swiss cheese.
3. Put under broiler for 3 minutes.

title: Hollendacke Ragge Brood(Holland Rye Bread)
description: A traditional Holland Rye Bread, infused with syrup and brown sugar, offering a rich, dense texture and a subtly sweet-savory flavor. A satisfying bread for any meal.
instructions:
1. Mix all ingredients together.
2. Add 3 cups boiling water.
3. Mix thoroughly.
4. Bake in 250° oven 2 1/2 hours, longer if needed. Cover with a pan of the same size.
5. Put cup of water in oven while baking.

title: Mrs. Frank Graham'S Pound Cake(Mother Of Rev. Billy Graham)
description: A classic pound cake recipe, delivering a rich, buttery, and dense crumb with a hint of mace, perfect for enjoying plain or with fruit. A timeless dessert staple.
instructions:
1. Cream butter and sugar until light and fluffy.
2. Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition.
3. Beat butter, sugar and egg mixture thoroughly.
4. Blend in flour, sifted with salt and baking powder,
5. gradually, mixing well.
6. Add mace and blend thoroughly.
7. Then slowly add heavy cream and mix well.
8. Turn batter into large 10-inch tube pan and bake in slow oven at 325° for 1 hour or until cake tests done.

title: Tossed Fresh Mushroom Salad
description: A simple and refreshing tossed fresh mushroom salad, featuring sliced mushrooms, tender celery, green onions, and pimento, marinated in a flavorful dressing. A light and healthy side dish.
instructions:
1. Combine ingredients.
2. Marinate and chill at least 1 hour. Serve.

title: Stuffed Cabbage And Sauerkraut
description: Hearty stuffed cabbage rolls filled with a mixture of ground pork and beef, seasoned with dill and caraway, then simmered with sauerkraut and bacon. A robust and flavorful Eastern European classic.
instructions:
1. Soak the head of cabbage for 10 minutes in boiling water. Drain and carefully separate leaves removing all hard core.
2. Mix together the ground beef and pork with egg, spices and chopped onion, bread crumbs and diced bacon.
3. Divide the mixture onto the cabbage leaves.
4. Roll and secure with toothpicks.
5. Arrange sauerkraut over bottom of pan.
6. Lay cabbage rolls on top. Sprinkle with crumbled bacon.
7. Pour V-8 juice over top and simmer for 1 hour.
8. Before serving, stir in sour cream and heat thoroughly. Serve with rye bread.

title: Polish Pierogi Dumplings With Fillings
description: Authentic Polish Pierogi dumplings with various fillings, featuring a soft, pliable dough made from flour, eggs, and water. A beloved dish, perfect for family meals or festive occasions.
instructions:
1. Mix flour, egg and salt.
2. Add enough water to make medium soft dough.
3. Knead until blisters appear.
4. Divide dough into 2 portions.
5. Roll each portion thin; cut into 2-inch squares.
6. Place a spoonful of filling in each.
7. Fold in half to make a triangle. Pinch edges.
8. Drop into boiling water until pierogi comes to top, about 10 minutes.
9. After pierogi are boiled and drained, brown butter in skillet and pour over pierogi.

title: Linda'S Goulash(Quick 'N Easy)
description: A quick and easy goulash, featuring ground beef and onions simmered with tomato soup, diced tomatoes, and macaroni, creating a comforting and flavorful one-pot meal. Perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions:
1. Cook meat and onions in a large frying pan (drain if necessary).
2. Add tomato soup.
3. Pull tomatoes into small pieces and add with juice to pan.
4. Add macaroni and pepper; simmer 15 minutes.
5. Add pork and beans; simmer an additional 15 minutes. After beans are added, mixture will thicken.
6. Serves about 6.

title: Ribbon Torte
description: A delicious ribbon torte recipe.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°.
2. Grease and flour two round cake pans. Combine dry cake mix, eggs, oil and water in large mixing bowl. Mix as directed on package, but do not add contents of small packet.
3. Turn half of batter (about 2 1/2 cups) into one pan. Blend contents of small packet into remaining batter and turn into remaining pan.
4. Bake and cool as directed on package.

title: Stuffed Cabbage Rolls(Servings: 6)
description: A delicious stuffed cabbage rolls(servings: 6) recipe.
instructions: 1. You will need about 6 foot of cheesecloth.

title: Pork And Sauerkraut Dinner(Easy Dinner For A Busy Day)
description: A delicious pork and sauerkraut dinner(easy dinner for a busy day) recipe.
instructions:
1. Turn slow cooker to high.
2. Mix envelope of soup mix with 3/4 cup of hot tap water.
3. Pour this strong soup mix in the crock. Trim roast of external fat and cut it into large chunks.
4. Place in crock.
5. Rinse and drain sauerkraut twice.
6. Add to the crock.
7. Mix the brown sugar into the applesauce and pour over the pork and sauerkraut.
8. Cover and cook on high for about 5 hours or until the pork is fall-apart tender.
9. Makes 3 to 4 servings.

title: Cheesy Rice Latkes
description: A delicious cheesy rice latkes recipe.
instructions: 1. Combine brown rice, carrots, egg yolks, flour, cheese, salt and pepper in a medium bowl.

title: Golubtzi (Stuffed Cabbage)
description: A delicious golubtzi (stuffed cabbage) recipe.
instructions:
1. Remove outer leaves from cabbage and cook in salt water for 5 minutes or until leaves are flexible.
2. Drain and cool.
3. Add minced onion to fat and brown slightly.
4. Add beef and brown.
5. Add cooked rice and spices.
6. Drop tablespoon of meat mixture into stem end of each cabbage leaf.
7. Start rolling from stem end and fasten with toothpicks.
8. Place bundles in baking pan and pour undiluted tomato soup over them.
9. Bake at 350° for 45 minutes.
10. Add sour cream about 5 minutes before serving.
11. Serves 6.

title: Russian Boat Dip
description: A delicious russian boat dip recipe.
instructions:
1. Cut bread around, leaving the outer crust, top only to make a cover for the bread.
2. Scoop out the inside.
3. Cut inside part into small pieces to use for dip.
4. Mix all ingredients together.
5. Pour into the bread cavity.
6. This also makes a pretty centerpiece.

title: Mary'S Turkey Goulash
description: A delicious mary's turkey goulash recipe.
instructions:
1. Cook ground turkey, bell pepper and onion until turkey is brown.
2. Add garlic, pepper and salt to taste.
3. Stir in tomato sauce and cook until done.

title: Nanny Craig'S Russian Salad
description: A delicious nanny craig's russian salad recipe.
instructions:
1. Mix jello, water, cream cheese and marshmallows over low heat until dissolved.
2. Let cool, then add remaining ingredients. Congeal.

title: Magic Fudge Pie
description: A delicious magic fudge pie recipe.
instructions:
1. Combine chocolate, butter and eggs.
2. Stir until creamy.
3. Add flour, sugar, vanilla and pecans.
4. Pour ingredients into pie shell.
5. Bake at 375° for 25 minutes.
6. Serve warm with vanilla ice cream or whipped cream.

title: Espresso Torte
description: A delicious espresso torte recipe.
instructions:
1. Dissolve coffee powder in hot water; set aside.
2. Beat egg whites until stiff.
3. Gradually add sugar, beating until egg whites hold distinct short, glossy peaks.
4. Fold in coffee liquid; set aside.
5. Beat yolks until thick and light in color.
6. Whip the cream until stiff; blend in liqueur all at once.
7. Fold whites, yolks and cream together.
8. Pour into pan on top of crust.
9. Cover and freeze until firm (8 hours or longer).
10. Serves 12 to 14.

title: Cold Beet Soup-Russia(Svekolnik)
description: A delicious cold beet soup-russia(svekolnik) recipe.
instructions: 1. Bring chicken stock and water to a boil with the vegetables, garlic salt, and pepper. Simmer 10-15 minutes. Stir in the sugar. Continue cooking for 30 more minutes. Let soup cool to room temperature, then stir in lemon juice. Chill soup in refrigerator for several hours or overnight. Garnish with chopped parsley and sour cream.

title: Beef Borscht
description: A delicious beef borscht recipe.
instructions:
1. In large kettle, brown meat in oil.
2. Add 3 cups water and bay leaves.
3. Bring to boil; reduce heat.
4. Cover and simmer 50 to 60 minutes or until meat is nearly tender.
5. Stir in cabbage, potatoes, onion, green pepper, parsley flakes, bouillon and dill weed.
6. Bring to boil; reduce heat.
7. Cover and simmer 25 to 30 minutes more or until meat and potatoes are tender.
8. Stir in tomatoes; heat through.
9. Remove from heat.
10. Season with salt and pepper.
11. Stir in cream and remove bay leaves.
12. Serves 6.

title: Russian Tea Mix(Microwave)
description: A delicious russian tea mix(microwave) recipe.
instructions:
1. Remove only 1/2 of husk from corn.
2. Trim small end.
3. Place in microwave oven for 3 minutes on High.
4. Remove husk.
5. Will be very juicy and tender.
6. Add butter if desired.

title: Raggmunkar(Small Potato Pancakes For 4 Persons)
description: A delicious raggmunkar(small potato pancakes for 4 persons) recipe.
instructions:
1. Mix flour and salt.
2. Add half of the milk and whip it into a batter, free of lumps.
3. Add the rest of the milk and the egg. Peel the potatoes and shred them (a food processor works fine). Mix the shredded potatoes into the batter immediately, so that they don't turn gray.
4. Let some margarine or butter melt and turn light brown in a frying pan.
5. Fry the batter as pancakes (not quite 1 dl (1/3 cup) batter per pancake).
6. Serve the Raggmunkar directly from the pan with crisp fried bacon, lingonberry jam and shredded carrots.

title: Chicken Or Turkey Chow Bake
description: A delicious chicken or turkey chow bake recipe.
instructions:
1. Mix all ingredients except 1 1/2 cups noodles.
2. Put into greased casserole dish.
3. Put balance of noodles on top and bake at 350° for 45 minutes.

title: Mom'S Apple Crisp (Microwave)
description: A delicious mom's apple crisp (microwave) recipe.
instructions: 1. Peel and core the apples and pace in the bottom of a microwave safe bowl or dish. In a mixing bowl, mix remaining ingredients and pour over the top of the apples. Then place a few apples on top and microwave for 10 to 12 minutes and serve hot with ice cream.

title: Braided Bread (Challah)(Makes Two Loaves)
description: A delicious braided bread (challah)(makes two loaves) recipe.
instructions:
1. In large bowl, dissolve yeast and sugar in water.
2. Stir in one cup flour, salt, two eggs and oil.
3. Add remaining flour gradually, stirring until combined.
4. Turn out onto a lightly floured board. Knead 8 to 10 minutes until smooth and elastic (adding small amounts of flour as needed).
5. (Pretend you are making pottery.) Put dough in a large oil-coated bowl, turning over once to coat whole ball.
6. Cover bowl with large dish cloth and let rise in warm place for one hour.
7. Punch down dough, turn over in bowl, re-cover with cloth and let rise another 45 minutes.
8. Punch down again, place dough on lightly floured board and cut in two.

title: Sue'S Easy Fruit Pie
description: A delicious sue's easy fruit pie recipe.
instructions:
1. Spoon pie filling in each tortilla; roll up.
2. Place in baking dish.
3. Combine sugar, water and oleo.
4. Microwave this mixture until dissolved thoroughly.
5. Pour this over filled tortillas. Sprinkle with cinnamon and bake at 350° for 45 minutes.

title: Easy Borsch
description: A delicious easy borsch recipe.
instructions:
1. In medium saucepan, bring water to boil; stir in soup mix, beets, sugar and lemon juice.
2. Simmer, covered, 30 minutes or until beets are tender.
3. Remove beets and cool slightly.
4. Grate beets coarsely; return to saucepan.
5. Serve hot topped with sour cream.
6. Serves 4 to 6.

title: Quick Turkey Schnitzel
description: A delicious quick turkey schnitzel recipe.
instructions:
1. Rinse cutlets and pat dry.
2. Season with salt and pepper or Adolph's seasoning.
3. Blend egg, water and Dijon mustard on plate. Put bread crumbs on another plate.
4. Dip cutlets in egg mixture, then in bread crumbs.
5. Put 2 tablespoons oil in large skillet. Heat oil on medium heat until hot.
6. Fry cutlets one minute on each side until lightly browned.
7. Serve immediately.
8. Serves 4 to 5.

title: Spinach Mushroom Salad
description: A delightful spinach and mushroom salad featuring crispy bacon, hard-boiled eggs, and green onions. Tossed in a tangy homemade dressing, it’s a refreshing and easy side dish.
instructions:
1. Remove stems from spinach.
2. Tear into bite size pieces. Combine spinach, mushrooms, eggs, bacon and green onions in a large bowl.
3. Combine oil, vinegar, sugar and seasonings in a jar.
4. Cover tightly and shake vigorously.
5. Top spinach mixture with dressing just before serving.

title: Tortellini Salad(Great For A Crowd!)
description: A vibrant tortellini salad perfect for gatherings, packed with colorful vegetables like red and green peppers, onions, celery, olives, and beans. Marinated in red wine vinegar and Italian dressing, it's a flavorful and easy make-ahead dish.
instructions:
1. Add all ingredients.
2. Toss and marinate at least 2 hours.
3. Add avocado or mushrooms if you like.

title: Beet Soup Or Borsch
description: A classic beet soup, or borsch, with julienned beets simmered in stock, thickened with a flour and sour cream mixture, and brightened with vinegar. This comforting soup is a staple of Eastern European cuisine.
instructions:
1. Add the sliced beets with juice to the stock with a tablespoon or two of vinegar.
2. In a separate bowl, mix the flour and water to make a paste and add to the sour cream, stirring or mixing with an eggbeater.
3. The trick to not getting lumpy sour cream in soup is to add some hot soup (not boiling) to the flour mixture, a ladle at a time, stirring or mixing as you do this, for 6 or 7 ladles. Add this mixture to the pot of soup, stirring while adding.
4. Make sure the soup is not boiling.
5. If soup is still too sweet, add another tablespoon or two of vinegar or dill pickle juice.

title: Ouida'S Russian Salad
description: This sweet and creamy 'Russian Salad' is a retro dessert featuring lime jello, marshmallow creme, cream cheese, bananas, and pineapple. It's a chilled, fruity treat that evokes nostalgic charm.
instructions: 1. Dissolve jello in hot water. Add cream cheese and Marshmallow Creme. Place over low heat until Creme is melted. Remove and let cool. Add other ingredients. Chill and serve.

title: Olivier Salad
description: A traditional Olivier salad, made with finely diced boiled potatoes, carrots, eggs, meat, green peas, and dill pickles. Dressed with mayonnaise, it's a staple for celebrations in Russia and other post-Soviet countries.
instructions: 1. Boil potatoes and carrots in skin (it helps to keep the vitamins). Let them cool and then peel them. Boil eggs and meat. Chop potatoes, carrots, eggs, meat and dill pickles into 1/2-inch squares. Add green peas and salt. Trust your own taste, everything must be in proportion. Stir mayonnaise only for the part of salad you are going to eat. It will keep better without it. Mix the salad and refrigerate for a while. If you want your salad a little tender, mix a part of mayonnaise with an equal part of sour cream. Bon appetit ! ! !

title: Stuffed Cabbage Leaves
description: Flavorful stuffed cabbage leaves with a savory filling of ground veal, beef, rice, onion, dill, and thyme in tomato sauce. A hearty and comforting dish that’s popular across many cultures.
instructions: 1. Combine meats and saut with onion in butter until lightly browned. Add rice, dill, thyme, salt and pepper and mix well. Place cabbage leaves in boiling water for 1 minute. Drain and pat dry. Spoon meat mixture on center of leaves; fold over envelope fashion and secure with toothpicks. Place in greased shallow baking dish; pour tomato sauce over, cover and bake in a slow oven (325°) for 45 minutes. Serves 6.

title: Sauerkraut Zole
description: A hearty Sauerkraut Zole featuring crispy bacon, caramelized onion, and tangy sauerkraut, thickened with a flour mixture and enhanced with caraway seeds. Served with bread dumplings and pork roast, it's a comforting and robust meal.
instructions:
1. Cut bacon strips 1/2-inch wide.
2. Fry until crisp; then remove bacon to dish.
3. Fry diced onion in bacon grease.
4. Put flour in bowl with water and mix well.
5. Add flour mixture to bacon grease in frying pan and keep adding water until right consistency. Rinse and drain sauerkraut; then add to pan along with sugar, caraway seed, salt and pepper.
6. Add cooked bacon and mix well. Put dumplings on top and cover and simmer according to package directions.
7. Cook pork roast as desired.
8. Serve with dumpling mixture.

title: Potato And Pea Casserole
description: A comforting potato and pea casserole baked in a creamy white sauce and topped with buttery bread crumbs and melted cheese. This easy-to-make dish is a perfect vegetarian side or light meal.
instructions:
1. Make white sauce by combining margarine, flour, salt, pepper and milk.
2. Cook until thickened.
3. Pour over peas and potatoes in casserole dish.
4. Toss bread crumbs in melted butter.
5. Add cheese for topping.
6. Bake at 425° for 10 minutes.

title: Coconut Cream Pies(2 Pies)
description: Rich and creamy coconut cream pies with a luscious custard filling made from egg yolks, sugar, flour, milk, water, and plenty of coconut. This classic dessert is a perfect treat for any occasion.
instructions: 1. Beat egg yolks. Combine yolks, sugar and flour; mix together, then add milk, water, coconut and salt in medium saucepan, mixing well. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly until thick. Remove from heat. Stir in vanilla and margarine. Pour into baked pie shells.

title: Roberta'S Baked Salami
description: A simple yet delicious baked salami, scored and roasted until tender, perfect for serving on cocktail rye and pumpernickel bread. This easy appetizer is great for parties or casual gatherings.
instructions:
1. Score salami (crisscross) on top.
2. Bake in 325° oven for about 2 hours.
3. Serve on small cocktail rye bread and pumpernickel bread.

title: Quick Borsch
description: A quick and vibrant borsch made from beets, consomme, cream of celery soup, horseradish, and garlic powder, blended until smooth and chilled. Serve garnished with sour cream for a refreshing twist on a classic.
instructions:
1. Place beets and half the beet liquid in blender.
2. Add remaining ingredients and blend until smooth.
3. Chill at least 3 hours.
4. Serve garnished with a dollop of sour cream and a pinch of chopped tarragon or parsley.
5. Serves 6.

title: Sports Event Chili(Crockery Cooking)
description: A hearty Sports Event Chili, slow-cooked with ground beef, stew meat, onions, bell peppers, garlic, tomatoes, and kidney beans, seasoned with chili powder, cumin, and oregano. Perfect for feeding a crowd and enjoying at any gathering.
instructions:
1. Brown beef and stew meat in corn oil; add onion, bell pepper, garlic and saute until soft.
2. Skim off excess grease.
3. Add beans, tomatoes, jalapeno, cilantro, cumin, black pepper, chili powder, oregano, salt and green chilies.
4. Simmer at least 2 hours.
5. Line 4 large bowls with tortillas and spoon chili into each.
6. Top with Cheddar cheese and onions.
7. Can be made before game starts.
8. Watch game while chili cooks.
9. Makes 4 to 8 servings.

title: Cherry Pretzel Torte
description: A sweet and salty Cherry Pretzel Torte with a buttery pretzel crust, a creamy cream cheese and whipped topping layer, and a vibrant cherry pie filling. This chilled dessert is a delightful indulgence.
instructions:
1. Beat together cream cheese and sugar.
2. Fold in Cool Whip. Spread on crust.
3. Top with pie filling.
4. Top with remaining pretzel mixture.
5. Refrigerate 4 hours.

title: Russian T Cakes(Cookies)
description: Delicate Russian Tea Cakes, or cookies, made with butter, sugar, vanilla, flour, and nuts, rolled into balls and baked until set. These melt-in-your-mouth confections are then coated in powdered sugar.
instructions:
1. Mix butter, sugar and vanilla; add mix of flour, salt and nuts. Chill; then roll in 1-inch balls.
2. Bake until set.
3. Roll in powdered sugar.
4. Bake at 350° about 15 to 20 minutes.

title: Chips Chili And Cheese Squares
description: Savory Chips, Chili, and Cheese Squares featuring green chilies, cheese, and green onions, layered with tortilla chips and baked in an egg and milk mixture. This easy-to-make dish is perfect for a quick snack or appetizer.
instructions:
1. Combine chilies, cheese, and green onions and spoon into a lightly sprayed 8 inch square cake pan.
2. Sprinkle tortilla chips over top.
3. Combine eggs, milk, and Tabasco and beat well.
4. Pour over chilies and cheese mixture.
5. Bake at 325 degrees for 30 to 35 minutes.
6. Serve warm or at room temperature.
7. Cut into small squares.
8. Makes about 3 dozen.

title: Pork Goulash - Gulasz Wieprzowy
description: A robust Pork Goulash (Gulasz Wieprzowy) with well-trimmed pork cubes simmered with onions and paprika in soup stock, thickened with flour. Served with potatoes, this hearty dish is a comforting Polish classic.
instructions:
1. Cut meat into large cubes, well trimmed.
2. Put meat, onions and seasonings in heavy casserole and simmer, tightly covered, turning occasionally until meat browns.
3. Dust with flour.
4. Add soup stock, tomato puree and the parboiled potatoes.
5. Cover and continue to simmer until meat and potatoes are done.
6. Serves 6.

title: Spiced Or Russian Tea
description: A warming Spiced or Russian Tea, brewed with allspice, sugar, and tea, then simmered with a blend of lemonade and orange juices. Perfect for chilly days, this fragrant beverage keeps well in the refrigerator.
instructions:
1. Boil water with sugar and allspice in it.
2. Add tea that has been steeped.
3. Add juices; simmer for several hours.
4. Will keep in the refrigerator several weeks.
5. Makes about 1 gallon.

title: Sauerkraut And Macaroni
description: A unique combination of Sauerkraut and Macaroni with crispy bacon, onion, and stir-fry pork, flavored with parsley, pimento, and garlic. This savory dish offers a delightful interplay of textures and tastes.
instructions:
1. Cook sauerkraut, bacon and onion until kraut is tender.
2. Cook macaroni according to package directions.
3. Drain well.
4. Cook stir-fry pork in tablespoon of oil.
5. Add parsley flakes, pimento, garlic salt, salt and pepper.
6. Mix cornstarch with cup of chicken broth; cook until thickened.
7. Combine macaroni, sauerkraut mixture and thickened broth.
8. Heat and eat.

title: Ommy'S Russian Salad Dressing
description: Ommy's Russian Salad Dressing is a simple yet flavorful blend of catsup, sugar, vinegar, oil, salt, paprika, onion, and garlic. Perfect for adding a tangy-sweet kick to your favorite salads.
instructions:
1. Mix all together, except the oil.
2. Add oil slowly and beat thoroughly.
3. Makes 1 cup of dressing.

title: Beef Stroganoff For Buns
description: Classic Beef Stroganoff, made with ground beef and bacon, sautéed with onions, and simmered in a creamy mushroom soup and sour cream sauce. Seasoned with paprika, it’s perfect for serving on buns.
instructions:
1. Brown bacon first and add meat.
2. Brown.
3. Add onions.
4. Blend in flour and seasonings.
5. Stir in soup.
6. Cook, uncovered, 20 minutes.
7. Stir and add sour cream and heat.

title: Sopa Xochitl(Zucchini Soup)
description: A comforting and easy-to-make zucchini soup featuring tender zucchini, sweet corn, and a touch of green chili peppers for a hint of warmth, simmered in a savory chicken broth.
instructions:
1. Saute onion in butter until tender, not brown.
2. Stir in broth, zucchini, corn, chili peppers and spices.
3. Heat to boiling. Reduce heat and cover.
4. Cook until zucchini is tender, about 5 minutes.
5. Stir in milk.
6. Heat until piping hot.
7. Add cheese. Garnish with parsley.
8. Serve with hot buttered and rolled tortillas.

title: Mexican Party Roll-Ups
description: These quick and easy party roll-ups feature a creamy, spicy blend of cream cheese, sour cream, and picante sauce spread on flour tortillas, perfect for a festive appetizer.
instructions:
1. In blender, combine the cream cheese, sour cream and picante sauce.
2. Blend until it is smooth with a creamy texture.
3. Spread the blended filling evenly on the open-face tortilla (you can decide how thick you want the filling to be to suit your personal taste).

title: Quick Buckwheat Cakes
description: Speedy buckwheat cakes that are simple to prepare, mixing dissolved yeast with salt, molasses, and buckwheat flour, then pan-frying them to golden perfection in minutes.
instructions:
1. Dissolve yeast well in warm water.
2. Add salt and molasses. Stir in flour.
3. Beat well.
4. Let stand 10 to 15 minutes.
5. Bake on hot griddle.
6. Makes 12 cakes.

title: Hot Hors D'Oeuvres
description: Effortless hot hors d'oeuvres made by assembling ingredients on cocktail rye bread, then broiling until golden and bubbly for a quick party snack.
instructions: 1. Assemble as listed and broil until golden.

title: Chicken Soft Tacos
description: Flavorful chicken soft tacos featuring baked and cubed chicken breast sautéed with garlic, served with fresh shredded lettuce, Cheddar cheese, black olives, and salsa in warm flour tortillas.
instructions:
1. Bake chicken at 350° for 45 minutes.
2. Cut up into 1/2-inch cubes.
3. Saute chicken with minced garlic in a large skillet. Serve on soft wraps.
4. Makes 8 to 10 servings.

title: Chili-Cheese Sandwiches
description: Savory chili-cheese sandwiches layered with cheese, chili sauce, and green chilies on rye bread, then coated in an egg and milk mixture before cooking, offering a comforting and flavorful bite.
instructions:
1. Place 4 slices cheese on each of 4 slices bread.
2. Spread each with 1 teaspoon chili sauce.
3. Divide chilies equally among sandwiches.
4. Top with remaining slices of bread.
5. Beat milk, egg, cumin and salt with fork in 9-inch pie plate until smooth.
6. Dip both sides of sandwiches into egg mixture.

title: Macaroni And Ground Beef Goulash
description: A hearty and comforting goulash combining browned ground beef, onion, and green pepper with tender elbow macaroni, tomatoes, salt, and pepper, cooked until flavors meld.
instructions:
1. Brown meat, onion and green pepper.
2. Cook macaroni by package directions and drain thoroughly.
3. Add tomatoes, salt and pepper to macaroni and cook 20 minutes.
4. Add meat mixture.
5. Toss together and serve while hot.

title: Georgia'S Special Cake
description: A unique and easy cake where all ingredients, including oranges, are mixed together and baked, with holes poked for a delicious topping to soak in.
instructions:
1. Mix all ingredients and bake at 325° in 2 or 3 layers.
2. Poke holes in cake for topping to soak in some.

title: Stuffed Green Peppers(And Stuffed Zucchini And Stuffed Cabbage Rolls)
description: Hearty stuffed green peppers, zucchini, or cabbage rolls filled with a savory mixture of sausage, onion, tomatoes, and rice, seasoned with Worcestershire, basil, and a touch of sugar, then baked until tender.
instructions:
1. Halve the peppers lengthwise, removing stem ends, seeds and membranes.
2. Immerse peppers in boiling water for 3 minutes. Sprinkle insides with salt and invert on paper towels to drain. In a skillet, cook the sausage or ground beef with the onion until the meat is brown and the onion is tender.
3. Drain the fat.
4. Stir in the undrained tomatoes, uncooked rice, worcestershire sauce, water, and salt and pepper.
5. Bring to a boil then reduce the heat, cover, and simmer for 15-18 minutes, or until the rice is tender. Stir in 1/4 c. cheese.
6. Fill the peppers with the meat mixture and place them in an 8 x 8 inch baking dish with any remaining meat mixture.
7. Bake at 375° about 15 minutes, or until heated through. Sprinkle with the remaining cheese.
8. Let stand a couple minutes before serving.

title: Cheesy Potato Pancakes
description: Deliciously crispy cheesy potato pancakes made by layering thinly sliced potatoes and sharp cheese in a skillet with melted butter, then cooking until golden and bubbly.
instructions:
1. Melt butter in a skillet and arrange the first layer of potatoes so that they cover the skillet, overlapping.
2. Next, season lightly and add a layer of cheese.
3. Repeat.
4. End with a layer of cheese.
5. Cover the pan and cook at a medium heat for 40 to 50 minutes.
6. Loosen and serve.
7. Garnish with orange and apple slices.

title: Celeste'S Mushroom Salade
description: A simple and fresh mushroom salad featuring thickly sliced mushrooms, fresh garlic, parsley, olive oil, and lemon juice, seasoned with salt and pepper for a vibrant side dish.
instructions:
1. Slice mushrooms thickly just before serving.
2. Mix with chopped parsley, garlic, oil, lemon juice, salt and pepper.
3. Use two times as much oil as lemon juice.

title: Blueberry-Almond Blintzes
description: Delicate blueberry-almond blintzes made from a smooth batter of eggs, milk, flours, brown sugar, and almond extract, cooked in butter until golden, ready for a sweet filling.
instructions:
1. Combine all ingredients, except butter, in a food processor or blender until smooth.
2. Heat an 8-inch crepe pan or skillet over medium heat.
3. Coat pan with about 1/2 teaspoon of butter.
4. Add 3 to 4 tablespoons of batter to the butter, tilting pan to make an even layer of batter.
5. Cook on one side only.
6. When bottom begins to brown, turn out to cool on a plate.
7. Repeat process with remaining batter.
8. Makes 18.

title: Syrniki (Cottage Cheese Balls)
description: Golden-fried Syrniki, delectable cottage cheese balls made by combining all ingredients into a stiff dough, shaping, rolling in flour, and frying until perfectly browned and ready to enjoy.
instructions: 1. Combine all ingredients, except flour. Knead until stiff dough forms. Shape into small balls and roll in flour. Fry in oil for 3 to 4 minutes until golden brown.

title: Party Mini Pizzas
description: Quick and savory party mini pizzas featuring a mixture of browned sausage and ground beef combined with melted Velveeta cheese, spread on party rye bread and sprinkled with oregano.
instructions:
1. Brown sausage and ground beef. Drain.
2. Add cheese and stir until melted.
3. Put small amount of mixture on rye bread slices, not quite covering the top of each slice.
4. Sprinkle with oregano. May be frozen prior to baking and kept in a Ziploc bag for future use.
5. Bake on cookie sheet for 10 minutes at 350°.
6. If frozen, bake 20 to 25 minutes or until cheese starts to bubble.

title: Simple Sweet And Sour Chicken
description: Effortless sweet and sour chicken baked with a simple yet flavorful sauce made from red Russian salad dressing, apricot preserves, and onion soup mix, creating a deliciously sticky glaze.
instructions:
1. Combine ingredients and spread on top of breasts.
2. Bake at 350° for 50 to 60 minutes.
3. An easy way to "sweet and sour" something.

title: Spareribs With Sauerkraut
description: Tender spareribs seasoned and baked with a flavorful sauerkraut mixture, including apple, tomato juice, brown sugar, caraway seeds, and carrots, creating a hearty and comforting meal.
instructions:
1. Season ribs with salt and pepper.
2. Place in Dutch oven. Combine kraut (including liquid) with remaining ingredients and spoon over ribs.
3. Bake, covered, in moderate oven (350°) for 2 1/2 to 3 1/2 hours or until ribs are done.
4. Serves 4 to 6.

title: Reuben'S Dip Pot
description: A unique Reuben's dip served in a hollowed-out bread loaf, filled with a creamy mix of sauerkraut, cream cheese, Swiss cheese, yogurt, and corned beef, perfect for dipping with bread cubes and vegetables.
instructions:
1. Cut 1/2-inch slice from top of loaf and reserve.
2. Using sharp knife, remove bread inside loaf, leaving 1/2-inch thick shell. Cut bread into 1 1/4-inch cubes.
3. Combine butter and oil; brush on cubes.
4. Brown cubes in broiler 6 inches from heat, or in toaster oven, stirring until uniformly colored.
5. Set aside.

title: Borscht Soup
description: A classic Borscht soup featuring tender beef, cabbage, onion, potatoes, and tomatoes, cooked until vegetables are soft, creating a comforting and flavorful dish often served with a dollop of sour cream.
instructions:
1. Cook meat until tender.
2. Take meat off bone and put back in broth.
3. Add all other ingredients.
4. Cook until vegetables are soft. Add tomatoes and serve.
5. Very good if a little sour cream is put in each serving.

title: Susie'S Appetizer
description: Savory appetizer bites made from browned meats mixed with melted Velveeta cheese, onion, Worcestershire, ketchup, and garlic, spooned onto party rye bread and frozen for later enjoyment.
instructions: 1. Brown meats together; drain, then pat with paper towels. In large bowl, mix in cheese until melted, then add remaining ingredients. Put on rye bread. Place on cookie sheet and freeze.

title: Chili-Cheese Bake
description: A comforting chili-cheese bake layered with hearty chili, crunchy crackers, sharp Cheddar cheese, and red onion, then baked until bubbly and delicious for a satisfying meal.
instructions:
1. Spoon 1/3 of the chili onto bottom of a 10 x 6 x 1 1/2-inch baking dish. Arrange 1/2 the crackers over chili.
2. Sprinkle with 1/2 the cheese and onion. Repeat layer. Top with remaining 1/3 chili.
3. Bake in a 400° oven for 20 minutes.

title: Coconut Cream Torte
description: A delightful Coconut Cream Torte featuring a moist cake, creamy filling, and toasted flaked coconut, with the added crunch of pecans. Perfect for a special occasion or a sweet treat.
instructions:
1. Prepare cake batter according to package directions.
2. Pour into three greased and floured 9 inch baking pans.
3. Bake at 350 degrees for 20-25 minutes or until a toothpick inserted near center comes out clean.
4. Cool for 10 minutes before removing from pan.
5. Cool completely.

title: Jerrie'S Russian Tea
description: A simple and comforting Russian Tea blend, combining instant tea with the fruity sweetness of black cherry gelatin and warm spices like ground cloves and cinnamon. Just mix with hot water for a soothing beverage.
instructions:
1. Mix all ingredients together.
2. Store in jars.
3. Add 3 teaspoons per cup of hot water.

title: Ukranian "Holubtsi"(Stuffed Cabbage Rolls)
description: Traditional Ukrainian Holubtsi, or stuffed cabbage rolls, filled with a savory mixture of ground beef, rice, and mushrooms, then simmered in a rich tomato sauce until tender and flavorful.
instructions: 1. You will need a large casserole dish.

title: Tamale Pie Dip
description: A quick and easy Tamale Pie Dip that combines crumbled tamales, beans, and melted Velveeta cheese with onions, served hot with crunchy Fritos for a satisfying snack or appetizer.
instructions:
1. Crumble tamales in saucepan.
2. Add other ingredients.
3. Stir over medium heat until cheese melts.
4. Serve hot with Fritos.

title: Potato Pancakes(Placki Kartoflane)
description: Crispy and golden Potato Pancakes, or Placki Kartoflane, made from grated potatoes and onion, seasoned and fried until perfectly browned. A classic comfort food, often served as a side or snack.
instructions:
1. Using grater blade of food processor, grate potatoes and onion.
2. Combine with salt, eggs and flour (a bit more if needed). Spoon batter with large soup spoon into hot lard and fry until golden brown on each side.
3. Serve immediately.

title: Blintze Casserole
description: A sweet and creamy Blintze Casserole, layered with tender blintzes and a rich mixture of sour cream, sugar, and citrus, then baked until golden and bubbly. A perfect breakfast or dessert dish.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°.
2. Line casserole with blintzes.
3. Add margarine.
4. Mix everything but eggs.
5. Beat eggs.
6. Add to mixture. Pour.
7. Bake at 325° for 1 hour.

title: Sandy'S Polish Sausage And Cabbage
description: A hearty and flavorful dish featuring Polish sausage (kielbasa) and tender cabbage, simmered with potatoes and carrots until everything is cooked through. A comforting one-pot meal perfect for chilly days.
instructions:
1. Put cabbage and water in large pot.
2. Add salt and pepper. Bring to a boil.
3. Add sausage wheels; simmer.
4. Add carrots. Scrub or peel potatoes; cut in chunks and add to simmering pot. Cover tightly; cook for 35 minutes after adding potatoes.
5. Serve. A good, one-pot dish for 4!

title: Tuna-Potato Pancakes
description: Savory Tuna-Potato Pancakes, combining flaked tuna with grated potatoes, scallions, and parsley, pan-fried until crispy. Serve these flavorful patties with a refreshing Cucumber Mayonnaise.
instructions:
1. Cover potatoes with salted water; cook until fork penetrates easily, but potatoes are still quite firm.
2. Drain and let cool while draining and discarding water or oil from tuna.
3. Grate potatoes coarsely, using grater or grating blade of food processor.
4. Gently mix grated potatoes, tuna, scallions, parsley, egg, salt and cayenne.
5. Form into 8 pancakes, shaping loosely but flattening slightly.
6. In a large skillet, heat enough oil for shallow frying.
7. Add pancakes; do not overcrowd pan.
8. Brown well on one side, then turn with slotted turner.
9. Drain second side and remove pancakes from oil.
10. Drain well on paper toweling.
11. Serve with Cucumber Mayonnaise.
12. Makes 4 servings.

title: Borscht(Cold Red Beet Soup)
description: A refreshing and vibrant Cold Red Beet Soup, or Borscht, made with tender beets, crisp cucumbers, and fresh dill, brightened with vinegar and a dollop of sour cream. Perfect for a light summer meal.
instructions:
1. Boil beets until tender.
2. Let cool for about 1 hour.
3. Peel and dice.
4. In large bowl, place diced beets, diced onion and diced cucumber.
5. Mix and add vinegar and sugar (mix together until sugar dissolves); add sour cream.
6. Tie two bunches of dill (with dental floss).
7. Mix in with the ingredients.
8. Stir gently.
9. Cover with Saran Wrap and marinate overnight.
10. Serve with buttermilk.

title: Make Ahead Enchilada Casserole
description: A convenient make-ahead Enchilada Casserole, layered with a rich mixture of different cheeses, sour cream, and seasoned with chili powder and cumin, all baked to cheesy perfection.
instructions:
1. Mix monterey cheese, 1 cup cheddar cheese, onion, sour cream, parsley, salt and pepper.
2. Set aside.

title: Tomato And Mushroom Salad
description: A simple yet elegant Tomato and Mushroom Salad featuring juicy cherry tomatoes, fresh mushrooms, and sliced green onions, tossed in a tangy vinaigrette. Chill for a refreshing side dish.
instructions:
1. Combine tomatoes, mushrooms and onions.
2. Mix the rest of the ingredients together.
3. Pour over and stir.
4. Chill for a couple of hours before serving.

title: Cocktail Rye Tidbits
description: Quick and savory Cocktail Rye Tidbits topped with a delicious mixture of browned hamburger, hot Italian sausage, herbs, and melted Velveeta cheese. Perfect as a party appetizer, and can be frozen for later.
instructions:
1. Brown hamburger and sausage.
2. Drain very well.
3. Add seasoning and Velveeta cheese; simmer until cheese melts.
4. Put spoonfuls on cocktail rye slices; can be frozen at this point for later use. Pop under the broiler until bubbly and serve at any party.

title: Gramma'S Candied Yams
description: Sweet and tender Candied Yams, simmered in a rich syrupy sauce of brown sugar, margarine, and cinnamon, then topped with fluffy marshmallows. A classic comforting side dish.
instructions:
1. Boil potatoes until tender.
2. Peel and slice lengthwise.
3. In a 10-inch skillet, melt margarine.
4. Add brown sugar, lemon extract, cinnamon and water.
5. Bring to a gentle boil and add sliced sweet potatoes.
6. Heat for several minutes on each side (sweet potatoes should be hot when removed from skillet).
7. Remove potatoes to a 13 x 9-inch dish.

title: Baked Chicken With Wild Rice
description: A simple and comforting Baked Chicken with Wild Rice, where chicken pieces are baked together with a flavorful blend of rice, mushroom soup, and onion soup. This one-pan meal is perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions:
1. In greased 9 x	13-inch
2. pan, mix rice, water, mushroom and onion soup together.
3. Layer
4. chicken
5. in
6. rice mixture and sprinkle seasoning on top.	Bake 1 1/2 hours in 350° oven.

title: Challah(Communion Bread)
description: A traditional Challah bread, a rich and slightly sweet braided loaf often used for communion or holidays. The dough is made with yeast, eggs, and a touch of sugar, resulting in a soft and beautiful bread.
instructions:
1. Dissolve yeast and 1 teaspoon sugar in 1/2 cup warm water. Let stand 5 minutes. Combine oil and next 4 ingredients in a large mixing bowl. Beat at medium speed with an electric mixer until blended.
2. Add yeast mixture and 2 cups flour; beat until smooth. Gradually stir in enough remaining flour to make a soft dough. (Dough should be sticky.)
3. Cover dough and let rest 10 minutes.

title: Mazurek(Polish Bar Cookies)
description: Delicious Mazurek, traditional Polish bar cookies with a crumbly base and a sweet, almond-infused topping. These rich, buttery cookies are a popular treat especially around holidays.
instructions:
1. Sift together 2 cups flour, 1 cup sugar and 1/4 teaspoon salt. Cut in margarine until mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
2. Set aside.
3. Mix 1 beaten egg and 3 tablespoons milk.
4. Add egg mixture to flour mix; stir until moistened (cut in to complete).
5. Pat evenly into greased 9 x 13 x 2-inch baking pan.
6. Bake at 350° for 35 minutes.
7. Beat 1 egg.
8. Stir in 3 tablespoons lemon juice.
9. Add 1/2 cup sugar.
10. Add nuts.
11. Remove pan from oven.
12. Spread with lemon mix.
13. Return to oven.
14. Bake 10 to 12 minutes.
15. Cool and cut into bars.

title: Mock Chicken Kiev
description: A flavorful Mock Chicken Kiev, featuring chicken breasts pounded thin, filled with a savory herb and garlic butter, then breaded and fried or baked until golden and juicy.
instructions:
1. Mash margarine with garlic, parsley and chives.
2. Divide into 2 equal portions and refrigerate until chilled.
3. Place chicken breasts between two sheets of waxed paper and pound with mallet until 1/4 inch thick.
4. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
5. Place herbed butter in center of each chicken breast, tuck in sides and roll up breast and fasten with toothpicks.
6. Heat oil in skillet.
7. Brown breasts quickly until golden.
8. Place in lightly greased ovenware and bake in 350° oven 1/2 hour or until chicken is done.
9. Serve with rice or noodles, a vegetable and tossed salad.
10. Serves two; 154 calories.

title: Beef Vegetable Borsch
description: A hearty Vegetable Borsch with beef, featuring tender sautéed vegetables like cabbage, carrots, and onions, simmered with beets and beef in a rich broth. Serve with a dollop of sour cream for a classic finish.
instructions:
1. In large kettle, melt butter.
2. Saute vegetables for 3 minutes. Add tomatoes, broth, vinegar, beets and meat; bring to boil. Cover; simmer for 30 minutes.
3. Season to taste.
4. Top with sour cream.

title: Armenian Lamb Stew
description: A savory Armenian Lamb Stew, slow-cooked with tender lamb, onions, green peppers, mushrooms, and tomatoes, seasoned with fragrant oregano. This comforting dish is perfect for a hearty meal.
instructions:
1. Brown lamb in butter.
2. Add onion, green pepper and mushrooms. Saute briefly until onion wilts.
3. Break up tomatoes and add with oregano to meat.
4. Simmer until done, about 30 to 45 minutes. Serve over rice.
5. Serves 4 to 6.

title: Palacinky(Blintzes)
description: Delicate Palacinky, or blintzes, thin crepes that can be filled with your favorite sweet or savory fillings. My family enjoys them with strawberry jam and cottage cheese for a delightful treat.
instructions:
1. Pour just enough batter in a crepe pan to cover the bottom. Carefully turn when brown.
2. Fill with whatever you wish.
3. My family's favorite is strawberry jam and cottage cheese.
4. Makes approximately 10.

title: Marshall Field Sandwich
description: A delicious marshall field sandwich recipe.
instructions:
1. For each sandwich:
2. Take 3 pieces beef steak rye and remove crusts.
3. Butter the bread.
4. Layer bread, lettuce, ham, tomato, bread, ham, 3 strips bacon, bread and hard-boiled egg (crumbled).

title: Italian Sandwiches
description: A delicious italian sandwiches recipe.
instructions:
1. Cook ground round.
2. Sprinkle with garlic salt and pepper to taste.
3. Add dried onion pieces.
4. Cook, stirring to make small pieces.
5. When almost done, sprinkle generously with Worcestershire sauce.
6. Continue cooking and stirring until done.

title: Pleasing Potato Pancakes
description: A delicious pleasing potato pancakes recipe.
instructions:
1. Combine all ingredients.
2. Blend well in a mixing bowl.
3. Drop by tablespoonfuls onto greased hot skillet.
4. Take the rolled patty mixture and drop into the pan.
5. Turn the heat to medium.
6. Cook on both sides until lightly browned.
7. Repeat the process.
8. Turn each one over with a spatula.
9. Do this until it is golden brown and crispy.
10. Drain the grease off and serve.
11. Ready to eat. Delicious!

title: Imitation Blintzes
description: A delicious imitation blintzes recipe.
instructions:
1. Cut crust of bread and roll each piece flat with a rolling pin. Spread each piece with layer of cream cheese.
2. Roll each piece up and cut in half.
3. Dip each in melted butter or margarine.
4. Then roll in mixture of sugar and cinnamon to taste.
5. Place on a cookie sheet.
6. Bake at 350° for 10 to 15 minutes.

title: Royal Mazurek(Mazurek Krolewski)
description: A delicious royal mazurek(mazurek krolewski) recipe.
instructions:
1. Cream butter; sift and add sugar and salt.
2. Add alternately one egg yolk and a little of the flour mixture until all has been used.
3. Add almonds.
4. Bake in two (17 x 11-inch) pans for 20 to 25 minutes at 350°.
5. Cut when hot.
6. Sprinkle with powdered sugar.

title: Cherry Cheese Cream Tarts
description: A delicious cherry cheese cream tarts recipe.
instructions: 1. Beat eggs, sugar, cream cheese, vanilla, and lemon juice until smooth.

title: Mom'S Stuffed Cabbage  (Sweet And Sour)
description: A delicious mom's stuffed cabbage  (sweet and sour) recipe.
instructions: 1. *If using lemon juice, use 2 tablespoons.

title: Potato Pancakes With Cheddar
description: A delicious potato pancakes with cheddar recipe.
instructions:
1. In a bowl beat egg, then beat in milk, salt and flour.
2. Add grated or chopped onion and grated cheese.
3. Wash and peel potatoes, then grate directly into egg mixture, working rapidly because grated potato tends to darken.

title: Vegetable Latkes
description: A delicious vegetable latkes recipe.
instructions:
1. Peel and coarsely shred potatoes and carrots.
2. Shred zucchini. Place in a bowl with onion.
3. Combine eggs, egg whites, salt and pepper; stir into vegetable mixture.
4. Sprinkle flour over top; stir to mix.
5. Heat nonstick pan over medium heat; brush with oil. Drop batter by heaping tablespoonfuls into pan; flatten lightly. Cook until browned.
6. Turn; brown other side.
7. Add oil as necessary.
8. Transfer to paper toweling to drain.
9. Keep warm until all latkes are cooked.
10. Makes 36 mini latkes.

title: Curry Chutney Chicken Salad
description: A delicious curry chutney chicken salad recipe.
instructions:
1. Combine mayonnaise, chutney, curry powder, lime juice, salt and pepper.
2. Pour over chicken and mix in almonds.
3. Chill before serving.

title: Barley Soup(Ukrainian)
description: A delicious barley soup(ukrainian) recipe.
instructions:
1. Wash the pearl barley; cook in a large amount of lightly salted boiling water until tender.
2. Meanwhile, cook the onion in the butter until lightly wilted.
3. Add the vegetables, mushrooms and soup stock.
4. Simmer until the vegetables are done.
5. Strain the cooked barley and add to the soup.
6. Stir in the sour cream and bring to a boil.
7. Season to taste with the salt and pepper. Garnish with the dill or parsley.

title: Coconut Torte
description: A delicious coconut torte recipe.
instructions:
1. Beat egg whites until foamy and add sugar; beat until stiff. Add crumbs and rest of ingredients.
2. Bake in a large pie tin or cake pan at 350° until done or golden, about 20 minutes.
3. Serve with ice cream.

title: Bowman'S Pot Pie
description: A delicious bowman's pot pie recipe.
instructions:
1. To flour, add a little salt and eggs; rub into flour as you do shortening for a pie crust.
2. Add sweet milk, enough to make a soft dough.
3. Roll thin and cut in squares.
4. Have meat broth with pieces of meat and potato chunks in it boiling, and drop in squares of dough one at a time.
5. Roll out more dough and continue dropping until all dough is gone.
6. Be certain that the broth is always boiling when adding more dough.
7. Cook until the potatoes are done.

title: "Snick Snack"
description: A delicious "snick snack" recipe.
instructions:
1. Brown ground beef and drain.
2. Mix ingredients and put mixture on individual pieces of party rye bread.
3. Heat in oven and serve.

title: Polish Drumstick Cake
description: A delicious polish drumstick cake recipe.
instructions:
1. First Layer:
2. Mix vanilla wafers, melted butter and 1 package crushed nuts.
3. Press into 9 x 13-inch pan.
4. Chill.

title: Russian Tea Cakes Or Mexican Wedding Balls
description: A delicious russian tea cakes or mexican wedding balls recipe.
instructions:
1. Mix butter, vanilla and sugar thoroughly.
2. Sift together flour and salt.
3. Add to creamed mixture.
4. Add nuts.
5. Chill dough; roll into 1-inch balls.
6. Place on ungreased baking sheet (cookies do not spread).
7. Bake at 400° for 10 to 12 minutes, or until set but not brown.
8. While still warm, roll in powdered sugar.
9. Cool.
10. Roll in powdered sugar again.
11. Makes about 4 dozen cookies.

title: Polish Pickles
description: A delicious polish pickles recipe.
instructions:
1. Bring to a boil and let cook for 3 minutes.
2. Use 1 to 2 cloves of garlic and 1 sprig of dill weed in each jar.
3. Put cukes in each jar with garlic and dill.
4. Pour hot liquid into each jar.
5. Put lid on and cover tight.
6. Let stand in cool dry place about 2 weeks. After that, put in refrigerator.

title: Borscht(Russian Beef, Vegetable Soup)
description: A delicious borscht(russian beef, vegetable soup) recipe.
instructions:
1. Boil beef in a large stockpot.
2. Add bay leaf.
3. Boil beef until tender (approximately 1/2 hour).
4. Skim foam that develops and discard.
5. In the beef broth, add carrots, onion, celery, beets and bell pepper.
6. Boil until beets are nearly white.
7. Add potatoes and tomato sauce.
8. After the potatoes are tender, add the cabbage and parsley.
9. Boil until cabbage is tender.
10. Add salt and pepper to taste.

title: Stuffed Cabbage(Moosewood Cookbook)
description: A delicious stuffed cabbage(moosewood cookbook) recipe.
instructions:
1. Parboil the cabbage in water, 10 to 15 minutes, or until outer leaves are easily removable.
2. Remove the first 12 leaves.
3. Make sure the cabbage is cooked well enough so leaves will not break when rolled.
4. Saute vegetable (not apple), nuts and seeds in butter until onion is transparent and nuts roasted.
5. Combine the saute with remaining ingredients and season to taste.

title: Whole Stuffed Cabbage
description: A delicious whole stuffed cabbage recipe.
instructions:
1. Cook cabbage head 10 minutes or until tender.
2. Drain and cool. Mix together other ingredients.
3. Peel back all leaves until the center of cabbage is exposed (about the size of a grapefruit). Cut this portion away.
4. Use half of filling to stuff center.
5. Roll enough leaves up to cover.
6. Use other half of filling and roll remaining leaves up.
7. Put the cabbage head and remaining leaves that have been rolled up into a baking pan.
8. Cover with spaghetti sauce.
9. Add 1/2 cup of water around cabbage head.
10. Cover and cook in the oven at 350° for 45 minutes to 1 hour.

title: Poppy Seed Hamwiches
description: Quick and savory ham and Swiss sandwiches on rye bread, elevated with a zesty horseradish, mustard, onion, and poppy seed butter spread. These hamwiches are perfect for a light meal or appetizer.
instructions:
1. Mix soft butter, horseradish, mustard, chopped onion and poppy seed.
2. Spread on 8 slices rye bread.
3. Top each of 4 of the slices with a slice of ham and a slice of cheese.
4. Cover with remaining 4 slices of rye bread.
5. Place on buttered grill or heavy frying pan. Brown on both sides.

title: Polish Cream Cheese Cake
description: Indulge in this rich and creamy Polish-inspired cream cheese cake, featuring a smooth blend of cream cheese, sour cream, and vanilla. A delightful dessert that's simple to prepare and perfect for any occasion.
instructions:
1. Beat cream cheese; add sugar and beat until light and feathery.
2. Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each one. Beat in vanilla and sour cream.

title: Mushroom Salad Italiano
description: A refreshing mushroom salad with an Italian flair, featuring fresh button mushrooms, crisp celery, and parsley, all tossed in a zingy lemon-garlic-olive oil dressing. Top it off with Parmesan cheese for an extra layer of flavor.
instructions:
1. To make dressing, combine lemon juice and next 3 ingredients. Beat in oil.
2. Reserve.
3. Up to 2 hours before serving, toss mushrooms, celery, parsley and cheese with reserved dressing. Serves 6.

title: Medium Seedless Rye Bread
description: Bake a delicious medium seedless rye bread in your bread machine, with a hint of honey and molasses for a touch of sweetness. Perfect for sandwiches or alongside your favorite meal.
instructions:
1. In the order listed, place the water, oil, honey, molasses, salt, rye flour, bread flour and yeast in the baking pan of a bread machine.
2. Place the pan in the machine and bake the bread according to the manufacturer's directions for a plain loaf. Makes 1 (1 1/2-pound) loaf (16 slices).

title: Apple Potato Pancakes
description: Savor these delightful Apple Potato Pancakes, a sweet twist on a traditional favorite, featuring a blend of eggs, flour, milk, and grated apples. Cooked to golden perfection and often served with a sprinkle of sugar and cinnamon.
instructions:
1. In a blender, mix the eggs, flour, milk and salt.
2. Blend until smooth.
3. Let the batter stand about 1 hour.
4. Sprinkle the juice of one half a lemon over the apples.
5. In a heavy skillet, heat 2 teaspoons of butter.
6. When the butter is bubbly, add the batter with apples slices and bake in 350° oven for 15 minutes.

title: Easy Russian Chicken
description: This easy Russian chicken features tender chicken parts baked in a savory-sweet glaze made from apricot preserves, Russian dressing, and onion soup mix. A flavorful dish that's simple to prepare for any weeknight meal.
instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°.
2. Skin chicken parts.
3. Place in 9 x 13-inch baking pan.
4. In separate bowl, mix preserves, Russian dressing and onion soup mix.
5. Pour mixture over chicken.
6. Bake at 375° for 45 to 60 minutes.
7. Serve with rice.

title: Sauerkraut Hot Dish #1
description: A comforting sauerkraut hot dish combining browned ground beef and onion with layers of sauerkraut, noodles, and creamy celery soup. Topped with melted Cheddar cheese, it's a hearty and satisfying casserole.
instructions:
1. Brown beef and onion.
2. Add salt and pepper.
3. Put half of ground beef mixture in casserole.
4. Add all sauerkraut, then rest of ground beef.
5. Then add cooked noodles and soups over top.
6. Bake at 350° for 1/2 hour.
7. Put cheese on top and bake 20 minutes more. Serve with your favorite breads.

title: Beef Goulash(Microwave)
description: Enjoy a quick and easy microwave beef goulash, featuring tender stew beef simmered with ripe tomatoes and pungent onion. Finished with a swirl of sour cream, it's a convenient and hearty meal.
instructions:
1. Place beef in a 3-quart microwave casserole.
2. Peel tomatoes and remove cores.
3. Cut tomatoes in chunks.
4. Place in casserole with beef.
5. Add onion, salt and pepper; stir.
6. Cover and cook on Roast for 40 to 50 minutes or until beef is tender.
7. Stir twice during cooking time.
8. Stir sour cream into mixture and let stand, covered, for 5 minutes.

title: Jewish Rye Bread Dip
description: Whip up this simple and flavorful Jewish Rye Bread Dip, a creamy blend of mayonnaise, sour cream, and a medley of dehydrated onion, parsley, and dill weed. Perfect for spreading on rye bread or serving as an appetizer.
instructions: 1. Mix all ingredients and refrigerate for 24 hours.

title: Barszcz(Beet Soup)
description: Warm up with a classic Barszcz (Beet Soup), a vibrant and hearty soup featuring boiled beets, cabbage, carrots, and celery, enriched with tomato juice and a touch of butter. Finish with a swirl of whipping cream for extra richness.
instructions:
1. Boil beets on medium heat in 4 quarts of water for 1/2 hour. Then add the rest of the vegetables and boil for 1/2 hour more. Add 1 pint of tomato juice.
2. Bring to boil for about 2 minutes. Add butter; stir in well.
3. Lastly, add cream.
4. Heat, but do not boil.
5. Serve hot.
6. I add 2 small or 1 large potato.
7. I also use vinegar, about 4 or 5 tablespoons to taste.
8. It will keep a couple of days in a cool place and seems to improve with standing.

title: Ricky Skaggs' One Pot Meal
description: Enjoy Ricky Skaggs' hearty one-pot meal featuring tender pork layered with potatoes, onions, carrots, and fresh sauerkraut. This comforting dish simmers to perfection, offering a flavorful and satisfying family meal.
instructions:
1. Amounts will vary according to family size.
2. Layer potatoes, onions and carrots on top of pork.
3. Top with sauerkraut.
4. Simmer until tender (3 to 4 hours).

title: Cream Cheese Blintzes
description: These delightful Cream Cheese Blintzes are made easy by using flattened white bread, filled with a sweet cream cheese and egg yolk mixture. Dipped in melted butter and coated in cinnamon sugar, they're a decadent treat.
instructions:
1. Roll bread flat with a rolling pin.
2. Mix cream cheese, 1/2 c. sugar and egg yolks together.
3. Spread evenly over the bread and rollup lengthwise.
4. Dip in melted butter and roll in cinnamon sugar.
5. Cool in fridge for 20 minutes and then cut into thirds. This can be frozen at this point and stored up to 4 months.
6. Bake 350° for 20 minutes or bubbly.

title: Blintze Souffle
description: Indulge in a sweet Blintze Souffle, featuring store-bought cheese blintzes baked in a rich custard of eggs, sour cream, sugar, and orange juice. A perfect brunch or dessert option.
instructions:
1. Melt butter in 2-quart casserole over stove.
2. Place blintzes in casserole.
3. Blend all other ingredients with well beaten eggs. Pour liquid over blintzes in casserole.
4. Bake at 350° for 45 minutes or until top browns.
5. Serve with powdered sugar or strawberries.

title: Pierogi Made Easy
description: Make pierogi easy with this clever recipe using lasagna noodles, filled with a savory potato and onion mixture. Baked to perfection, it's a convenient way to enjoy the classic flavors of pierogi.
instructions:
1. Grease a 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking dish with Pam.
2. Boil potatoes until tender when tested with fork.
3. Cook lasagna noodles according to package directions.
4. Saute butter and onions in a small skillet.
5. When the potatoes are done, mash them and add the cheese and mash together.
6. In your baking dish, place 3 lasagna noodles.
7. On top of the noodles, spread out 1/3 of the potato/cheese mixture over that.
8. Follow with layers as listed. Put the last three lasagna noodles on top and rest of the onions and butter.
9. Cover and bake in 225° oven for approximately 45 to 55 minutes.

title: Turkey Goulash
description: A comforting turkey goulash featuring tender turkey simmered with tomatoes, green pepper, onion, and mushrooms, seasoned with onion and garlic powder. Served with elbow macaroni, it's a hearty and flavorful meal.
instructions:
1. Combine turkey, seasonings, tomatoes, juice, green pepper, onion flakes, sweetener and mushrooms with liquid.
2. Bring to a boil.
3. Add macaroni and continue to boil, uncovered, until macaroni absorbs all of the juice and is tender, about 20 minutes. Divide evenly.
4. Serves 4.

title: Spinach, Bacon And Mushroom Salad
description: A fresh and vibrant spinach salad with crispy bacon and earthy mushrooms, all tossed in a simple yet flavorful red wine vinegar and olive oil dressing. Perfect as a side or a light main course.
instructions:
1. Cut the stems off the spinach and wash the leaves well in cold water. Drain leaves and place in a salad bowl. Add bacon and mushrooms. Mix together the vinegar, oil, salt and pepper.
2. Pour over salad and toss well. Serves 6.

title: Marinated Mushroom Salad
description: A delightful marinated mushroom salad featuring fresh mushrooms, cauliflower, celery, and green pepper, all tossed in a zesty oil and vinegar dressing with onion, sugar, and black pepper. Perfect as an appetizer or side dish.
instructions:
1. Thoroughly combine oil, vinegar, onion, salt, sugar, black pepper and dry mustard in large bowl.
2. Rinse and slice thin the mushrooms and add to marinade.
3. Toss to coat.
4. Slice cauliflower flowerets.
5. Slice celery.
6. Cut the green pepper in strips.
7. Add all to mushrooms and let marinate at least 1 hour.
8. Just before serving halve the cherry tomatoes and add.

title: Russian Refresher Mix(An Imitation Russian Tea - Without The Tea)
description: Enjoy an imitation Russian Refresher Mix, a caffeine-free alternative to Russian tea, combining powdered orange drink, sugar, cinnamon, and ground cloves. Mix with hot water for a warm and comforting beverage.
instructions:
1. Combine all ingredients in a medium bowl.
2. Mix well. Put in a 1-quart airtight container.
3. Label. Store in a cool, dry place. Use within 6 months.
4. Makes about 3 1/2 cups of Russian Refresher Mix.

title: Polish Sausage Casserole
description: A quick and easy Polish sausage casserole featuring sliced Polish sausage, potatoes, and Cheddar cheese, baked in a creamy celery soup base. A hearty and satisfying meal that comes together in no time.
instructions:
1. Cook up spaghetti noodles; drain.
2. Add butter, salt and Parmesan cheese to taste.
3. Boil Polish sausage, slice up, add to noodles and sprinkle parsley flakes on.
4. Fast, easy and delicious!

title: Hanky-Panks
description: These addictive Hanky Panks feature a savory mixture of seasoned ground beef, sausage, and melted Velveeta cheese, spooned onto party rye bread. Perfect as an appetizer for any gathering.
instructions:
1. Cook ground beef and sausage together; season to taste.
2. Drain fat.
3. Stir in cheese cubes and microwave a few minutes, stirring every 30 seconds to melt.
4. Place spoonfuls onto rye bread which has been placed on cookie sheets and bake approximately 8 to 10 minutes at 350°.

title: Nuria'S Russian Salad
description: This classic Russian Salad features a delightful combination of diced potatoes, boiled eggs, and tuna, all bound together in a creamy mayonnaise dressing. Perfect for a refreshing side dish or a light meal, it's simple to prepare and full of flavor.
instructions:
1. Dice potatoes and boil until tender.
2. Boil eggs.
3. Drain potatoes.
4. Chop boiled eggs.
5. Combine potatoes, eggs, tuna, mayo and Italian dressing.
6. Finely chop 1/2 jar of olives and add. Form a mold on a medium platter and arrange rest of olives (whole) in an attractive pattern on top of mold.
7. Serve with crackers or vegetable salad, if desired.

title: Broccoli And Mushroom Salad
description: A simple and refreshing salad featuring crisp broccoli, earthy mushrooms, and tender celery, all tossed in a sweet and tangy vinaigrette. This easy-to-make dish is perfect for a light lunch or a healthy side.
instructions:
1. Mix vegetables together and let set for 1 hour.
2. Mix sugar, oil and etc. together and pour over vegetables.
3. Let stand 1 hour before serving.

title: Turkey-Mushroom Salad
description: Enjoy a light and savory turkey-mushroom salad with a zesty lemon-mayonnaise dressing. This dish, perfect served on crisp romaine leaves, combines tender turkey, earthy mushrooms, and crunchy celery for a delightful experience.
instructions:
1. Combine first 4 ingredients.
2. Blend together mayonnaise, lemon juice, onion and salt.
3. Add to turkey mixture; toss lightly. Chill.
4. Line bowl with romaine leaves.
5. Spoon in salad.
6. Makes 6 servings.

title: Quick Beet Borscht
description: Savor this quick and easy beet borscht, a delightful vegetarian version made with canned beets and beef broth. Enhanced with cabbage, onion, and a touch of lemon, it's a comforting soup perfect for a chilly day.
instructions:
1. Heat beef broth, if using a bouillon cube add
2. 10 1/2-ounces water, beets with liquid, cabbage, sugar, and onion to boiling. Reduce heat; simmer gently five minutes.
3. Stir in lemon juice. Chill thoroughly.
4. Top each serving with a spoon of sour cream. Serve with Ritz crackers.
5. This borscht can also be served hot.

title: Beef Stroganoff(Serves 4)
